quests.title: Welcome to GregTech CEuUp!
quests.subtitle: We hope you enjoy your stay
quests.desc:
This questbook is intended as a guide on Gregtech: Community Edition Unofficial Modern Unoffical Port (also known as GTCEuUp), a port of GTCEu Modern, a minecraft mod, which is in itself a fork of Gregtech.

Currently this mod has limited integration with vanilla Terraria, but total Gregification awaits in the near future. 

The questbook you're reading is brimming with information to guide you through the mod, so check it out if you find yourself getting lost.  We wrote it with both old-school veterans and newcomers in mind.

quests.title: The Beginning of it All
quests.subtitle: Begin your playthrough as before
quests.desc:
As all Terraria worlds do, this one starts with chopping some trees. 

"Huh, I thought GregTech was supposed to shake things up a bit..."

quests.title: What do I need to progress?
quests.subtitle: Gather some resources!
quests.desc:
Here's a nifty list of some resources that you'll need in bulk for early-game. Collect more than we're asking for in this quest - you'll end up running out before you know it, and it's always a good habit to gather more than you need in GregTech.

 The bamboo will be needed for paper later, and the sand and clay are used for your first GT machine, the humble Coke Oven.

quests.title: Ore Generation
quests.subtitle: More like SNORE generation!
quests.desc:
The ore generation in GregTech is very different to vanilla Terraria (although vanilla ores still generate). All GT ores generate in large veins.

Here is what you need to know:

- Each ore tile will drop 16 raw ores.

- Many ores smelt directly into ingots in a furnace. You don't need exactly copper ore for copper: Bornite, Chalcopyrite, Malachite, and many others can smelt into the stuff. Use TMI!

-Check the Ore Generation quest tab to see what ores generate in what veins.

- Most vanilla ores can be crafted into 16 of their GT counterparts. There is currently some weirdness between GT and Terraria ores (for example, two different tins) which will be resolved in a future update.

quests.title: Get moving!
quests.subtitle: Earlygame logistics
quests.desc:
These pipes are dirt cheap and not particularly fast, but do a great job of moving things from one place to another.

They lack the advanced filtering capabilities of later pipes.

quests.title: Better Than Iron
quests.subtitle: Murder, she Wrought
quests.desc:
Wrought Iron has a very low carbon content, but that's enough to make it tougher than regular Iron.

Durability doesn't exist in terraria, so iron tools will work fine, and wrought iron tools currently are identical to iron ones. It's still an important material for future machines, and it also smelts into steel faster than regular iron does.

Craft it by smelting iron ingots.

quests.title: STOP! Hammer time!
quests.subtitle: Your first GT Tool!
quests.desc:
Alright - your first proper GregTech tool. The first of many.

GT tools can be made out of many different materials. When used in crafting recipes, they aren't consumed. You'll want to start with an Iron tool - but you could opt for Wrought Iron if you already tackled that quest.

Hammer: A crafting tool that shows up in a lot of earlygame recipes. Also can do everything a regular Terraria hammer can.

The most common recipe that the hammer is involved in is plates, which cost 2 ingots. Don't worry, they get cheaper later.

quests.title: Hammer down!
quests.subtitle: Mining just became efficient
quests.desc:
This hammer isn't quite like the ones you'll use for crafting. It actually works as a 3x1 pickaxe!

Make good use of these hammers! They'll help you to clear out an entire vein in record time.

Note: Any quest with GregTech tools can be complete with tools crafted from any material - not just the one displayed.

quests.title: Iron, Iron, Iron!
quests.subtitle: Isn't it ironic... don't you think?
quests.desc:
In the real world, iron isn't only found as pure Iron Ore. Instead, it's found in a variety of oxide forms or minerals.

You'll want to go and collect quite a bit of Iron, because you're going to need a bunch very soon. 

Any of the raw ores we're asking for can be directly smelted into Iron. That comes to four different veins that can give you Iron.

Whilst it may be common habit to hoard ore in modded Minecraft prior to unlocking some kind of ore multiplication, don't worry about ore processing yet. Direct smelting remains the most efficient method for a long, long while, given how easy it is to obtain a lot of ore in a mining trip.

Gather any of the requested Raw Ores to complete this quest!

quests.title: Important Tools
quests.desc:
More GregTech tools are required for progression - here are the ones to lay eyes on.

Wrench: A crafting tool, but also the correct tool for GregTech machines... perfect if you think using pickaxes is unethical.

File: A crafting tool... and that's it. Yay!

Saw: Yet another crafting tool. Also makes trees drop rubber wood.

There is also an item of legend that can be made if you gather all the GT tools in one place...

Note: Any Quest with GregTech tools can be completed with the tool crafted from any material, not just the one displayed.

quests.title: More Tools
quests.desc:
More tools - they're not critically important right now, but you'll need to make these at some point.

Screwdriver: A crafting tool. It's used for special configuration of machines and covers.

Wire Cutter: A crafting tool. You'll need it if you want to configure wire connections. Right-clicking on wires will enable or disable connection to other blocks.

Mortar: A crafting tool, used to grind various things down into dust form.

Knife: A pretty mediocre weapon, better suited as - you guessed it - a crafting tool!

Soft Mallet: A tool to toggle machine operation with a simple left-click.

There are a couple of other tools that are quite niche, but deserve a mention.

Hoe: Grants bonus seeds when harvesting herbs.

Scythe: Collects vine rope. 

Plunger: A tool to clear stuck fluids from GregTech machines.

quests.title: Coke Oven
quests.subtitle: Your first big project... hopefully
quests.desc:
The Coke Oven will likely be your first GregTech multiblock. Congratulations!

It is used to make Coke from Coal, or Charcoal from Wood, in addition to liquid Creosote as a bonus output.

Placing down the controller will show the multiblock structure around it. You'll need 8 coke brick blocks around it.

Note: Any Brick block can be replaced with a Coke Oven Hatch. The Hatch allows for automation via pipes.

If the Coke Oven happens to fully fill up on Creosote that you are not using, break and replace the Coke Oven block. Automated fluid voiding is available later in the game.

Lore: The concept of multiblocks is a modded Minecraft classic. Think of IC2's Nuclear Reactor, or Railcraft's Coke Oven - truly revolutionary at the time!

quests.title: Onwards to the Age of Bronze!
quests.subtitle: Gather more resources!
quests.desc:
More resources are always nice. You're getting close to entering the age of Steam - Copper and Tin are required in many Steam Age machines, so check the ore tab to find out what veins they can be found in, and get mining!

If the different ore names are confusing, you can tell which elements an ore holds at a glance by reading the molecular formula in its tooltip. Copper is Cu, Tin is Sn, Iron is Fe, and you can consult a periodic table for the rest. It's time to brush up on your chemistry - you'll need it for later.

Be sure to collect lots, as a certain Copper-Tin alloy will be needed to get through the Steam Age.

quests.title: Bronze Age? No, Steam Age!
quests.subtitle: Humanity's first alloy - and yours too!
quests.desc:
Bronze is an alloy of Copper and Tin. Grind down 3 parts Copper and 1 part Tin into dust, then combine and smelt them. If you are confused on how this is done, take a look at the "More Tools" quest.

Don't spend your entire supply of Copper and Tin on Bronze right now! You'll soon be able to get a better ratio for your Bronze.

The progression now continues in the Steam Age chapter.

Lore: Bronze in real life is closer to a ratio of 7:1 for Copper to Tin. Here (and in most of modded Minecraft) this is not the case for the sake of game balance. There are a lot of things like this that need to be tweaked - no realism here.

quests.title: The Bucket
quests.subtitle: The Bucket
quests.desc: Fluid handling is much more important in GT than vanilla Terraria. You should probably make more than one of these.

quests.title: Charcoal?
quests.subtitle: Not here!
quests.desc:
This is GregTech - charcoal smelting is not possible in a Furnace. You'll need to make a Coke Oven to get your grubby hands on some charcoal.

It'll come in handy for steel production and circuit parts later. Finding a coal vein can also get you lots of coal for the same purposes.

quests.title: More mods?
quests.subtitle: Omw to install LuiAFK
quests.desc:
This mod is playable on it's own, but should be compatible (although not neccessarily integrated) with other Terraria mods.

 Feel free to add Magic Storage to reduce earlygame storage nightmares, or Fargo's Mutant Mod for its various utilities, or basically anything.

quests.title: A Minor Test of Strength
quests.subtitle: Put your micro-crafting talent to the task!
quests.desc:
Don't worry, these crafts are actually useful - you'll be required to make hundreds more of these components. It's good to start building the habit.

It's generally wise to get familiar with the idea of "bulk-crafting" - if you need 1 plate, try making 8, etc. This means you'll have lots of these intermediates ready for future crafts. It might seem unreasonable to craft stacks of rods or screws especially this early on, but they will always end up being used, saving you a lot of micro-crafting in the future.

quests.title: Early Investments
quests.subtitle: Sometimes, you gotta be inefficient... to be efficient!
quests.desc:
Iron, Copper and Tin will only get you so far. Redstone and Diamond will occasionally be required during the Steam Age, whereas Nickel, Lead and Silver are also nice to have. All will come in handy soon enough.

Consult the ore tab if you're struggling to find these materials!

quests.title: Important Information
quests.subtitle: Read this, and read it again!
quests.desc:
Be sure to properly read this quest - it has some important information that will remain relevant all throughout your playthrough.

Informative quests such as this will appear in the chapter they are unlocked. You can also consult the "Guides + More" quest tab if you feel like you're forgetting something.

The quest structure is intended to guide you through all the major milestones in GregTech progression. Every single quest should be beneficial to look at in some way - if something is 100% optional, we'll mention it, so don't worry about missing anything crucial.

Lastly, it's good to be savvy with TMI. GregTech items will frequently have several recipes, so you should never assume the first recipe TMI displays is the most efficient. "Professional" TMI usage involves the identification of patterns in autogenerated recipes (example: all the Iron related items macerate into Iron Dust).

When complex recipes arise - and we won't sugarcoat, GTCEu can be intimidating - we'll make sure to direct you towards the more efficient routes. However, we urge you to experiment on your own. It's no fun to follow the quest book by rote, right?

quests.title: Tutorialised
quests.subtitle: Tips and tricks to help you prosper
quests.desc:
New to this updated pack is a Guides + More section. The information inside is available to view at any time, but whenever it becomes relevant, you will be directed towards it for some light reading.

The information found inside may interest you if you would like more of an insight into some of GTCEu's mechanics - and in some cases, is absolutely indispensable.

Quests that unlock a tutorial node will have an orange-ish descriptor at the bottom of the quest description.

Get reading!

quests.title: Boss Battle
quests.subtitle: Hey Cthulu, I think you have some metal in your eye
quests.desc:
Bosses will drop large quanites of GT resources to help with your progression, in addition to their normal drops.
The intended boss progression is as follows:

Steam: King Slime

LV: Eye of Cthulu, Eater of Worlds/Brain of Cthulu, Deerclops

MV: Queen Bee, Skeletron, Wall of Flesh

HV: Flying Dutchman, The Destroyer, The Twins, Skeletron Prime

EV: Queen Slime, Plantera

IV: Mourning Wood, Pumpking, Everscream, Santa-NK1, Ice Queen

LuV: Golem. Martian Saucer

ZPM: Duke Fishron, Lunatic Cultist

 UV: Lunar Pillars, Empress of Light, Moon Lord

quests.title: What Else Can I Play?
quests.desc:
Like 2D Gregtech but interested in 3D?

We've gathered some GregTech Modern modpacks in this quest.

Monifactory
Monifactory is a 1.20.1 modpack based on Nomifactory CEu (which was on 1.12.2!). It aims to streamline and simplify the earlier parts of GregTech, while interweaving other tech mods into GTCEu Modern's progression. It also features a Hard and an Expert mode for the challenge-minded!

Cosmic Frontiers
- Ghostipedia et al.

Cosmic Frontiers is a 1.20.1 modpack with a focus on interactions between many mods and GTCEu Modern. You'll explore the stars above and travel to the depths below, all whilst tackling intricately woven progression that ties together the mechanical and the arcane.

In its current state, the pack is considered to be in early alpha. However, progress has been curated through LuV. It's an experience like no other!

All the Mods: Gravitas 2
- ATMTeam

Starting from primitive Stone Age technology, hand-knapping your tools and foraging for food, you'll need to progress through the ages covering many classic and new mods. This is seamlessly complemented by mods such as TerraFirmaCraft, Create New Age, Stargate Journey and (obviously) GregTech.

You'll find yourself transitioning from a primitive beginning, smashing rocks together, to smashing atoms together, soon powering your voyages to the stars and beyond!

Star Technology
- trulyno

Star Technology is more of a laid-back GregTech skyblock modpack where you play as a scientist stranded in an alternate dimension after a dimensional travel experiment catastrophically fails.

Progression in this pack will take you above and beyond UHV, with numerous unique Stargates to craft, and even remixed "Gregified" Mystical Agriculture.

Once again, this pack features a Hard mode, and a Desert mode (called Abydos!).

Note that the pack is still under heavy development!

quests.title: We Need To Go Deeper!
quests.subtitle: Plumbing the Depths
quests.desc: Different Ores generate at different depths. You'll find coal quite close to the surface, iron a little deeper, and stuff like diamond and redstone quite deep in the caverns. Nether and end ores spawn all the way down in hell!

quests.title: No More Penalty

tasks.label: Look at all these HP machines...

quests.subtitle: Pressure can be good sometimes
quests.desc:
You may recall that regular Steam machines had a duration penalty, and were twice as slow. Not great.

High Pressure Steam machines no longer bear a duration penalty for recipes, making them objective upgrades.

Make one of the proposed HP machines to complete this quest.

quests.title: High Pressure
quests.subtitle: Pressure! Pushin' down on me...
quests.desc:
High Pressure Boilers will be a better source of Steam for powering your Steam machines.

Keep in mind that you will still be using Steam to power your Electric machines in LV via the miracles of energy conversion.

Note: You will need more than a handful of HP Boilers. The more you make, the less you'll need to worry about running out of power.

The Solid Boiler burns solid Fuel. It's self explanatory - better fuels last longer.

The Liquid Boiler burns either Lava or Creosote.

The Solar Boiler burns nothing - it's fueled by the sun.

Looking for a recommendation? Honestly, either goes, as all three boilers are equivalent in generation. Craft the boiler that best suits your preferred resource.

Get any of the proposed boilers to complete this quest.

quests.title: Primitive Blast Furnace
quests.subtitle: Steel Yourself for Greater Things
quests.desc:
The Primitive Blast Furnace (PBF for short) is a multiblock you will become very familiar with in the next few hours.

It is important that you get a Macerator before tackling this quest - if you haven't made one yet, this multiblock will be four times as expensive.

Using your fancy new Macerator, you'll need to process lots of Clay, split into Clay Dusts and Brick Dusts.

Build the structure using the multiblock preview.

Steel will be of utmost importance throughout the entire game, so make sure you keep that PBF running. We should clarify that the PBF isn't the only way to get Steel throughout your playthrough, though!

The PBF is also much, much faster in this than normal GregTech. Have fun with that.

quests.title: We Need More Fuel!
quests.subtitle: Dropping the air quality
quests.desc:
Sure the PBF runs fast, but only because of its nasty Coke habit

\*ba-dum-tss

Making more coke ovens will greatly increase your ability to feed its addiction. It will also make you lots of extra creosote, which can in turn be used to make steam.

GT Multiblocks can wallshare to save on materials. They can also share hatches for easiy parallellization in the future, although the Coke Oven unfortunately is not able to do this. You can still save 3 bricks in its construction by sharing a wall, though.

It's probably smart to use some simple item and fluid pipes to route coal/wood in and charcoal/coke/creosote out.

Lore:  In GregTech 5, one single Ingot of Steel would take you 360 seconds in the PBF when using Coal or Charcoal! That's over 6 hours for a whole stack! Of course, with 4 PBFs, that would cut it down to around 1.5 hours.

Pollution was also (infamously) a thing, so four of these monsters would guarantee your character would periodically get negative effects such as slowness, mining fatigue, or even nausea and poison.

Pollution is currently not present in GTCEuUp, but it might be soon :).

quests.title: Journey to the Underworld
quests.subtitle: Get Digging
quests.desc:
No obsidian portal this time, you need to dig your way to hell.

 Deeper depths have more ore veins to mine, and with those veins, new ores to discover. Check the Ore Tab to learn more about Underworld Veins.

Underworld veins will take more effort to find - and are more dangerous to mine - than Underground veins.

quests.title: S is for Sulfur
quests.subtitle: A little bit of exploring never hurt anyone
quests.desc:
Mine some Sulfur from the Underworld. It will make rubber quite a bit cheaper.

Lore: With the existence of Small Sulfur Ores scattered in GregTech 5 ore veins, you could mine enough Sulfur in the Overworld to skip having to visit the Nether. Sounds broken, but it was still as much of a grind as Nether travel!

quests.title: The First of Many Motors
quests.subtitle: Low Voltage beckons...
quests.desc:
Good news - we're going to start to cut down on the amount of components you'll need to submit for quests as you progress further into the modpack. Remember that TMI is your true mentor if recipes are getting overwhelming.

Getting back on topic, set yourself up for LV by crafting six LV Electric Motors! This may seem like a lot, but that's the bare minimum required to get to the good stuff in LV.

Once you complete this Quest and the Electronic Circuit Quest, you are ready to move on to the LV Age.

quests.title: Greg has struck again
quests.subtitle: #realism
quests.desc:
Obtaining paper is both  difficult and inefficient. Thanks, Greg.

You'll need to grind Bamboo into Paper Dust, and then smush the dust with a mallet and water.

Note 1: Don't worry, we're not cruel enough to have the recipe consume the bucket.

Note 2: Bamboo grows fairly quickly in the Jungle. However you can start making paper with wood in LV to save yourself future trips if you want.

Note 3: Paper will be used as a poor-quality wrapping material for the upcoming resistors.

Note 4: It is truly a pleasure to share this moment with you, our fellow Greg enjoyer.

Note 5: This is the last note I can write for you, as I am now out of paper.

quests.title: Armoured Tanks
quests.subtitle: Just kidding, tanks for fluids
quests.desc:
GregTech can quickly get overwhelming with the plethora of fluids it introduces, so you may be looking for a way to store them. 

Introducing... Drums! They have large internal buffers, and any Drum can be broken and picked up and will keep the fluid inside.

Additionally, internal tanks from machines will interact with items containing fluid. This is great for manual crafting - this means you can use fluid drums instead of multiple buckets in crafting. To void fluid contained in a Drum, craft it into itself.

Lore: These drums are actually ported from GregTech 6.

quests.title: Primitive Water Pump
quests.subtitle: There's no such thing as "free" water
quests.desc:
The Primitive Water Pump is an easy way to automatically generate water for your boilers. It doesn't physically pump water from the world, instead generating it internally. Therefore, you don't need access to a body of water to make use of it.

It's a multiblock!

This should be more than enough for a few boilers, as they don't need much water to keep up.

The Output Hatch is where water will be generated. Use pipes to move it out. Later you can use a ULV or LV Output Hatch to further boost the Pump's output.

Lore: This Multiblock was ported from GT IMPACT, a classic 1.7.10 GregTech modpack.

quests.title: Malding
quests.subtitle: Reduce the amount of malding by molding
quests.desc: Make the listed molds for future alloy smelting. Be sure to make Molds - don't make Extruder Shapes, as they're used in a different machine.

quests.title: Steam Extractor
quests.subtitle: The Steam Extractor extracts...
quests.desc: Not many items are made in the Extractor during the Steam Age, but it's used to make Rubber, which you'll need after Steel.

quests.title: Sticky Resin
quests.subtitle: Mitosis Jones
quests.desc: Rubber wood can be used after getting electricty, but for now you need lots and lots of gel for sticky resin. Get hunting! King Slime can be a good source if you're running low, just be sure you can consistently defeat him so you don't lose the 20 needed for the summon.

quests.title: Rubbering up
quests.subtitle: Put that resin to use!
quests.desc:
Rubber is an excellent insulation material, and that's exactly what it's used for in GTCEu.

If you've found sulfur you'll want to use the Alloy Smelter to make Rubber Bars, but they can be made at a higher sticky resin cost without the sulfur. These ratios aren't particularly great, but we promise that making Rubber Bars will be much more efficient at LV.

Lore: In base GregTech 5 (and in base GTCE), you needed to get string and black wool carpets as your first insulation material. That was obviously far, far more fun.

quests.title: Electronics #1: Resistors
quests.subtitle: The easier component for circuits
quests.desc:
Resistors are components that will be used to make your first Circuit.

It wouldn't be unwise to prioritise the recipes that use Fine Wires, as they'll save you more materials in the long run.

Wires may be expensive right now, but they'll get a lot cheaper in LV.

quests.title: Fluid Pipes
quests.subtitle: Liquids, gases... we don't discriminate
quests.desc:
Fluid Pipes will transport Steam between machines for you.

Each Pipe has a certain throughput in mB/t, and a Temperature Limit. This is relevant right now, as Steam will burn Simple Pipes. Some pipes have special attributes you don't need to worry about for now.

Each material has several sizes for Pipes. The bigger the Pipe, the higher the throughput, but the more expensive the craft. For example, a normal Bronze Fluid Pipe transfers 120 mB/t.

Get any Bronze Fluid Pipe to complete this quest. The small and normal ones in particular are used in other recipes as well.

Reminder: 1 second = 20 ticks... assuming the server isn't lagging (Yes, even in Terraria, for GregTech purposes anyway).
quests.title: Pressure Cookin'
quests.subtitle: Putting the Steam in Steam Age
quests.desc:
Boilers! You can get by with only one, but we would recommend making at least two. The solid boiler burns coal or wood, the liquid lava or creosote, and the solar one uses the power of the sun.

Important: Boilers can explode! This still catches out experienced GT players, so keep this warning in mind when setting your Boiler up.

Water does not gel well with high temperatures, and a Steam Explosion (Launch!) may occur under the following conditions:

- The Boiler is overheating. Its temperature is indicated by the red bar in the GUI.
...and...
- The Boiler does not have any water. This is signified by the absence of a blue bar in the GUI.
...AND...
- The Boiler receives water whilst both conditions are true.

You can avert catastrophe by filling the boiler with water before heating it up, and making sure the water supply is constant. The drain is slow - 1 mB of Water boils into 160 mB of Steam - so you shouldn't have to worry about running out.

Use pipes to automatically pipe water from a primitive pump!

Note: In this mod, explosions are configured to not damage any block or entity, but you'll still lose your machine.

quests.title: Steam Machine Behaviour
quests.subtitle: Surprisingly Steamy
quests.desc:
Steam Machines accept any Steam that is pumped from nearby Boilers into their internal storage tank. They will run recipes from TMI up to 32 EU/t (LV-tier).

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on Steam in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Steam Alloy Smelter
quests.subtitle: The Alloy Smelter makes alloys...
quests.desc:
The Alloy Smelter is used for, well, alloys. You'll want it for the efficient Bronze recipe, and for Red Alloy in the near future.

Out of all the Steam Age machines, this is the most steam-hungry. If you ever find yourself running out of steam, give your machine a love-tap with any Soft Mallet to momentarily pause it.

quests.title: Electronics #3: Vacuum Tubes
quests.subtitle: The harder component for circuits
quests.desc:
Crafting Vacuum Tubes might make you question your existence. Sorry about that.

This is mostly due to the fact that Wires and Rods are (sadly) very expensive right now, but they'll get cheaper in LV.

Vacuum Tubes are also technically your first Ultra Low Voltage (ULV) Circuit! Congratulations! You won't find very much use for these by themselves, though.

quests.title: Glass, at last!
quests.subtitle: I can see through you
quests.desc:
Glass can be expensive on sand, but it will be cheaper later on. You've probably already noticed that juicy Arc Furnace recipe - don't worry, we'll get there soon.

Glass tubes will be required for your first Electronic Circuit.

quests.title: Red Alloy
quests.subtitle: A completely fictional alloy
quests.desc:
Red Alloy is an alloy of Copper and Redstone, prized in the GregTech universe for its conductivity.

You will need some for Electronic Circuits.

Lore: This material originally comes from RedPower - what a throwback!

quests.title: Electronics #4: The Circuit
quests.subtitle: Your first Electronic Circuit!
quests.desc:
We made it to LV! Give yourself a pat on the back!

We also want you to make an LV Machine Hull for this quest. You'll need one if you're to make any LV machine.

Circuits are an integral part of progression in GregTech, so keep climbing.

Once you complete this Quest and the LV Motor Quest, you are ready to move on to the LV Age.

quests.title: Potin Seller...
quests.subtitle: ...I'm going into battle, and I need only your finest Potin.
quests.desc:
Potin Fluid Pipes have a very high throughput for the material tier and cost. You may want to look into making some of these to aid with fluid logistics.

To get Potin, you should start by crafting its Dust form.

Get a Potin pipe of any size to complete this quest.

Lore: Potin Fluid Pipes were first seen in GT++, an addon to GT5, and are a considerable power spike for players in a relatively unknown pack called GT:NH. We wanted to spread some of that joy.

In the real world, Potin is an alloy used in coins, not pipes. GTCEu just gets more unrealistic by the day...

quests.title: Steam Forge Hammer
quests.subtitle: The Forge Hammer hammers...
quests.desc:
With the creation of this machine, many recipes that once required you to use a hammer can instead be made here!

It can process Stone into Silt, and Silt into Sand - and it's zippy at that to boot.

Last but not least, it makes Plates at a better ratio: three Ingots for two Plates. A worthwhile investment!

quests.title: Steam Macerator
quests.subtitle: The Macerator macerates...
quests.desc:
The Macerator can grind up just about anything. Processing a single piece of Iron Ore in this machine will take twenty seconds - twice the time it takes to smelt an ingot in a furnace!

The Macerator also marks the advent of Ore Processing, but that's a scary topic that we'll dip our toes into in the next quest.

The macerator will output only the first item shown in recipes from TMI. The other slots are locked until you make a HV Macerator. How unfortunate...

Our (important!) advice: it's actually better to keep directly smelting for now! The efficiency loss is made up with faster mining. Use the Macerator to make things like Clay Dust instead!

quests.title: Invar
quests.subtitle: An Iron-Nickel alloy
quests.desc: An important alloy for later. Can make tools too, if you'd like.

quests.title: Electronics #2: Circuit Boards
quests.subtitle: The circuit-er part of circuits...
quests.desc:
Chop wood into wood plates and combine it with Resin to make an empty board.

Stick in some expensive Copper Wires (these'll get cheaper in LV) and you've got the base for your first Circuit.

Almost there!

quests.title: What is this Mess?!
quests.subtitle: ...and how do I get rid of it?
quests.desc:
In its simplest form, Ore Processing in GregTech boils down to converting one Ore Block into two Ingots through two Dusts, like several other tech mods. 

The added spice is the amount of steps, either optional or required.

Ore Processing will be explained further once you get into LV.

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on rudimentary Ore Processing in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Steam Grinder
quests.subtitle: The Steam Oven's elder brother
quests.desc:
...or perhaps it was the opposite?

The Steam Grinder can pulverize large batches of items quickly and effortlessly, sort of like a parallel Macerator.

This thing is incredibly good for its tier. It's actually as strong as 5.3 HV Macerators, and only requires a trickle of Steam to power.

Lore: This Multiblock originally comes from GT++. It required Tumbaga to craft, an alloy with a mix of Gold and Bronze.

quests.title: Steam Oven
quests.subtitle: The Steam Grinder's big brother
quests.desc:
...or little brother. Don't let him know!

The Steam Oven is a multiblock option if you need to smelt large quantities of items automatically.

It effectively behaves as a parallel furnace, smelting up to 8 items at a time for a low cost of Steam.


Lore: This Multiblock - despite being very similar to its brother, the Steam Grinder - isn't ported from a GregTech version or addon. Its actual progenitor is the Steam Oven from Railcraft, which dates back to 1.4.7!

quests.title: Steam Compressor
quests.subtitle: The Steam Compressor compresses...
quests.desc: The Steam Compressor unlocks some tantalising new recipes that will help you to progress towards Steel.

quests.title: Item Pipes
quests.subtitle: All your logistics woes are solved
quests.desc:
Item Pipes are a simple yet effective way to move items around instantly, but with throughput limitations.

Higher tier pipes provide ridiculously large throughput.

These are more complex than simple item pipes, as they effectively have a robot arm cover on them. Select "active" and then pull/push to make them tick. You can also set filter, amount stocked, etc.

Also do note that the filter is enabled by default. Turn if off if you just need whatever to flow through.

The priority mechanics are somewhat complicated, so skip ahead if you aren't interested:

A Destination will have a Routing Value calculated, which is the sum of all "Routing Values" of the individual Pipes to that Destination. Whichever Destination has the lowest Routing Value will be the one selected for Insertion.

Restrictive Pipes typically have the lowest Priority for insertion due to their higher Routing Value.

Get any of the suggested Item Pipes to complete this quest.

Lore: Oooooh, YEAH! Item Pipes were in GregTech 5, and for whatever reason they weren't kept for GTCE, so you had to use other mods to transport items. Who does that!? Jokes aside, we hope you enjoy Item Pipes being back in GregTech!

quests.title: Multiblock Tanks
quests.subtitle: So long, and Tanks for all the fish!
quests.desc:
You DO have access to some beefy fluid storage right now, but you'll need to make a Multiblock to access it!

The Wooden Multiblock Tank holds 250,000 mB, but you won't be able to use it to store hot Fluids such as Lava, or Gases such as Steam or Oxygen.

The Bronze Multiblock Tank holds 500,000 mB, lifiting the maximum fluid tempreture and can now store Gases. 

The Steel Multiblock Tank holds 1,000,000 mB, and lifts all the aforementioned fluid restrictions.

Get any of the Tanks to complete this quest. The Steel version is way too expensive right now - so you should probably make the Wooden  or Bronze one instead.

Fluid transfer can either be facilitated through the Controller block, or through up to two Valves.

Unlike Drums, these Tanks aren't portable... but you probably guessed that already.

Lore: Just like Drums, these multiblocks are ported from GregTech 6.

quests.title: Aesthetic Ultra
quests.subtitle: Spaghetti has never looked so swanky!
quests.desc:
Keep in mind that pipes can be placed behind blocks! They are on their own layer.

They'd probably look pretty cool behind a frame.

quests.title: Lv Fluid Regulator
quests.subtitle: A cover!
quests.desc:
The Fluid Regulator is sort of the equivalent to a Robot Arm for Fluids.

It can transfer fluids, but has two useful modes:

- Supply Exact will transfer the amount of specified Fluid per tick if available. No more, no less.
- Keep Exact will make sure the exact amount of Fluid in the attached machine is being kept.

quests.title: Lv Robot Arm
quests.subtitle: You're going to hate making these
quests.desc:
When placed on a machine, Robot Arms are a more configurable version of a Conveyor Module. They allow you to transfer items in specific batches, at specific rates, or keep a certain amount of items stocked.

Practical uses for the Robot Arm will be explained in future Quests as a tutorial.

Note: The functionalities provided by the Robot Arm work only if it is required to perform the operation.

Lore: GregTech 5 had blocks such as the Buffer, Filter and Type Filter to allow fine control over items. In GTCE it's all been replaced by the almighty Robot Arm and various Filter Covers. Much more compact!

quests.title: Lv Conveyor Module
quests.subtitle: A crafting component, but also a cover!
quests.desc:
Less common as a component, but used in a number of critical machines.

When placed on a machine, conveyor modules will transfer items. You can configure them to import or export.

They can also accept various Item Filters.

quests.title: Lv Electric Pump
quests.subtitle: A crafting component, but also a cover!
quests.desc:
When placed on a machine, pumps will transfer fluid. You can configure them to import or export.

It can accept a Fluid Filter.

quests.title: Lv Electric Piston
quests.subtitle: A crafting component - did you expect anything fancier?
quests.desc:
A specialized motor needed to make certain machines.

One of the more intricate crafting components, as it's made using a motor.

quests.title: Electricity Generation
quests.subtitle: You must construct more turbines
quests.desc:
The Steam Turbine is the only Generator you can realistically use in LV. Later, you'll unlock more interesting Power options, such as natural gases or petrols.

When provided Steam, the turbine will output EU at a ratio of 2 Steam = 1 EU.

This quest unlocks two new tutorials on Electricity and Cables in the Tips and Tricks tab - check them out to learn more!

If you're using a single Steam Turbine, you only need 1x Tin Cables. On the other hand, if you upgrade to 2 Steam Turbines, you should use 2x Tin Cables - else they may end up burning.

This is because of the Amperage mechanic... but that will be explained later so you can enjoy playing. Don't worry!

quests.title: But what are Amps?
quests.subtitle: Amps and You: a how-to
quests.desc:
This questline serves as a quick guide to GregTech Amperage. As for Voltage mechanics, we'll go over those once you reach MV.

TL;DR: For safety, match the Amperage limit of your Cables with the max amount of Amps provided by your Energy Producers.

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on Amperage in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Aluminium Ingot
quests.subtitle: We're basically in MV now, right?
quests.desc:
Well... can you show us an MV Circuit as well?

Aluminium is the main ingredient for MV machinery. Check aaaaaall the way to the other side for the Sapphire Quest if you haven't acquired any Aluminium Dust yet.

Once you setup an MV Electrolyzer, you can get Aluminium through many other ores. All of this quest's rewards bear Aluminium, so keep an eye out for them when mining.

The process of smelting Aluminium can be cut down by about a third with Nitrogen, which is obtained by centrifuging Air from a Gas Collector.

You will still need MV Circuits in order to make MV machines!

quests.title: Battery Preparations!
quests.subtitle: Batteries are great!
quests.desc:
Make some Battery Alloy, used for Battery Hulls.

Antimony is found in the Nether, so don't forget to check its Ore Tab.

Note: This quest does not contain functional Batteries - head downwards for that.

quests.title: GregTech Storage
quests.subtitle: Earlygame buffering
quests.desc:
Crates are flat upgrades to Chests. A single block space that stores more items - what more could you ask for?

Where they shine is the fact that they accept Covers, making them perfect candidates for buffers in automation.

quests.title: Cover Behaviour
quests.subtitle: Extra utility without taking up blockspace!
quests.desc:
Covers are placed by right-clicking with Cover in hand on a face of a machine, and removed with a Crowbar (or by mining the machine, but that's way slower).

Pretty much any GregTech "machine" will accept Covers, even if they wouldn't provide any functionality.

Opening a Cover is done by sneak-right-clicking it with an empty hand. You can also right-click with a Screwdriver, or sneak-right-click on another face using the grid.

For Covers that transfer Items/Fluids, you will have a button to enable/disable other forms of transfer through the cover:

- Disabled: only the cover will be allowed to transfer on this machine side.

- Allow Filtered (only relevant with a filter!): other transfer will be enabled only if it matches the filter

- Allow Unfiltered: any other transfer is allowed on this machine side.

Placing a cover of a lower voltage tier on a machine is safe.

quests.title: Machine Auto-Output Behaviour
quests.subtitle: Let's learn.
quests.desc:
All machines typically have one Front Face, and one Output Face.

The Front Face should be obvious.\nThe Output Face is the face with a dot or hole on it. By default, this is at the back of the machine as you place it.

Machines can auto-output through their output face. To enable auto-output, click the appropriate button in the GUI. Fluid and item auto-outputs are toggled separately. Further control over outputs can be achieved with Filters and Covers, which are explained in the quest to the upper left of this one.

Right-clicking a machine with a Wrench changes the output side, and shift-right-clicking changes the front side. Keep in mind that the front side cannot also be the output side! 

By default, items and fluids cannot be inserted through the output side, but this can be toggled with a Screwdriver. Either right-click the output face directly, or shift-right-click on the grid corresponding to the side of the output.

There are unique machines that do not process recipes, such as the Pump, Miner, Fisher, Item Collector, etc. These machines will output on their Front Face (which is still marked as a dot).

quests.title: Basic Wiremill
quests.subtitle: No more painful wires!
quests.desc:
You could make any other LV Machine, but it would be easiest to start with the most useful one. We know, we know... you wanna make some of the crazier stuff first, but trust us, this is the best starting point.

The Wiremill lets you make two Wires from one Ingot. That should be three times cheaper than what you were doing up until now! Plus, aren't you sick of crafting Wire Cutters?

To the right of this Quest, you'll find some important machines which will grant you cheaper intermediates.

Note: Recipes inside LV Machines go up to a maximum usage of 32 EU/t (LV). Anything higher than that and you will need a MV (or better) Machine. Needless to say, we aren't quite there yet.

You will need a Programmed Circuit for certain recipes. We're sure you don't want to spend a precious Circuit to craft one, so read the dedicated quest for important details.

With this machine safely crafted - welcome to the age of all things electric!

quests.title: Basic Bender
quests.subtitle: The Bender bends!
quests.desc:
Arguably the second best LV machine to craft, but we're down if you want to argue otherwise.

This unlocks the ability to convert one Ingot into one Plate, which is a better ratio than the Forge Hammer.

quests.title: Basic Lathe
quests.subtitle: The Lathe lathes!
quests.desc:
The Lathe is an important machine to help shape metals. Thanks to it, you can turn one Ingot into one Rod and 2 small piles of Dust, and one Bolt into one screw. That's twice the material efficiency on those recipes as opposed to crafting by hand. 

However the MV Extruder has the ability to turn one Ingot directly into two Rods alongside its many other functions.

Lore: The GT6 Lathe acts like GTm's lathe, but Bolts lathe into one Screw and 1/72 of a pile of Dust. Disgusting!

quests.title: Basic Polariser
quests.subtitle: The Polarizer polarizes!
quests.desc: One of the cheapest - if not the cheapest - LV machine. Use this to make Redstone-free Magnetic Rods for your Motors. Whenever you start to batch your Motors, this machine will save you stacks and stacks of Redstone.

quests.title: Magnet
quests.subtitle: Why can't these items I mine behave and enter my inventory!?
quests.desc:
Sneak right-click when in hand to activate your Magnet. Do it again to deactivate. The battery capacity of your magnet depends on the battery used to craft it.

If you're worried about getting overwhelmed by Cobblestone or Netherrack, use the trash can from FTB Utilities (top left corner of your inventory) to void them

Note: The magnet has a whitelist filter turned on by default (in other words, it's not going to pick anything up.) Right-click the magnet to tinker with the item filter settings. There's even a tag filter - you could, for example, whitelist only raw ores!

quests.title: Bronze Large Boiler
quests.subtitle: Boom, boom, boom, boom
quests.desc:
The Large Boiler is an optional big-boy variant of the Small Boilers. It runs off solid or liquid fuel.

If you want Steam-based power production, this will be your first port-of-call. Other power options are listed in the MV chapter, if you want to invest in those, you can skip this quest with the checkmark.

One "easy" fuel option is producing Charcoal from Coke Ovens. One piece of Charcoal lasts 20s in the LBB and the wood can be sourced from a tree farm.

Important: This Boiler CAN also explode similarly to the regular Boilers! Usually, the explosion can be rather devastating... but it's configured to not damage blocks or entities in this modpack.

Water supply is crucial. Dedicate a Primitive Pump with a LV Output Hatch in the same chunk as the Boiler. Any biome will still be enough to supply enough water.

Once fully heated up, the LBB produces 800 Steam per tick, which is 400EU/t after conversion, or 12.5 Basic Steam Turbines. Such (relatively!) immense power comes at the cost of automating fuel.

With this in consideration, the GUI lets you throttle the Boiler down to 25% power for 200 Steam per tick. That's 100EU/t, or slightly above 3 Basic Steam Turbines.

Note: An in-depth tutorial about Multiblocks is given after you get the Electric Blast Furnace. We recommend you aim for that first.

Lore: The LBB used to be terrible, however it recently got rebalanced in 7.3.0 to make it actually viable to use.

quests.title: LV Superconductor
quests.subtitle: We aren't losing a single Amp more
quests.desc:
Superconductors are special Wires available for each Voltage tier. They don't come with a Cable counterpart, but that isn't necessary, as they have absolutely zero cable loss.

They also won't damage you when you touch them. This makes them the best choice for wiring, if not a little expensive in comparison to the other wires.

If you weren't already enamoured, the 1x variant also covers 2 Amps. Isn't that nice?

Now is a good time to replace your crappy Tin Cables. Recycle them in the Packager to recover both the Tin and Rubber used to craft them.

Can't figure out how to make some of these? Here's a hint - Tricalcium Phosphate and Pyrolusite ought to help.


Lore: No version of GregTech has had actual Superconductors for LV up until Gregicality and GTCEu, though some modpacks had lossless cables.

quests.title: Basic Thermal Centrifuge
quests.subtitle: No, not THAT kind of Thermal
quests.desc:
The Thermal Centrifuge is the most energy hungry out of the Ore Processing machines. It takes Crushed Ore or Purified Ore to give a different Byproduct from the Ore Washer, but at the same rate.

Lore: The GT5 Thermal Centrifuge required 48 EU/t to run. Yes, this may be above 32 EU/t, but GT5 had special mechanics where certain machines such as this one could accept more than 1 Amp.

In GTCE, the Thermal Centrifuge was changed to only work at MV with 60EU/t recipes. Let's be completely honest with you... what a complete waste of time! Never worth it. Thankfully, we've buffed the machine to be much more relevant.

quests.title: Lv Sifter
quests.subtitle: You're as Exquisite as the gems you sift!
quests.desc:
The Sifter sifts Gem type Purified Ores to find Exquisite and Flawless Gems, which can be cut into normal Gems. It also finds Purified Dusts of that gem. Overall, this gives a higher yield than other means.

A small amount of Flawless Emeralds will be required in MV, and running ores in Sifting Machine is how you obtain these.

Note: If you crushed Gem Ores (such as Diamond), it is critical you use this machine to recover the actual gems in non dust form. However, this is a rather slow process, so you may want to smelt this type of ore directly into the Gem.

quests.title: Basic Chemical Bath
quests.subtitle: The Chemical Bath bathes...
quests.desc:
Outside of Ore Processing:

You can make Paper out of Wood so that you no longer rely on Sugar Canes*. You're probably sick of them by now, especially if you've played Nomifactory.

There are some other less important recipes, such as applying colours to various items and blocks, or bleaching them.

* Sugar is still useful as a requirement for TNT production in HV, so don't dismantle your setups.

For Ore Processing:

Some Crushed Ores have alternate routes when washed into Purified Ore, involving bathing in either Mercury or Sodium Persulfate. This step isn't really important, unless you really want that special Byproduct. This can be a nice alternate source of Gold (hint, hint!).

quests.title: Basic Ore Washing Plant
quests.subtitle: Wash that mouth out
quests.desc:
This machine combines washing for simple automation, but also advanced and precise washing for byproducts.

Washing a Crushed Ore will grant a Purified Ore which can be refined further, some useless stone, and more importantly, the first Byproduct!

Any Ore will give at minimum 2/3rd of a Dust of this Byproduct through washing. Prioritize this step the most.

Using Distilled Water over Water cuts recipe duration in half, but it's also more difficult to produce.

quests.title: Programmed Circuits
quests.subtitle: Don't want to spend your circuits to select recipes?
quests.desc:
By clicking the Circuit Configuration Slot in machines, you can create a ghost Programmed Circuit. 

This is a "fake circuit" that allows you to select a configuration for recipes. There's no need to spend your circuits crafting Programmed Circuits anymore.

The purpose of this ghost circuit is to prevent recipe clashes - for example, the same Wiremill can produce wires of different thicknesses, with the same material!

Electric Machines and Item Input Buses will have a Ghost Circuit Slot.

Lore: This feature is originally from GTNH.

quests.title: Basic Canner
quests.subtitle: This machine does nothing exciting
quests.desc:
Make this machine when you feel like it's time you invested into Batteries.

It'll also be required if you want to make Storage Disks from Applied Energistics 2.

quests.title: Batteries!
quests.subtitle: Batteries for storing energy!
quests.desc:
Batteries can be put into the energy slot at the bottom middle of machine GUIs, or in a Battery Buffer.

When inside your inventory, they can be sneak-right-clicked to allow the batteries to charge your EU items.

LV, MV, and HV Batteries come in three forms, allowing you to pick the one you find the best depending on materials you have. Lithium has the best capacity, followed by Cadmium, then Sodium.

Our recommendation? Find a Salt vein, and either electrolyze Salt for Sodium, or wash crushed Lepidolite for Lithium.

Get any Battery to complete this quest.

quests.title: Battery Buffer
quests.desc:
By itself, the Battery Buffer does nothing. However, if you place batteries inside its internal storage, it will act as a power buffer.

The Battery Buffer handles two Amps in, and one Amp out, per Battery. The output side has the dot, and all other sides are used as inputs.

Our recommendation is to have your generators input energy into it, and power your machines from the output side. This is because the Battery Buffers will demand many Amps of power when it charges, and this could prevent you from using your other machines if it's set up in a way where it can divert power from your other machines to itself.

It'll also charge both EU and RF Tools inside it.

This is a must have if you don't have unlimited power at hand. Get any LV Battery Buffer to finish the quest. However, the 16 slots variant is likely overkill at this stage.

Note: Don't loop your lossy Cables back to your Battery Buffer! If you think about it, you'll just end up voiding energy.

Lore: To be frank with you, the GTCE Battery Buffer was... mediocre. It's been reworked in GTCEu to provide a mix between GT5 and GTCE's functionality. Energy now splits evenly across all batteries!

quests.title: Electric Blast Furnace
quests.subtitle: A big milestone!
quests.desc:
This Quest explains the core mechanic of the Electric Blast Furnace, whilst the quest below will explore the mechanics of the Multiblock Parts that you'll also need. Be sure to refer to them.

Anyway, the EBF is your first electric multiblock! Congrats. It's used to process materials at high temperatures.

You'll become familiar with the EBF to make Aluminium and progress to MV Age. It can also make Steel faster than a PBF.

It's important to keep in mind that the EBF is a beefy boy. The minimum power cost to run a recipe is 120 EU/t! That's slightly under 4 LV Generators. This is why it was locked behind the Battery Quest. You may wish to consider building a separate power line, with separate Generators and Battery Buffer to run this.

quests.title: Basic Autoclave
quests.subtitle: "Oops, I turned my Diamonds into dust"
quests.desc:
The Autoclave is typically the last step of Gem Processing. It is used turn a select few Gem Dusts back into regular Gems.

It's veeeery slow - thus, it is generally not advised to crush gem ores at all, as the recovery process is too expensive.

Note: In this modpack, the Autoclave is required to make Fluix Crystals if you're to progress in Applied Energistics. Sorry.

quests.title: Basic Electromagnetic Separator
quests.subtitle: Magnets... how do they work?
quests.desc: A rarely used machine. It uses magnetism to separate some Magnetic ores and gain additional Byproducts you would not obtain otherwise.

quests.title: What is this Mess?: Part Two
quests.subtitle: You can't escape Greg...
quests.desc:
This quest is a light look over the full scale of regular Ore Processing. Why "regular"? Some ores require special treatment, but that's a topic for much later. Oh no, we're already getting carried away!

Each quest surrounding this one gives a different machine that has some involvement in Ore Processing.

Note: Don't get too preoccupied with Ore Processing! It's very slow at this point, and somewhat power hungry. Around 90% of ores are not important enough to warrant going out of your way to obtain their byproducts. That's a legitimate stat, by the way.

quests.title: Progressing Through LV
quests.subtitle: Too many options, too little time!
quests.desc:
LV will require you to craft a lot of different machines! You may also find it useful to have multiple of the same machine, set to different programmed circuit values.

Here's a friendly tip if you're struggling to set goals: pick an item in a later quest and go backwards from there.

With that in mind... why don't we start with a Fusion Reactor? Never mind, slightly too ambitious - let's check a closer goal instead. Ah, yes, the Electric Blast Furnace - the main "end goal" of LV - should be a good target.

The EBF is expensive, which is why you should initially focus on machines to make components and materials cheaper. We'll do our best to guide you in the right direction with these quests.

Other important goals are crafting the LV Assembler and making your first MV Circuits.

quests.title: Basic Fluid Solidifier
quests.subtitle: The Fluid Solidifier solidifies...
quests.desc:
Build this machine next to your Chemical Reactor to automatically solidify Rubber into Rubber Sheets.

You can make your Chemical Reactor auto-output if you wrench the side of your Chemical Reactor and enable Fluid auto-output.

There will be more Fluids to solidify, so you may re-purpose your Fluid Solidifier when not crafting Rubber - or make a second one!

quests.title: This is Hacking
quests.subtitle: (Ok, maybe not)
quests.desc:
The Prospector is an amazing tool that'll help you find veins in no time.

Its GUI is interactive. Right-clicking will scan for ores, displaying them on a dynamic map.

Lore: Back in OUR day, the portable Prospector was locked to LuV. Kids these days have it easy.

quests.title: Cupronickel Coils
quests.subtitle: Baby's first heating coil
quests.desc:
Prepare 128 Cupronickel, 32 Bronze and 16 Tin Alloy to craft the Heating Coils required for your first Electric Blast Furnace.

This is still less than half (!) of the cost of the whole EBF in raw materials. You will also need about a stack each of Invar and Steel, so get mining.

The Cupronickel Coils will allow the EBF to perform recipes up to 1,800K (ignoring Voltage bonuses). That's pretty hot if you ask us.

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on Coils in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Universal Macerator
quests.subtitle: What? Why?!
quests.desc:
You: That's it, I'm uninstalling! This pack is stupid! We're barely in LV, but you're asking me to make you a HV machine!?

Yes! This is indeed not completable yet, which is also why this quest appears in the HV Chapter.

Remember how disappointing it was that you couldn't get byproducts with your Macerator? Well... that disappointment won't be going away until you reach HV.

The Macerator will unlock its second and third slot at HV. Trust us - this is great news for your Ore Processing. In fact, one byproduct is completely locked behind this (after the Thermal Centrifuge step).

Note: Unlike GTCE, the MV Macerator in GTCEu doesn't have a second slot.

Writer: Come back to me when you are stronger, young Cricket.

quests.title: Basic Gas Collector
quests.subtitle: Time to sell some Air
quests.desc:
The Gas Collector collects Air that can then be centrifuged into Oxygen and Nitrogen.

Lore: GregTech 5 players remember when they had to use a Compressor with IC2 cells to get Compressed Air... here, cell chemistry is no more.

quests.title: Basic Packer
quests.subtitle: Look who's packing
quests.desc:
The Packager compresses Tiny Dusts and Small Dusts into full Dusts, which can be compressed further into Ingots.

It can also work in reverse, change between sizes of Wires, and much more...

Unpacking Cables back into Wires and Rubber will be very useful once you rewire your Energy Network.

Yeah, we know... the 3x3 compacting recipes don't seem useful, because you can just use a Crafting Table. We urge you to consider the utility of this machine when used for automation purposes.

For an automated Dust setup, route Tiny Dusts into a buffer inventory (a chest will do) placed next to your Packager.

Next place a Robot Arm and configure it to "Import", and "Supply Exact 9". Finally, let the Machine auto-output on another side.

Lore: In GTCE, the reverse operation required a separate machine - the Unpackager.

quests.title: Basic Chemical Reactor
quests.subtitle: Chemistry for dummies
quests.desc:
The Chemical Reactor will be required for a lot of recipes.

Let's give it a spin - why don't we use your freshly made Chemical Reactor to unlock a new, better recipe for Rubber? Rubber is unusable as a Fluid, so you will need to make another machine to process it further.

You can't make Rubber using the Chemical Reactor alone, so here are some other machines you might want to aim for: 

A Solidifier is useful to solidify your Rubber directly into Rubber Sheets. You'll want these moving forward.

You could then make use of an Assembler to use your molten Rubber for Cables. This isn't important for Tin Cables, but better cables will need to be made in the Assembler.

Note: Submit a fluid by having it stored in a bucket in your inventory. Sorry - it's an FTB Quests quirk.

quests.title: Dead Cells
quests.subtitle: GTCE killed cell chemistry... GTCE killed Cells!
quests.desc:
Cells are an alternative form of Fluid storage. All Cell types with the same type and amount of Fluid inside them stack. They can be automatically filled with a Canning Machine.

Cells hold one bucket of Fluid.

Universal Cells hold up to one bucket, but also can store fractions of Buckets. They're particularly ideal if you want to store leftover Fluid without crafting a whole Drum. 

Cells made from higher tier materials can hold even more fluid, but Drums and Super Tanks will hold more by then.

Like Drums, Cells can be placed into a crafting grid to clear their contents.

quests.title: C-C-Combo!
quests.subtitle: Time to end the misery of hand crafting rotors!
quests.desc:
Use both of your new machines to turn a material into a Fluid, and solidify it back to a Rotor! This combination makes Rotors far easier to craft - four Ingots per Rotor, as well.

You can also do the same for Gears and Small Gears, which will then be superseded when you make a MV Extruder.

Note: Some metals, like Steel, require higher voltages than LV for Fluid Extraction, so make sure to keep an eye on the recipe tier in TMI.

quests.title: Soldering Alloy
quests.subtitle: I got soul but I'm not a solder
quests.desc:
Soldering Alloy is used solely as a Fluid, and in most cases is interchangeable in circuit recipes as a cheaper alternative to Tin.

The choice is yours. Save more materials? Make Soldering Alloy. Spend less time crafting materials? Tin is the way to go.

quests.title: Sapphire
quests.subtitle: One Step Closer To MV
quests.desc:
With LV power, the primary way of getting Aluminium Dust is by electrolyzing Sapphire or Green Sapphire. Aluminium is the main MV material, similar to how Steel is crucial in LV, and Bronze is important in the Steam Age.

It's possible to obtain Aluminium by processing ores such as Fullers' Earth, but the commitment required to see similar yields is more than sourcing your Aluminium from Sapphire. For this reason, you can skip the quest with the checkbox.

It would be wise to obtain enough Sapphire to electrolyze into a full stack of Aluminium Dust - it will be useful very soon. Plus, bonus Oxygen is always nice.

quests.title: Basic Electrolyzer
quests.subtitle: Separating anything you can think of
quests.desc:
Outside of Ore Processing:

Similar to the Centrifuge, this machine does a lot of things - we can't hope to list them all! Fortunately, it's one of the cheapest LV machines, so grab it as soon as it becomes necessary.

For Ore Processing:

Okay, we lied. It's not exactly used for Ore Processing, it's actually used in the decomposition of Dusts. However, most of the useful electrolysis recipes are locked at MV. You can peep at the molecular formula in a dust's tooltip to see what you can get from electrolysis.

Take caution! Due to how ores work, directly smelting dusts into Ingots can give higher metal yields than electrolysis. However, it means you lose the other products in the dust. You will have to decide whether you want to prioritise direct value, or byproduct count.

quests.title: Basic Cutter
quests.subtitle: The Cutter... cuts...
quests.desc:
If you haven't made a Mixer yet, you'll need it to craft this.

For now, you should use Water for its recipes instead of trying to acquire Lubricant.

The Cutting Machine additionally unlocks converting Rods into four Bolts.

This machine is the only way to obtain Gem Plates, which are needed to craft Filters, and by extension, the Gas Collector.

quests.title: Basic Centrifuge
quests.subtitle: Jack of Many Trades
quests.desc:
Outside of Ore Processing:

Oh boy, what can't this machine do? Decomposition recipes to separate dust types, separating air, do your taxes, and many others...

The list is too long! Let's just say you need the Centrifuge.

For Ore Processing:

This takes Impure Dust or Purified Dust and turns them into clean Dust, plus an added tiny Dust Byproduct. This is outside of the optimal chain for Byproducts, but this is also the cheapest Ore Processing machine of the presented array to run and to setup on a small scale.

Lore: In base GTCE, this was objectively the strongest Ore Processing machine as it was fairly easy to use, and gave three tiny dusts, just like the Ore Washer or the Thermal Centrifuge. We tuned it down in favor of diversity.

quests.title: Basic Mixer
quests.subtitle: The Mixer mixes...
quests.desc:
It's recommended that you put this machine off until you actually require it for for progression - explore elsewhere if you're fresh into LV.

Some alloys previously made by hand can also be made in the mixer, except at higher yield.

quests.title: Spray Can
quests.subtitle: Time for server tag wars
quests.desc:
Unfortunately, this is only an Empty Spray Can, which you have to fill with Chemical Dye before using.

Spray Cans do not provide any functionality involving machines, but can be quite visually appealing and useful!

Any (or almost any) GregTech tile entity (machine, pipe, cable...) can be painted to one of the 16 colors of your choosing. This is great for screenshots, or to separate different setups for convenience in a Multiplayer base. They also help with distinguishing between pipelines and cables.

Holding the Spray Can in the offhand will automatically paint any block you place.

quests.title: Lv Assembler
quests.subtitle: Assemblers, Avenge!
quests.desc:
While this is an extremely important machine for progression, this will not help you immediately as it is expensive. Most of the recipes you will want to pursue require fluids. Set up a little bit more infrastructure before rushing this.

With that out of the way, the Assembler is crucial to gameplay. Consider it as important as a Crafting Table, except... well, it's a machine, duh.

We as "modpack developers" also love to use this to include all sorts of custom recipes - and speaking of custom recipes, the Assembler now unlocks Applied Energistics 2!

Once you have enough infrastructure you will be able to make use of better recipes for:

- Vacuum Tubes, giving over double the previous yield

- Resistors, granting 4 resistors per craft!

- Circuit Boards, greatly reducing copper used

Wahoo!

Lore: Back in the days of GregTech 2, the Assembling Machine only had two slots, and was only really used for alternate Circuit and Hull recipes.

quests.title: Voiding Covers
quests.subtitle: The best mechanic - making an item or fluid disappear!
quests.desc:
Voiding Covers can be attached to GregTech Machines, Crates or Drums. They will void stuff you don't want. How fancy!

For machines, it will only void items in the Output slots. The Input slots are ignored.

You can attach a Filter for more precise voiding.

An advanced version also exists, which requires a MV Circuit. It enables voiding above a set threshold - great for for overflow control.

Note: They default to being switched off, to prevent accidental voiding. Right-click them with a Soft Mallet to activate them.

Obtaining any Voiding Cover will complete this Quest.

quests.title: Lv Extractor
quests.subtitle: The Steam Extractor extracts...
quests.desc:
Wait, we're not in the Steam Age anymore!

The reason we didn't present LV versions of Steam machines is because they are NOT required. You might not want to invest into an upgrade that does next to nothing. Except for this one specifically, because it's a big improvement over the Steam Age.

The Basic Extractor gains Fluid capabilities, which enables Fluid Extraction. Use it to melt Rubber that you previously solidified, to melt Soldering Alloy for your circuits, and to melt Red Alloy for the best Vacuum Tube recipe in the Assembler!

Lore: Did you notice this machine doubles as both an Extractor and a Fluid Extractor? The two of them were separate machines in previous versions of GregTech.

quests.title: Lv Circuit Assembler
quests.subtitle: Hopefully, your first MV Circuit expense
quests.desc:
From now on, all Circuits should be made in a Circuit Assembler. Use it now to enjoy far cheaper LV and MV circuits.

Each time you upgrade to a new tier of Circuit Assembler, you will be able to craft more advanced themes of circuit. These will use complex and sometimes unfamiliar ingredients, but will be more efficient to craft overall.

Lore: The Circuit Assembler has made appearances in and out of GregTech versions. GT5 did not have it, and it was reimplemented in GT5 Unofficial. GTCE decided not to port it, and the tug-of-war continues with its inclusion in GTCEu.

quests.title: Lv Arc Furnace
quests.subtitle: Here lies Plasma Furnace
quests.desc:
The Arc Furnace uses a lil' bit of Oxygen to smelt items in a different fashion than Furnaces.

You can use it to make easier Wrought Iron, double your Glass, and gain access to Annealed Copper.

It can also recycle various components and machines back to their original material.


Lore: Previous versions had both the Arc Furnace and the Plasma Arc Furnace. In that case, why not use one machine for both? Well, that's exactly what we thought - but we ultimately decided to remove one of the two plasma recipes. And then the other plasma recipe. 

In short, the Plasma Arc Furnace is gone. RIP.

quests.title: Breath of Fresh Oxygen
quests.subtitle: Ackshually, it's Dioxygen...
quests.desc:
You could waste precious years of your life in TMI looking for the most optimal Oxygen recipe, given the many ways to obtain it. Various minerals will produce it when electrolyzed.

If only the quest book had more information!

The best way to get Oxygen is by electrolyzing Silicon Dioxide. Silicon Dioxide is obtained from various sources, most notably Glass Dust. We would highly recommend setting up an automated, passive line of machines for Oxygen via this method! At first, the machines will consume tons of power, but once the machine buffers are filled up, the machines will rarely turn on. 

To figure out how to get renewable Glass for this purpose, check out the relevant quest in the Renewables \& You section.

Another method is centrifuging Air after making a Gas Collector, which you might not be able to make just yet. This recipe also gives Nitrogen, which is handy as it can speed up certain Electric Blast Furnace recipes.

Technically, you could source it by electrolyzing Water. In reality, electrolysis of water is more suitable for Hydrogen production due to the higher energy cost.

Try your best not to void Oxygen! This applies to most fluids and chemicals in GregTech, and is a good habit to pick up. The less Oxygen you void, the less energy and time you spend on generating more.

Note: Submit a fluid by having it stored in a bucket in your inventory. Sorry - it's an FTB Quests quirk.

quests.title: Glued
quests.subtitle: Extractor? I hardly knew 'er!
quests.desc:
You can now centrifuge Resin, which will still give Raw Rubber - but this time alongside Glue (and the less useful Plantball).

Give yourself a headstart and get on producing Glue. You can use it to make cheaper Resistors, as well as cheaper Circuit Boards in the Assembling Machine (and it is especially useful in the near future).

Note: Submit a fluid by having it stored in a bucket in your inventory. Sorry - it's an FTB Quests quirk.

quests.title: Gallium Arsenide
quests.subtitle: I hate byproducts, they're awesome
quests.desc:
Many materials going forward cannot be smelted directly from any Ores! They are instead obtained as byproducts from processing of other ores. Gallium and Arsenic are among these materials.

For Gallium, you may, ranged from worst to best (compromising difficulty and yields):

- Electrolyze Sphalerite for a low chance of dust. Note that you lose out on direct smelting value.

- Put Crushed Bauxite in the Chemical Bath. Note that this requires Sodium Persulfate.

- Obtain it as a Byproduct of Sphalerite Ore Processing in the Thermal Centrifuge or Centrifuge.

For Arsenic, you will have to mine and centrifuge Realgar.

quests.title: Phenolic Printed Circuit Board
quests.subtitle: A Good Board
quests.desc:
This Circuit Board is used to create MV Circuits!

Note: You have two options when making the Phenolic Printed Circuit Board. If you use Sodium Persulfate or Iron III Chloride, making boards takes only a quarter of the Silver as opposed to the crafting recipe.

However, given how painful it may be for you to obtain these chemicals right now, you can simply use more Silver by mining most of a Galena Vein...

The quest for Sodium Persulfate is in the MV chapter, if you're down for some light reading.

Lore: Back in GTCEu 1.12.2, these circuit boards were called "Good Circuit Boards". Not exactly a fitting name, as they're rather evil to craft.

quests.title: The Digital Age? Already!?
quests.subtitle: Time to ditch those chests
quests.desc:
Getting into Applied Energistics at this point is possible, but also a little bit involved...

You will need to make quite a few LV machines, and the LV-tier recipes for some AE2 items are comparatively expensive without access to more advanced technology.

In LV, you're able to craft:

- Energy Acceptor

- ME Terminal

- 1k ME Storage component

- ME Chest

Channels are disabled in this modpack, so feel free to put a few ME Chests with 1k Cells side-by-side to increase your storage capacity! You can use an ME Terminal to access all their contents at the same time.

Note: This pack includes Fluix Crystals, but they are generated as a GT material, rather than the default AE2 Fluix Crystals. To get them, put Fluix Dust in the Autoclave, which is made from Certus Quartz Dust, Nether Quartz Dust and Redstone in a Mixer. No need to charge the Certus Quartz. It gets... charged in the Mixer... or something like that.

Feel free to use AE2's in-game guide! Alternatively, you could consider:

The official Applied Energistics 2 website: https://appliedenergistics.org/guides

An instructionary video made by Pansmith, which covers Applied Energistics 2 on Minecraft 1.12.2. The fundamentals are still near-identical, however.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u0ouTWgdcXk

quests.title: Diode
quests.subtitle: Don't be swayed by TMI!
quests.desc:
Don't let TMI fool you... there's only one possible recipe right now for this Primitive Diode.

Diodes have a infamously nasty recipe, but they will become cheaper and more convenient once you can obtain Polyethylene.

This is required for MV Circuits.

quests.title: First MV Circuit!
quests.subtitle: Does that mean we're at MV yet?
quests.desc:
Well... gotten any Aluminium Ingots yet?

Each Electric Age has Circuits corresponding to its tier. The Good Electronic Circuit is the first MV Circuit you can make out of the three tiers of MV Circuitry.

Always be on the lookout for a cheaper way to make your circuits - new sets of recipes become available each time you make a new tier of Circuit Assembler. Conveniently, this tier of Circuit unlocks one now! Its recipes are far, far cheaper than hand-crafting Circuits.

Having this and Aluminium will grant access to MV Machines.

quests.title: Aluminium Dust
quests.subtitle: Aluminium, not "Aluminum"
quests.desc:
As mentioned in the previous quest, electrolyse the Sapphire Dust to get your Aluminium Dust.

Now all you need is an EBF! If you haven't started to work towards one, move to the right on this quest page and you'll end up there.

quests.title: Multiblock Techniques
quests.subtitle: By the end of this, you'll be an expert
quests.desc:
Multiblocks bear a large variety of interesting mechanics that might seem daunting, but don't worry - we've written a guide to help you learn.

This quest unlocks a bunch of tutorials on Multiblocks in the Tips and Tricks tab - check them out to learn more!

quests.title: Cobble is Life
quests.subtitle: What is this, Skyblock?
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

The Rock Breaker generates different types of igneous Rocks. In order to operate, Water and Lava (doesn't need to be source blocks) must both be adjacent to it. Those rocks might be pulverized and electrolyzed into certain materials.

Certain rocks, such as Red Granite, need, at minimum, EV power to generate.

Any Rock Breaker will complete this Quest.

Lore: Obsidian requiring Redstone to generate is in reference to an ancient Minecraft bug, MC-4239. Placing Redstone where the Cobblestone would go in a cobble generator would make it generate Obsidian instead. This bug was resolved in snapshot 14w25b for Minecraft 1.8, over 9 years ago at the time of writing!

quests.title: Turbo Charger
quests.subtitle: 100% Battery
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

The Turbo Charger charges RF and EU items such as Drills extremely quickly.
It accepts up to 4 Amps for every item it charges, making it twice as fast as the Battery Buffer. It cannot discharge, though.

Any Charger will complete this Quest.

quests.title: Fishing 50
quests.subtitle: Women want me, fish fear me
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

The Fisher catches fish from Water. It requires a 5x5 area of Water directly below to operate. It uses the same loot table as normal Fishing, so it can catch things like Enchanted Books. Each operation consumes 1 String and EU equivalent to its tier (32 for LV, etc.) That's about it.

Fish Oil can be extracted from Fish, which can be used for Bio Diesel.

Get any tier Fisher to complete this quest. (They only go up to EV. If you foresee needing enough Fish to justify an IV or higher Fisher, make a feature request on our GitHub or Discord.)

quests.title: At The Speed Of Light
quests.subtitle: I Have Become Chronos
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

The World Accelerator... well, accelerates the world. Placing it down and powering it will cause it to tick-accelerate any Tile Entities around it. Using a Screwdriver, you can also toggle it to tick-accelerate random blocks in its area, such as farmland. 

Keep in mind that GregTech machines are blacklisted from tick acceleration - this is for the better, as tick-accelerating a GregTech machine can lead to it consuming many Amps at once and nuking your power system.

quests.title: Building Resources
quests.subtitle: If you build it, they will come
quests.desc:
If you're a builder, weep no more - this custom multiblock, freshly made with the powers of KubeJS, is made for you.

By providing the multiblock with a near-negligible amount of Construction Foam, you'll gain access to increased yields of many different building blocks to spice up your base.

Don't go too crazy!

(Crazy? I was crazy once!)

quests.title: Oh Yeah, we Drillin'
quests.subtitle: We Drill Now? We Drill Now.
quests.desc:
Drills are electric tools which come in different tiers. The LV Drill is quite good: mining a 3x3x1 area. Higher-tier drills can mine 3x3x3, 5x5x5, and up to 9x9x9 to make huge holes!

This suggested drill is with a Diamond tip, which might be the best material for it right now.

Note 1: All Electric Tools have a lot more durability than indicated, as they use Energy to prevent damage from being applied! On top of that, they can be enchanted as Pickaxes can.

Note 2: Drills can now be configured to different ranges, not just the maximum or a single block.

Note 3: You can now craft electric versions of other tools too! Hooray for less Wire Cutter crafting!

Lore: Up until GTCEu, Electric tools required Stainless Steel, which locked players out of experiencing their glory until HV!

quests.title: Break the Targets!
quests.subtitle: I've had it with these blocks!
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

The Block Breaker... well, we'll leave it to you to figure out what this one does. There are likely some nifty farms you can create with clever usage of this machine - especially with the many vanilla mechanics introduced between 1.12.2 and 1.20.1!

Higher tiers of Block Breaker come with an additional speed bonus.

Any Block Breaker will complete this Quest.

quests.title: Gotta Catch 'Em All
quests.subtitle: Oops, dropped my Pickaxe.
quests.desc:
This quest's subject is optional, but may still be of interest to you. We've put it here in the name of completeness.

Item Collectors will pick up items - similar to any kind of Vacuum Chest you may be familiar with from other mods.
