Higher tiers of Collector will increase the collection radius over multiple chunks. This can be useful if your farm relies on in-world drops, such as Sugar Cane, or... uh, bizarrely, chicken eggs.

Any Item Collector will complete this Quest.

quests.title: I'm Fetching Me Mallet!
quests.subtitle: Shoutout to Yung Onion
quests.desc:
A feature of the Soft Mallet that you may be aware of is its ability to pause machines. However, did you know that if you sneak whilst pausing a machine, it'll tell the machine to continue operating until it finishes its recipe?

This is perfect if you need to make changes to a multiblock but don't want to void items, as unforming a multiblock will void all inputs.

quests.title: Nitrogen
quests.subtitle: Free nitro?
quests.desc: Nitrogen is commonly obtained from centrifuging air. It's very useful, speeding up recipes in many multiblocks like the Electric Blast Furnace and the Pyrolyse Oven. We would highly recommend setting up a dedicated passive line to generate Nitrogen!

quests.title: Gotta go fast
quests.subtitle: Subsonic!
quests.desc:
Concrete and Studs are two decorative block families added that increse your speed when walking on them, with Concrete increasing your speed more.

Get either of them to complete this quest.

quests.title: Pyrolyse Oven
quests.subtitle: Thermal Decomposition
quests.desc:
The Pyrolyse Oven is an electric equivalent to the Coke Oven. It can produce Coal Coke and Charcoal with a Creosote byproduct just as before, but also has additional Organic Byproducts, the most beneficial of these being Wood Tar.

Another product is Biomass, which is more pertinent for your progression as of this moment.

The byproducts can be Distilled for an array of organic resources, with different byproducts giving different ratios of different chemicals.

You will need to take a peek at the Distillation Quest to get to grips with the power of distillation...


Note: With 40 EU/t recipes, it needs either 1 MV energy hatch or 2 LV energy hatches. Multiblocks will not use 2A of one tier to reach the next one for recipe voltage minimums. 

In other words, the controller needs to say at least "MV" in the GUI to run recipes which say "MV" in TMI, even if they can draw enough EU/t with a lower tier hatch configuration. On the bright side, if you're using two LV hatches, you can choose to only power one, leaving the other disconnected - as long as you supply enough EU.


The Pyrolyse Oven remains optional, should you choose to use Breweries instead. However, it is very rewarding to construct one either for Power, or for the various Byproducts, and we highly recommend you give it a look.

There are recipes that can accept Nitrogen to double the speed. This isn't quite worth pursuing right now, but Nitrogen will eventually be free and infinite.

Lore: In GT5u, the structure for the Pyrolyse Oven was infamously a giant box with only 9 coils inside. Players usually spent several minutes debugging what is wrong due to a lack of a TMI preview.

quests.title: Biomass
quests.subtitle: Plants aren't industrial enough!
quests.desc:
Welcome to the natural Ethylene route! This method uses plant matter to produce Ethanol.

Before we get ahead of ourselves, Sugar Cane works fine in a Brewery for a decent yield of Biomass.

Otherwise, you could try the "Plantball into Bio Chaff" route, and then turn it into Biomass, either in the Brewery or the Pyrolyse Oven - the latter being more efficient.

Note: Only one Bio Chaff can be obtained from maceration until you make the HV Macerator.

Lore: You may recognise Plantballs from IndustrialCraft2! In IC2 Experimental, there was this rather... obscure way to turn Biomass into Biogas for power, which sadly required too much investment to be really worth it.

quests.title: Local Brewery selling Booze
quests.subtitle: *hic*... *hic*
quests.desc:
The Brewery can be used to make Lubricant from Redstone and Creosote/Oil. Lubricant has some niche uses, namely being used in the Cutter to significantly reduce the duration of its recipes.

The Brewery is a very, very slow machine, but it requires almost no power to run its recipes. Building many Breweries will help you obtain enough Biomass on your quest for plastic. Oh, and you can use it to brew Potions too!

Note: The Brewery "unlocks" the Biomass path towards Ethylene. The same can be done in the Pyrolyse Oven. The other path involves Oil.

Lore: You could make your own Brewery if you want to build up a company and start selling Booze... in GregTech 6.

quests.title: Ethylene
quests.subtitle: One of many hydrocarbons
quests.desc:
Our warmest congratulations on making it here.

The way you should obtain Ethylene was described in the previous Quest, depending on the route you chose.

Ethylene will shortly be used in polymer production. It's also useful in a few other reactions.

quests.title: Ethanol
quests.subtitle: Yo, wait - that's alcohol!
quests.desc:
Put your previously made Biomass into a MV Distillery to make Ethanol.

You will need this for Ethylene by reacting it with Sulfuric Acid in a MV Chemical Reactor... unless you chose to go down the Oil path.

Once you have a hefty starting stock of Ethanol, consider making and distilling Fermented Biomass in a Distillation Tower (when you get there!) for extra chemicals.

Do not try to use Ethanol as a Power source... it's quite possibly its worst use. If it's organic Power that you want, we suggest taking a look at Benzene instead!

quests.title: Naphtha
quests.subtitle: We're still confused on how this is pronounced
quests.desc:
Distillating Oil will give you Fuel that you will need to desulfurize.

Hydrogen Sulfide is perfectly recycled in an Electrolyzer.

To automate this process, simply place your Chemical Reactor and your Electrolyzer next to each other. Be sure to use your Screwdriver to enable input from the output side.

Naphtha is a good Product source. Keep following the quests to the right for more details.

quests.title: Fuel Cracking
quests.subtitle: Fuel and oil cracking can get complicated...
quests.desc:
Don't panic! We'll mostly be doing this to get hydrocarbons.

There are many ways to acquire Ethylene from Oil processing. It's easy to get lost in all the options and recipes!

The best way is to steam-crack Naphtha in a Chemical Reactor. Be sure to make the Severely Steam-Cracked version for a higher yield of Ethylene.

Once in HV, you'll have the option to do this recipe in the Cracking Unit at 100% efficiency. Unfortunately, the recipes you are doing with the Chemical Reactor have a loss of 50%.

Note: All of this looks and feels terribly inefficient - we hope that's strong motivation to tier up! The Distillation Tower will be a huge upgrade for petrochem, but you're not quite there... yet. Right now, you'll have to use a Distillery to get Ethylene.

quests.title: Electricity Generation in MV
quests.subtitle: Things are 'boutta get rough
quests.desc:
Higher-tier Generators will increase their fuel consumption proportionally to their Generation rate, keeping the duration of fuels and the specific amount of energy from fuels the same. The Advanced Steam Turbine will use 5,120 mB Steam per second, equivalent to 256 mB/t.

Get any of the generators to complete this quest, but you may prefer starting off with the Advanced Steam Turbine.

There are a few new power options available for MV. Selecting the Generators and pressing U will let you see which fuels count as Combustion or Gas.

Quests in the top left corner of this Chapter are for the valuable Power options.

If you stick to Steam for your Power generation, you'll probably need a Large Boiler.

quests.title: Stainless Steel
quests.subtitle: HV is in your grasp
quests.desc:
Any Steel you encounter in day-to-day life is most likely Stainless Steel. It is extremely resilient against corrosion.

Stainless Steel is the material for HV components, like Aluminium for MV and Steel for LV. It is also used in high quantities for the Distillation Tower.

It requires Chromium, obtained mainly from Ruby, and Manganese, obtained from Pyrolusite and others in its vein.

Smelting Stainless Steel requires HV Power, which you likely do not have at this point. Scaling Power Production is crucial.

If you haven't already, it would be a good idea to get a second EBF going. They can share blocks, so that means your second EBF only requires 10 Cupronickel Coils instead of 16.

Have one EBF run off MV for Aluminium and any other recipes requiring MV Power, and one running off HV with 2 MV Energy Hatches.

quests.title: Transformers
quests.subtitle: LV In Disguise
quests.desc:
Transformers allow you to step up and down safely between Voltage tiers. They convert the power they receive into an equivalent amount of power 1 voltage tier above or below the input voltage.

Apart from running machines on different voltage tiers, Transformers come in handy for transporting power across longer distances, as cable loss has less of an effect at higher voltages. Just like real life!

Transformers have 5 low voltage sides, and 1 high voltage side.

The normal Transformer can only do 1A <-> 4A conversion, but Adjustable Transformers can be changed between 1A <-> 4A, 2A <-> 8A, 4A <-> 16A, and 16A <-> 64A conversions. 

Use a Soft Hammer to change between transforming up and down, and a Screwdriver on Adjustable Transformers to change the amperage.

Higher tier Transformers will require Coils.

quests.title: Advanced Mixer
quests.subtitle: Mixing more complex alloys
quests.desc:
If you're wanting to dive into MV, start here.

The dusts created in an MV Mixer have great utility.

quests.title: MV Components
quests.subtitle: Hated crafting these in the previous tier?
quests.desc:
Here's a list of the components you will need in MV. To alleviate the pain, be sure to autocraft or bulk-craft these parts, especially Motors.

Familiar with the emitter? This tier's emitter requires a Flawless Emerald in its recipe, which'll be only attainable with the Sifting Machine for now.

Conveyors, Pumps and Robot Arms are objective upgrades to LV versions if you use them as covers.

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on Overclocking in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Advanced Electrolyser
quests.subtitle: We made it to MV, and, well...
quests.desc: The Advanced Electrolyzer allows you to decompose far more Dusts. This will make getting materials such as Aluminium a lot easier!

quests.title: A Balanced Quest
quests.subtitle: Clay is an extremely balanced useful material for electrolysis
quests.desc:
All four output Dusts obtained from Clay electrolysis are immensely useful.

We'll ask you to give us a total of 416 Clay Dust for this quest. You may also want to look into other sources of similar materials, such as Sodalite. Check emi for some ideas.

You should use the Sodium and/or Lithium to make some MV Batteries.

Grr, all this useless Water you're getting keeps clogging the machine! Maybe you should deal with it by using a Voiding Cover.

quests.title: Digital Miner
quests.desc:
This kind soul will mine ores for you when placed above a vein.

Vertical range is unlimited below the miner. Horizontal range is displayed in the tooltip.

The MV variant may not have the range to mine a full vein, but the HV variant does, so you may wish hold off for now. Get either to complete this Quest.

You will need Power. Charged Batteries may work, but they tend to run out of power, which is a bit inconvenient.

Our top suggestion is to use a filled Drum of some kind of fuel (if you're using Steam, no dice!) and a Generator. 

Place the Drum above your Generator and right-click it with a Screwdriver to auto-output into the Generator.

Note: Do not forget to chunkload all 9 chunks of the ore vein with FTB Utilities (open the map in the top left corner, claim the chunks with left-click, and enable force loading with shift-left-click).

Tip: You can either use an ME Chest or a 2x2 Drawer as the output inventory to make transporting the ores back home a simple task. The ME Chest can be powered with EU directly.

quests.title: Steel Alloys
quests.subtitle: Steel Your Heart
quests.desc:
Vanadiumsteel is required in small quantities in progression. It's nearly four times as durable as Steel if used in GregTech tools. The Vanadium comes from Vanadium Magnetite, which you may have encountered in Magnetite veins.

Other Steel alloys include Blue Steel, Red Steel, and Black Steel!

Take some Blue Steel free of charge - it's twice as durable as Steel.

quests.title: Autocrafting
quests.subtitle: Autocrafting at last!
quests.desc:
Molecular Assemblers can be used to automate Crafting Table recipes, which means you can automate just about anything now.

To maximise the number of recipes a single Molecular Assembler can do, make a 3D matrix of Molecular Assemblers and ME Pattern Providers - where each component is surrounded on 6 sides by the other.

Using higher-tier materials in crafting will yield more Molecular Assemblers.

Warning: Try to avoid using GT tools in AE2 autocrafting! It causes AE2 to freak out... you may or may not destroy the TPS, or even worse, end up having thousands of tools being crafted as soon as one of them breaks.

If you're stubborn enough to really want to use GT tools in AE2 autocrafting, prioritise encoding recipes that use tools with high durability, but do not setup autocrafting for the tools themselves. Instead, use the alternate Assembling Machine recipes.

quests.title: Pressing Matters
quests.subtitle: Who likes meteor hunting, anyway?
quests.desc:
Making AE2 Presses will unlock all the Processors from AE2. This opens up ME Drives, ME Pattern Providers, ME Crafting CPUs... that's almost the rest of AE2 at your fingertips! (Or at least... not until you obtain Silicon Ingots.)

AE2 Printed Circuits are now made in the Forming Press.

quests.title: Advanced Cutter
quests.subtitle: Upgrades, people, upgrades!
quests.desc:
The MV Cutter is required to cut Silicon Boules to prepare them for Laser Engraving.

We recommend you supply this machine with a steady stream of Lubricant to cut down on the processing time.

Lubricant can be brewed from Oil and Talc, Soapstone or Redstone.

quests.title: Laser Engraving
quests.desc:
The Laser Engraver engraves patterns into Silicon Wafers based on the type of Lens it is given. After the wafers are engraved, they must be passed through a Cutting Machine to turn them into components ready to be put into Circuits.

Higher tier Engravers can etch more complex wafers for more efficient Circuits.

quests.title: Upgrade your EBF!
quests.subtitle: LV is so old-school!
quests.desc:
A LV Battery Buffer and two LV Energy Hatches likely won't cut it anymore. Let's remedy that!

Upgrade your Electric Blast Furnace with a MV Energy Hatch. Now, you can run it with MV Generators without having to mess around with Transformers.

quests.title: Advanced Assembler
quests.subtitle: Assengers, Avemble
quests.desc:
The Assembler is a staple for every Tier; an important goal to unlock more recipes and progress further into the Age. Many in the community see crafting a voltage tier's Assembler as a sign of properly reaching said voltage.

That being said, welcome to MV!

The MV Assembler unlocks Transistors, Capacitors and Inductors, used for the next tiers of Circuits.

quests.title: Greenhouse
quests.subtitle: The power of KubeJS
quests.desc:
GregTech doesn't provide ways to farm plants or trees, so we made a custom multiblock for the modpack!

The multiblock Greenhouse will be your source of Wood and other plants if you wish.

Its Wood production should be enough to run 2 Pyrolyse Ovens at their lowest Power.

You can also use this for automated Resin. The yields are kept low to incentivize other rubber sources later in the game.

Note: With 40 EU/t recipes, it needs either 1 MV energy hatch or 2 LV energy hatches. Multiblocks will not use 2A of one tier to reach the next one for recipe voltage minimums. 

In other words, the controller needs to say at least "MV" in the GUI to run recipes which say "MV" in emi, even if they can draw enough EU/t with a lower tier hatch configuration.

Note: The structure requires a lot of Wrought Iron. Make sure you have an Arc Furnace for that.

Lore: We originally implemented a Chemical Reactor recipe for wood in the modpack to solve the tree problem. Our implementation turned out to be completely busted, so we decided that a rebalance was due. Hopefully, the Greenhouse is a happy middle ground.

quests.title: Kanthal Coils
quests.subtitle: Is it getting hot in here?
quests.desc:
Kanthal Heating Coils are the next major step in progression. They increase the temperature of your Blast Furnace to 2,700K, allowing you to process new materials. They may also accelerate less hot recipes - check the quest to the top-left for details.

You will need a staggering 128 Ingots for a full set of Coils, which will easily take over an hour. We advise that you move on to other projects while your EBF is hard at work - that or build another one!

An MV Chemical Bath is required to cool Hot Ingots down. They're hot when held, as the name implies. Maybe you should save your hands and automate this...

With the construction of a Kanthal EBF, you're now able to make Silicon Ingots, opening up the vast majority of AE2!

quests.title: 250IQ
quests.subtitle: Stop burning your darn hands!
quests.desc:
This is a guide on how to automatically route all your Hot Ingots to your Chemical Bath, while keeping everything automated in tip-top shape.

The cheap way would be to use filters, and manually add Hot Ingots one by one... which is so boring! Let's try the fun option.

Install an Item Tag Filter Cover on the side of your Chemical Bath, open it and set it to whitelist "hot_ingots".

From here, you can route with any logistics system of your choice.

Connect the output of your EBF(s) to the input of your Chemical Bath and the output dump (Chest or Crate).
You can also have a Restrictive Pipe in front of the output dump to ensure the Chemical Bath will be prioritized.

Note: If you wish to know more tags for more automation setups, they will display if you press Shift when hovering over an item. For instance, an item tagged with #forge:plates/copper would be filtered as plates/copper.

Other Note: The Item Tag Filter partially supports regex. Hover over the info icon for more information.

quests.title: Moore's Law
quests.desc: A circuit component that you can now produce thanks to the MV Assembler and your shiny new Kanthal EBF.

quests.title: First HV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: ...but hold on before getting too giddy
quests.desc:
Alright... technically, obtaining HV Circuits and Stainless Steel lets you progress to HV - but let's not rush things.

The first thing you should make with these is a MV Circuit Assembler to unlock the new tier of Circuits right away; cheaper recipes is the real payoff here.

quests.title: Download more RAM
quests.subtitle: Random Access Memories
quests.desc: Random Access Memory is required for more advanced Electronic Components.

quests.title: More LV Circuits
quests.subtitle: You have unlocked a new tier of Circuits!
quests.desc:
The Integrated Logic Circuit is an alternate LV Circuit, which is easier to mass-produce, and can be upgraded to MV (and then HV).

Note: This is not a strict upgrade to the cost of Circuits - you would need to get to the next Tier for that, just slightly further down in MV. It is still an important upgrade, and is required to get to HV circuits.

Lore: The circuit progression in GTCEu is mostly based on GT5u's progression. It has been rebalanced to not be as painful. Specifically this circuit tier, which was a trap!

quests.title: More MV Circuits
quests.subtitle: More... I demand more!
quests.desc: The MV Circuit for the Integrated Tier.

quests.title: Silicon Lottery
quests.subtitle: These wafers are fully fungible
quests.desc:
Silicon Boules are made in the EBF with half a stack of Silicon Dust and a pinch of Gallium Arsenide. Their primary purpose is engraving Circuit Wafers.

After making the Boule, process it in the Cutter to get 16 Silicon Wafers ready for engraving.

The recommended Silicon source at this point would be... hm, either Glass Dust or Sodalite.

Warning: Each Silicon Boule takes 7.5 minutes to make at 120 EU/t, or 1,080,000 EU! If the EBF loses power midway, the recipe will start to lose progress.

With that being said, Silicon Boules take an absolute age to craft, so consider parallelizing the recipe in multiple EBFs and stockpiling them.

quests.title: Advanced Circuit Assembler
quests.subtitle: Yippee, yahoo
quests.desc:
As is common for every tier, the Circuit Assembler is an essential part of progression, always opening up a new Tier of circuits.

At this point you should be placing an Extractor next to your Circuit Assembler. Send off dozens of stacks of Soldering Alloy Ingots, and you won't have to worry about refilling again.

quests.title: Ultimate Smelter
quests.subtitle: Possibly the most powerful furnace you've seen
quests.desc:
At its lowest power (Cupronickel coils and LV Voltage), the Multi Smelter smelts 32 items in just 12 seconds - equivalent to 26.7 Furnaces, or 3.3 Steam Ovens!

If that wasn't already enough to convince you, standard overclocks can be applied with sufficient voltage.

And even better - higher tier Coils increase the amount of items it can smelt in parallel!

quests.title: Best LV Circuits
quests.subtitle: It Feels Good to be the Best
quests.desc:
The best LV Circuits! You can start to churn these guys out, as you'll be making them for the rest of the game.

Try to keep a stockpile of these crafted at all times - making these on-demand can take a while.

Reaching EV will unlock methods to make them even cheaper.

quests.title: Best MV Circuits
quests.subtitle: MV has peaked
quests.desc:
The best MV Circuits! You will be making these for the remainder of the game.

Eventually (in IV), they will become even cheaper.

quests.title: First EV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Standing out amongst the rest
quests.desc:
Your first EV Circuit gets a unique texture. If you still consider yourself to be in MV, hold off on making too many of these, as they won't benefit you right now. We can't stop you from making these if you just wanna flex on us, though.

It'll be needed in HV.

quests.title: More HV Circuits
quests.subtitle: On that HV grind
quests.desc:
Better HV Circuits - much cheaper and easier to make than before.

Before you move on to HV, make sure you have scaled up your Power Production, and automated the most important processes with AE2!

quests.title: Capacitors
quests.subtitle: No cap, bussin frfr
quests.desc:
A circuit component that you can now produce thanks to the MV Assembler!

Capacitors are commonly used in computers to store information during power failures, amongst many other uses.

quests.title: Blue Alloy
quests.subtitle: You're blue now... that's my alloy
quests.desc:
Blue Alloy is an excellent Cable material for HV power.

Nothing is preventing you from using it for MV power too! Make sure that you don't overvolt your machines, though.

Superconductors for both MV and HV are unlocked... later.

Electrotine is obtained from the Nether, so don't craft it in the Mixer!

Furthermore, Electrotine can be separated for Electrum and Redstone.

quests.title: Inductors
quests.subtitle: Z = jwL
quests.desc:
A Circuit Component that you can now make thanks to the MV Assembler.

If you want the cheapest recipe, Nickel-Zinc Ferrite is the way to go.

quests.title: Polyethylene
quests.subtitle: Baby's first polymer!
quests.desc:
If you want to be less fancy, just call it plastic.

For the polymerization process, add Oxygen, which gives better returns than Air, then solidify like you would with Rubber.

Polyethylene (PE) is the key to unlocking many new things. It's commonly used as Sheets, or in Fluid form.

In addition, some recipes are now more efficient! Check out diodes and machine hulls (both LV and MV).


Lore: The OreDict and fluid name for Polyethylene in 1.12.2 GTCEu was plastic, for cross-mod compatibility.

quests.title: Patterns
quests.subtitle: Autocrafting at last!
quests.desc:
Patterns are used for AE2 autocrafting. Using Polyvinyl Chloride increases the yield by 4 times, and higher-tier plastics have even greater yields.

Don't get too excited, though - to really get into the automation side of AE2, you'll need to get some Silicon Ingots first.

A sub-note for autocrafting... whilst it's possible to perform chemical chains on-demand with AE2, doing so can build some bad habits that'll come back around to bite you in higher tiers. We urge you to passive things wherever you can.

quests.title: Super Tank
quests.subtitle: Super Tanks are OP!
quests.desc:
No, but seriously.

This is the ultimate portable Fluid storage solution. 4,000 buckets of storage - in one single block!

Like Drums, Super Tanks keep their fluids when broken, and can be cleared in the crafting grid.

If you hunger for even more storage, take a look at higher tier Super Tanks or AE2 Fluid Storage Cells.

quests.title: Polyvinyl Chloride
quests.subtitle: You've already made one polymer, what's two?
quests.desc:
Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) is the 3rd-most produced plastic in real life. Here in CEu, it can be substituted for Polyethylene in the production of Plastic Circuit Boards and other items to increase the yield. It is required from EV onwards to wrap Cables.

PVC item pipes also have the highest throughput available for a while, but most of your setups right now won't need such speed. Still, it's good to keep this knowledge in your pocket.

Pro tip: The fluid form of PVC is never used in base GTCEu, so feel free to solidify all of it into sheets.

quests.title: Plastic Boards
quests.subtitle: It's a Casio on a Plastic Board
quests.desc:
At this point, you have all the tools available to start making Plastic Boards. Higher-tier polymers in future will increase your PCB yields substantially.

The recipe chains are starting to get pretty long, so we recommend processing large batches in two dedicated Chemical Reactors.

quests.title: Sodium Persulfate / Iron III Chloride
quests.subtitle: Let's etch some Copper off Circuit Boards!
quests.desc:
Sodium Persulfate can be used in Ore Processing in the Chemical Bath to get various bonus outputs you would not normally obtain. It's completely optional, but pretty rewarding.

Wait, stop! That's only a distraction - the real topic of this Quest is...

Sodium Persulfate and Iron III Chloride are used to make cheaper Good Circuit Boards, as their inclusion cuts Silver usage by a factor of four.

They're also required to make Plastic Circuit Boards.

Making Sodium Persulfate will yield Hydrochloric Acid as a coproduct, which you may re-use to make Iron III Chloride. This should the best approach right now, but the decision is entirely yours.

With more automation later in the game, Iron III Chloride will be the cheaper and more straight forward path.

This Quest requires both Fluids to complete. You'll need both anyway...

quests.title: Sulfuric Acid
quests.subtitle: "...what he thought was H2O was H2SO4!"
quests.desc:
TMI might look a little overwhelming here, so we'll go through the best path for Sulfuric Acid here.

Making Sulfuric Acid is a three step process. It is used in many important reactions, so you should automate its production ASAP.

To automate it, you will need three LV Chemical Reactors. We would recommend building a dedicated setup somewhere with a single LV Generator to power all three machines. You can then spaghetti your pipes to handle input and output ...or simply use AE2.

- Step 1: React Sulfur with Oxygen to form Sulfur Dioxide.
- Step 2: React Sulfur Dioxide with more Oxygen to form Sulfur Trioxide.
- Step 3: React Sulfur Trioxide with Water to form Sulfuric Acid.

Lore: GregTech 5's Chemical Reactor only had one Fluid slot. To circumvent that, they would do cell chemistry. The way you would automate Sulfuric Acid was by having one cell travel from machine to machine and then loop back to its starting position. A somewhat fun and interesting automation challenge, but also a chore!

quests.title: Hydrochloric Acid
quests.subtitle: You'll need a lot of this!
quests.desc:
Obtain Hydrochloric Acid by mixing Hydrogen and Chlorine.

This acid is also obtained as waste from a lot of reactions involving Chlorine. Depending on your setups, you may never need to produce it directly.

Note: We have a $medicine command on our Discord:

"Do not attempt this in real life - it is not only illegal, but also stupidly dangerous".

This applies to toying with chemicals. Please don't do it.

Side note - we can guarantee you have a sample of this with you right now! It's in your stomach as a part of gastric acid.

quests.title: It will Melt Your Flesh
quests.subtitle: ...if you don't read this quest.
quests.desc:
You may notice that some fluids say they are "Acidic", with Sulfuric Acid being one of them.

This means that many fluid pipes will not be able to transport them safely!

At MV, the cheapest pipes for acid transportation are probably Gold, with the more expensive Vanadium Steel or Chrome pipes taking second and third place.

Stainless Steel or Polytetrafluoroethylene (try saying that fast) will be valid options once you are in HV.

You have three options for acid storage at around this point: Glass Vials, Gold Drums, or Super Tanks. Glass Vials are like Fluid Cells, and are made to store acids. Gold Drums are self-explanatory. Super Tanks will be unlocked after Polyethylene, and can store any fluid without restriction.

WARNING: Acids in the wrong pipes will cause voiding, pipe burning, and high chance of death! Be careful!

Lore: The acid transportation and storage mechanics originate from GregTech 6.

quests.title: Better Batteries
quests.subtitle: Baby Got Battery
quests.desc:
MV Batteries will appeal to you if you want to buffer Energy, just like LV.

The cost in Sodium, Lithium or Cadmium jumps up to 8 dusts, so you should use whatever you have the most of. The difference in capacity isn't THAT huge, after all!

Get either Battery to complete the Quest.

quests.title: Advanced Extruder
quests.subtitle: The biggest upgrade you'll make
quests.desc:
We SINCERELY hope at no point you made the LV Extruder, because only the MV Extruder is actually useful. If you did, immediately recycle it through the Arc Furnace.

The Extruder shapes solid materials into (almost!) any form using Ingots. This is a great way to reduce the amount of microcrafting, as well as keeping a high conversion ratio.

The Extruder can make Rods, Bolts, Small Gears, Gears, and Rings at the highest efficiency in a single step!

You may also like to use it for Pipes, Long Rods, and Rotors.

quests.title: Large Cells
quests.subtitle: Like cells... only larger!
quests.desc:
Aluminium Cells are a big upgrade over the Steel Cells or Universal Fluid Cells.

They can hold any fraction of Fluid - up to 32 buckets!

quests.title: Fluid Drilling Rigs
quests.subtitle: Fluid Drilling Rigs in CEu!
quests.desc:
It's quite the investment to own a Fluid Drilling Rig, but it is by far the most prominent source of Oil.

It pumps fluids from underneath Bedrock itself. Note that the fluids aren't actually there, instead being simulated.

Fluid veins are stored within chunk-sized regions. Each vein has a different fluid, but they are mostly types of Oil.

You can't carry out prospecting for fluid veins yet - this will come at HV.

Each vein generates with a different base yield. This is typically between 150L to 300L per second.

When drained, fluid veins will slowly deplete. This will cause the fluid yield to decrease over time until it reaches its depletion yield. At that point, you should move the Rig to a different vein.

A Basic Fluid Drilling Rig will last for 100,000 operations (with 1 second per operation) until depletion. This is enough for well over 10,000 buckets of Oil. Higher tier Rigs will massively increase the yield, while also decreasing the depletion rate.

What you can find in the Overworld:

Oil: richer in Light Fuel, the most appreciated variant.
Light Oil: richer in Refinery Gas, usable for power.
Heavy Oil: richer in Heavy Fuel, which has uses that are more niche.
Raw Oil: richer in Naphtha, the best source of the early-game oil products.
Natural Gas: grants Refinery Gas with no distillation required.
Salt Water: grants Sodium and Chlorine, useful later.

What you can find in the Nether:

Natural Gas: grants Refinery Gas but with higher yield than the Overworld vein.
Lava: hopefully, you know what this is.

When starting out, you may have to tap randomly until you find something you like. Remember that whatever you pump could also be used to refuel your Fluid Rig.

quests.title: US Simulator
quests.subtitle: Ever mined into an Oil deposit?
quests.desc:
Get any kind of Pump, place it above an Oil spout and... give it some power.

Do not pump above an Ocean, as the pump will attempt to pull water, resulting in getting the pump stuck. Sorry!

Each Oil deposit can have between 3,000 and 9,200 buckets of Oil, which is a LOT! Refining the Oil onsite into either Light Fuel or Diesel (or both) is perfectly viable. The next few Quests will explain each type's pros and cons.

Store any obtained Oil in Drums, but you would do yourself a favour to use a Super Tank as soon as you can make them.

Warning: Do not forget to chunkload with FTB Utilities (open the map in the top left corner, claim the chunks with left-click, and enable force loading with shift-left-click).

Note: The Oil path is one path towards Ethylene. The other paths involve getting natural with some Ethanol.

For now, Oil is optional. It becomes mandatory much later when you get to EV.

Oil can also be refined in the form of Light Fuel, Diesel or even Gasoline, which are excellent power options.

If you chose this path, you should use it for both Power and Ethylene.

Oil spouts are a great starting point - they will tide you over until you unlock Fluid Drilling Rigs.

Lore: Oil geysers were originally from Buildcraft. If you played a GregTech 5 modpack without Buildcraft, you needed to use the Fluid Rig and tap randomly to try and get lucky, or wait until HV, where prospecting for fluids was available.

quests.title: America Simulator
quests.subtitle: This quest was sponsored by the US Military
quests.desc:
Underground, you may come across pure Oilsands ore veins. You can centrifuge the Dust to get Oil.

Note: The Oil path is one path towards Ethylene. The other paths involve getting natural with some Ethanol.

For now, Oil is optional. It becomes mandatory much later when you get to EV.

Oil can also be refined in the form of Light Fuel, Diesel or even Gasoline, which are excellent power options.

If you chose this path, you should use it for both Power and Ethylene.

Oilsands are a great starting point - they'll tide you over until you unlock Fluid Drilling Rigs.

quests.title: Distillery

tasks.label: Either an LV or MV Distillery

quests.subtitle: The Illusion of Free Choice
quests.desc:
Before we start, here's some important information if you're aiming to make Ethylene:

Taking the Oil route will require at least a LV Distillery.

With the Biomass route, you will need a MV Distillery.

Get either to complete this quest.

Got all that? Alright! Let's discuss a complex topic: Distillation. We'll try to help you understand how and why the TMI recipes are the way they are, so stick with us.

Nearly all the Distillery recipes are duplicates from the Distillation Tower, except that they only have one Fluid output, and void everything else.

In other words, you could argue the Distillery is an inferior Distillation Tower.

This doesn't mean that the Distillery is the worse of the two. It is unlocked earlier, and it is smaller, cheaper, and more energy efficient per recipe.

Hell, in many cases, you won't even mind losing the other Fluids.

Distilleries are an essential component for power generation, whether you pick Diesel or Benzene.

You'll probably want many of them at as low-tier as possible to avoid the energy losses from Overclocking.

quests.title: The Church of Natural Farts
quests.subtitle: Whoever smelt it...
quests.desc:
Refinery Gas, LPG and Methane are Gas Fuels. LPG (Liquid Petroleum Gas) is a mix of hydrocarbons typically used in heating appliances.

Refinery Gas is obtained either through Natural Gas or Light Oil, the latter yielding more, yet requiring an extra step of distillation.

Use of a Centrifuge to separate into LPG and Methane should always be done, as it's practically free.

LPG and Methane are burned in a Gas Turbine. Among the other fuel options, they are the easier to set up which makes them excellent to get started. Their drawback is that they cannot be refined further, so upgrading the Fluid Drilling Rig may be a top priority if you stick to this fuel source.

Methane can also be obtained from a few natural sources. Between us, they're memes, but you can explore them if you wish. Ultimately, your power source is your choice.

quests.title: The Church of Benzene
quests.subtitle: In the end, it's always Benzene
quests.desc:
Benzene is a Gas Fuel. To skip some frustration, let's check out the two best ways to obtain it.

The first option is typically Wood based. Put Logs in the Pyrolyse Oven for Wood Tar. The Charcoal you obtain can then be processed in Extractors for even more Wood Tar, which you distill for Benzene.

Your second option is Heavy Oil based. Put Heavy Oil in a Distillery for Heavy Fuel, which you severely steam-crack and distill again for Benzene.

For information on how to process Oil, check the Light Fuel and Naphtha Quests.

In general, it is better to prioritize placing down more machines (parallelisation) over overclocking the recipes. That way, you end up losing less energy processing the resources.

Benzene is burned in a Gas Turbine. This power source starts weaker than its Diesel cousin, but gets a big boost when refined to Nitrobenzene in HV. Ultimately, your power source is your choice.

quests.title: Light Fuel
quests.subtitle: Turn up the Lights in here, baby
quests.desc:
Distillating Oil will give you Fuel that you will need to desulfurize.

Hydrogen Sulfide is perfectly recycled in an Electrolyzer.

To automate this process, simply place your Chemical Reactor and your Electrolyzer next to each other. Be sure to use your Screwdriver to enable input from the output side.

Light Fuel is a good Power option, but there's something even greater... check the Quest to the left.

quests.title: The Church of Diesel
quests.subtitle: Mother, how is diesel made?
quests.desc:
Diesel is a Oil-based Combustion Fuel.

To mix Light Fuel and Heavy Fuel, the right ratio for Distilleries is 3:2 from Oil or Raw Oil.

This would necessitate 3 Distilleries for Light Fuel, or 2 Distilleries for Heavy Fuel.

You can cut the amount of Oil required drastically by using Heavy Oil for Heavy Fuel specifically. Oilsands is a great option if you wish to go down this route!

(For the math nerds, you go from 8.33 Oil -> 6 Diesel, to 5 Oil + 0.4 Heavy Oil -> 6 Diesel)

Note: Do not attempt to put Oil in the Distillation Tower You might think it's a good idea because it gives both Light Fuel and Heavy Fuel. However, this process requires a lot of overclocking to be worth your time, and you will lose more energy than what the byproducts are worth.

In general, it is better to prioritize placing down more machines (parallelisation) over overclocking the recipes. That way, you end up losing less energy processing the resources.

Diesel is burned in a Combustion Generator. It can be further refined to Cetane-Boosted Diesel in HV. Compared to the other power sources, it is generally more efficient, but a more demanding processing line. If you take the Oil route for Polyethylene, this can work great with existing infrastructure. Ultimately, the power source you want to use is your choice.

quests.title: Multiple Channel Pipes
quests.subtitle: "I thought channels were disabled!"
quests.desc:
Regular GregTech Fluid Pipes can only transport one fluid type at a time.

Quadruple Fluid Pipes can transfer four different types of fluids. Nonuple Fluid Pipes can transfer nine different types! Useful for compact setups.

Nonuple Fluid Pipes will be useful in EV for handling the many outputs of the Distillation Tower.

Get either to finish this quest.

quests.title: IC Chips
quests.subtitle: I C U
quests.desc:
Using a Ruby Lens, engrave a Silicon Wafer into an ILC Wafer.

This can be processed in a Cutter for IC Chips, which are important components that will be precious when improving upon your circuit crafting capabilities.

quests.title: ULPIC Chips
quests.subtitle: Pickin' up Chips
quests.desc:
Using a Sapphire Lens, engrave a Silicon Wafer into an ULPIC Wafer.

This can be processed in a Cutter for ULPIC Chips, which are important for some multiblock components!

quests.title: Long-Distance Items
quests.subtitle: Wondering how to move those ores around?
quests.desc:
Long-Distance Item Pipes can be used to send items across great distances. Place one endpoint at each end, and connect them with pipeline blocks.

These pipes instantly transfer from the input side to the output side.

They also have a minimum distance between pipe endpoints, so you can't use these for short-range operations.

quests.title: Long-Distance Fluids
quests.subtitle: Wondering how to move that oil around?
quests.desc:
Long-Distance Fluid Pipes can be used to send fluids across great distances. Place one endpoint at each end, and connect them with pipeline blocks.

These pipes instantly transfer from the input side to the output side.

They also have a minimum distance between pipe endpoints, so you can't use these for short-range operations.

These pipes have a reputation of being buggy to set up. If they don't seem to work at first, try rotating the endpoints with a Wrench.

quests.title: I Believe I Can Fly
quests.subtitle: ...well, almost Fly
quests.desc:
This Jetpack takes most Combustion Fuels (excluding oils) to enable a primitive form of flight.

Fill it like you would fill a Drum, or with a Canning Machine.

Press H (default) to enable Hover Mode.

quests.title: I Believe I Can Fly II
quests.subtitle: ...still not quite there.
quests.desc:
This jetpack enables flight, and requires energy.

Charge it like you would charge a Battery.

Press H (default) to enable Hover Mode.

(Yeah, yeah, a jetpack is supposed to work off "propellants". Y'all are always here to nitpick.)

quests.title: Tricorder
quests.subtitle: For the nosiest GregTech players
quests.desc:
The Portable Scanner, or Tricorder, is GTCEu's debug tool.

Using it will show you a block's details, the details of the energy network, its current status and contents, and its impact on performance.

It'll also display a percentage of fluid remaining in the fluid vein for the Fluid Rigs.

Lore: This little useful device comes from GregTech 5. It was extremely useful in the early days, because very little information (basically nothing) was given by GUIs or WAILA.

quests.title: 200IQ
quests.subtitle: Knock, knock - who's that? It's MENSA!
quests.desc:
In this quest, we'll bestow upon you cursed wisdom... the ability to easily automate the Electrolyzer.

The Smart Item Filter is a filter that recognises Recipe Maps. When placed on the side of a machine, you can set it to "Electrolyzer" to allow insertion of electrolyzable items specifically. You heard that right - no need to filter manually!

Oh, and it only gets better. If you combine this bad boy with a Robot Arm, it'll only supply the exact amount of items required for a successful recipe to the machine.

Place the Robot Arm on your Electrolyzer, open its GUI, then place the Smart Filter inside. Ensure that the Smart Filter is set to Electrolyzer.

Next, configure it to Import and Supply Exact. Finally, attach any inventory (a chest will do) onto the side where you placed the Robot Arm. Ta-dah! You've figured out how to handle all Electrolyzer recipes!

What about the output of the Electrolyzer? You may want to route the Fluids to several Tanks - or even Super Tanks if you've gotten that far. We highly recommend that you avoid any methods involving voiding Fluids... except for Water for obvious reasons.

Note: The Robot Arm has to facilitate the item transfer for its filtering functionality to work! Inserting directly into the machine (for instance, pushing with a Hopper!) will ignore the Robot Arm's Supply Exact setting.

quests.title: Getting industrial
quests.subtitle: Getting industrial
quests.desc:
Welcome to your first (of many) forays into Hydrocarbons! You may already know a few of them, like Petrol, Plastic, Alcohol... well, in GregTech, you'll mass-produce many of these things, and more! This topic can be confusing at times, so you'll want to read carefully in order to avoid getting lost in the details. Now...

Here in MV, there are two main goals of working with Hydrocarbons - Power and Plastics. The interesting part is how you'll accomplish this. There are two main paths you can take: Petrochemicals and Biochemicals. Both have their pros and cons - Petrochemicals typically provide higher yields and energy-efficiency, while Biochemicals are fully renewable. Your source of Power and Plastics is entirely up to you.

Ready to dive in? The Petrochemical route(s) are to the left of this quest - you've got three options here. For those with green thumbs, the Biochemical routes are diagonally right. Best of luck!

quests.title: Desulfurization
quests.subtitle: Waiter! Why is there Sulfur in my soup?
quests.desc:
If you've tried to distill an Oil into Light Fuel, Heavy Fuel, etc., you might've realised that you can't. Instead, you've gotten this sulfurized version of it. How dreadful!

To fix this, we'll need to desulfurize it. Combine whatever you'd like to desulfurize with Hydrogen Gas in a Chemical Reactor to produce the pure product, along with a byproduct of Hydrogen Sulfide. What now?


Luckily, Hydrogen Sulfide is perfectly recycled in an Electrolyzer.

To automate this process, simply place your Chemical Reactor and your Electrolyzer next to each other. Be sure to enable input from the output side. We've graciously provided a Fluid Filter so that your desulfurized prize doesn't go into your Electrolyzer too - remember to set it to filter both input/extract so that the right fluids can flow both ways!


But now, how do we get our refined goodies out of our Chemical Reactor? This is where the Electric Pump comes in - use it on a different face of the Chemical Reactor and extract your prize!

Tip: You only need two buckets of Hydrogen Gas. The easiest way of obtaining it is by electrolyzing water. You don't need to mass-produce Hydrogen (yet), don't worry!

quests.title: Heavy Fuel
quests.subtitle: Things are getting really Heavy around here...
quests.desc:
Heavy Fuel can be obtained from many sources, but your best bet is distilling it from Heavy Oil. 

You could just burn it in a combustion generator now for Power, but there's something even greater... check the Quest to the left.

quests.title: Wood Tar
quests.desc:
This is the main way of getting Power via the Biochemical route. Put Logs in the Pyrolyse Oven for Wood Tar. The Charcoal you obtain can then be processed in Extractors for even more Wood Tar!

Check out the Distillation quest to see what you can do with this wondrous substance.

quests.title: High Voltage Machines
quests.desc:
When your power generation allows for it, HV machines are well worth using for on-demand processing. The quadrupled energy cost compared to their LV counterparts shouldn't bother you too much.

Note: Crafting Hulls in the Assembler is slightly more efficient. Prioritise this recipe!

quests.title: Super Batteries
quests.subtitle: Science-fiction batteries!
quests.desc: Energium Crystals store a significant amount of EU. They're also arguably cheaper.

quests.title: HV Autoclave
quests.subtitle: The Road To Power Storage
quests.desc:
The HV Autoclave - despite being optional - is a machine you'll definitively want to grab.

This unlocks unique Batteries that are far superior to their standard counterparts.

quests.title: High Voltage Bathing
quests.subtitle: Please don't bathe with electronics...
quests.desc:
The HV Chemical Bath is used to dye Lenses using Chemical Dye. This will let you engrave more types of Wafers!

This is also required for Eyes of Ender.

quests.title: One Hundred Million Pressure Cookers
quests.subtitle: Steam isn't the only answer, but we won't stop you
quests.desc:
If you're still running off the Large Bronze Boiler, you may (read: may not) want to upgrade to the Large Steel Boiler.

It's slightly more efficient than the LBB, and produces 900 EU/t worth of Steam...

We'd politely recommend that you try other power options at this point.

Anyway, here's a checkmark to skip the quest.

quests.title: Advanced Maintenance
quests.subtitle: Huge News for Maintenance Haters
quests.desc:
You now have access to two new Maintenance Hatches. Both hatches do not require Maintenance when first placed in a Multiblock. We're going to ask for both here.

The Automatic Maintenance Hatch is pretty simple - it eliminates the need for Maintenance, forever.

The Configurable Maintenance Hatch is more interesting. You can configure it to cut off 10% duration on recipes, at the cost of making Maintenance happen three times as fast. That's 16 real hours of activity.

Reminder: You can put Tape in the maintenance Hatch to automatically fix problems.

Lore: The Automatic Maintenance Hatch texture and mechanic comes from TecTech, an addon mod originally made for GregTech: New Horizons. 

You'll see more from TecTech if you stick with us all the way to the late game.

quests.title: We call this "fun"
quests.subtitle: Wouldn't you agree? Fun!
quests.desc:
Just like MV, you'll need to make higher-tier components.

The painful Flawless Gems have been replaced with slightly-more-painful Eyes of Ender. Also notice that the Motors require 2x Silver Cables.

Hulls and Machines use Gold Cables.

Tips to make life less painful:

- Consider using Electrotine decomposition and Ore Processing for Electrum.
- Consider using the Chemical Bath with Mercury for Gold from a wide variety of ores.

quests.title: HV Assembling Machine
quests.desc: The HV Assembling Machine is the next step in progression, but also unlocks a handful of useful utilities - snoop around this quest for more.

quests.title: Infinite Water
quests.subtitle: Truly revolutionary - water
quests.desc:
Time to say goodbye to your Primitive Water Pumps. They've done you proud.

Place this on the side of a machine to give it 16 buckets of Water every second!

Later on in EV, you can use these to craft Infinite Water Cells for your AE2 network.

quests.title: I see the world in colour
quests.subtitle: ...and it's beautiful
quests.desc:
The Simple System on Chip will be used for the best ULV Circuit recipe.

We'd also recommend going out of your way to make all the other useful colored Lenses ahead of time. They are listed here in an optional task.

quests.title: Best ULV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Good on you for making these!
quests.desc: ULV Circuits are used in some AE2 recipes, such as the Annihilation Core and Formation Core.

quests.title: Digital Fluid Storage
quests.subtitle: "Fluid Discretizer"? Who's that?
quests.desc:
Digital fluid storage is pretty cool. Each 1k ME Fluid Storage Cell holds up to 5 types of fluid, and can hold over 8,000 buckets of those 5 fluid types! That's more than a Super Tank 2!

You may want to format Fluid Storage Cells to certain types to prevent the network from clogging. Make a Cell Workbench to do so.

quests.title: Upgrade your EBF II
quests.subtitle: Noticing a pattern here?
quests.desc:
The Low Power Integrated Circuit requires an HV Cutting Machine inside your Cleanroom.

HV Energy Hatches will be nice additions so you can run your Multiblocks (mainly your Electric Blast Furnace and Large Chemical Reactor) directly off a HV line.

You'll need to upgrade your EBF with 2 Energy Hatches to obtain Nichrome coils - fun!

quests.title: High Voltage Chemistry
quests.subtitle: Chemistry-heads rejoice!
quests.desc:
The HV Chemical Reactor unlocks a ton of new recipes. Ah, who doesn't love the ever-growing demand on more advanced Chemistry?

Note: We've actually unlocked a ton of new content by reaching HV. However, not every processing line or item is crucial right now. It'd make the HV chapter far too dense if we included everything!

You could still check out the EV Chapter if you want to push yourself.

quests.title: Almost Titanium
quests.subtitle: ...means it WASN'T Titanium!
quests.desc:
Titanium requires a slightly more involved process than the previous materials to process. To get started, you'll need Rutile, which can be obtained by processing Bauxite.

Once you've gotten enough Rutile, your journey to Titanium continues in the next quest. There's no need to rush things.

You may have already noticed that Bauxite is also an excellent source of Aluminium.

Visiting The End allows you to access a richer Bauxite vein, as well as Ilmenite - an even better source of Rutile.

Tip: Ore processing for Bauxite is ridiculously good! Including the Thermal Centrifuge step easily triples your Rutile yield.

Bonus Tip: There is also an alternate route you can process Purified Bauxite in a Mixer and process into a greater return of Rutile, however this is much harder to initally batch so it is more ideal to use when you set up a dedicated Titanium processing line.

quests.title: Almost Titanium²
quests.subtitle: Nope, still not Titanium
quests.desc:
To get your grubby hands on some Titanium, Rutile must first be reduced to Titanium Tetrachloride in an HV Chemical Reactor, then reduced again in an EBF with Magnesium to finally arrive at Titanium.

The Carbon, Chlorine, Oxygen, and Magnesium used in the process can be perfectly recovered from the byproduct Carbon Monoxide and Magnesium Chloride with no loss. If you set up a system to recover them, the only material that you'll need to provide for Titanium is Rutile!

Titanium Tetrachloride also acts as a catalyst in the production of plastics. A touch of TiCl₄ in the polymerisation process can increase the polymer yield to 133%. Naturally, this is completely optional.

quests.title: HV Superconductors
quests.desc:
By this point, normal Cables have such insignificant loss that superconductors aren't a requirement.

You can use those as a flex!

Superconductors are still required to craft Field Generators.

quests.title: Nichrome Coils
quests.subtitle: A long way to go before we beat the Sun
quests.desc:
Nichrome Heating Coils increase the temperature of your Blast Furnace to 3,600K.

Despite not being required yet, it's still a good idea to upgrade your primary EBF for those sweet, sweet Coil bonuses.

Lore: In the original GregTech 5, there were only three types of coils. This was the last tier.

quests.title: Titanium
quests.subtitle: Titanite Slab
quests.desc:
Titanium has the highest corrosion resistance and strength-to-density ratio of any metallic element, so it's only natural that we'll be using it as the main material in our EV machinery.

Confirm that your closed loop for Rutile is up to standard, and prepare to run a dedicated EBF for hours.

A quick aside - now we're at EV, single block generators are about to fall out of style! You'll need to run several HV Generators to keep up - later, upgrades to Multiblock Generators will be required.

Whilst Multiblock generators are much more expensive, you'll find them to be much more energy efficient!

quests.title: 300IQ
quests.subtitle: Can we get much smarter? Apparently so
quests.desc:
This is a guide on how to automatically route all your Hot Ingots to your Vacuum Freezer while keeping everything automated perfectly fine.

You already know the drill from the Chemical Bath, so let's get right to the chase.

Install an Item Tag Filter Cover on the Input Bus of your Vacuum Freezer, open it and set to whitelist hot_ingots. The slot is to test items to make sure you configured the Filter properly in case you forgot. Drag ingots from emi!

From here, you now have the option to route either with Item Pipes or AE2.

With Item Pipes, connect the output of your EBF(s) to the input of your Vacuum Freezer and the output dump (Chest, Crate, or ME Interface).
You can also have a Restrictive Pipe in front of the output dump to ensure the Vacuum Freezer will be prioritized.

With AE2, place a ME Storage Bus on top of your Cover and set to very high priority. Any hot ingots entering your ME system will automatically be moved into the Vacuum Freezer.

Note: If you wish to know more tags for future automation setups, they will display if you press Shift when hovering over an item. For instance, an item tagged with #forge:plates/copper would be filtered as plates/copper.

Other Note: The Item Tag Filter partially supports regex. Hover over the info icon for more information.

quests.title: Ice Cold
quests.subtitle: Did you know vacuums actually make things hotter?
quests.desc:
The Vacuum Freezer is a multiblock used to cool down Hot Ingots. All ingots made in the EBF with a temperature above 1750K will need it.

Most of the time, your Electric Blast Furnace will be the bottleneck, so you don't need to worry about overclocking your freezer.

Since Hot Ingots hurt you when held, item routing will be very useful.

quests.title: MV Superconductors
quests.desc: 4A of MV energy transmission may be appealing, but you probably don't need these at this point. Frankly, you might find it easier to just jump straight to the HV Superconductors. The option is always there if you need it. Boron can be obtained as a Salt processing byproduct.

quests.title: HV Circuit Assembler
quests.desc:
The next Circuit Assembler. This tier of Circuit Assembler is only required for one type of Circuit - your first ever Mainframe.

You can put this off until EV.

quests.title: The End of an Era
quests.subtitle: ...but not the end of your playthrough
quests.desc:
Regardless of whether you're playing on Peaceful or not, you will have to defeat the Ender Dragon to return to the Overworld safely.

Prepare yourself with a bow, a few arrows, a blade, and some beds (speedrunner strats!).

This quest will print the location of the nearest Stronghold in chat. This won't work if you have cheats disabled, unfortunately.

The End has even more new veins and materials to harvest, such as Platinum and Naquadah! Similar to Nether ores, End ores are worth two Overworld ores.

Eyes of Ender are additionally used in crafting certain components, or for creating Quantum Eyes.

quests.title: Mainframes - first IV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Whoah, what a unique... texture
quests.desc:
The first IV circuit. Congratulations on making it this far!

This will be used when you get to EV.

Lore: The original texture for the Mainframes was taken from an image of an IBM zEnterprise mainframe.

quests.title: The Cleanroom
quests.subtitle: They put me in a room... a clean room
quests.desc:
The Cleanroom is a hollow multiblock free of contaminants. Going forward, most new circuit-related recipes, as well as some unique blocks like Fusion Casings, will need to be run in machines inside a Cleanroom.

Creating it will put your Polyethylene automation to the test for all the Plascrete and/or Cleanroom Glass you will make.

We'll start you off with a 5x5x5 Cleanroom, but you should upgrade its size when you start feeling cramped.

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on the Cleanroom in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

Lore: The Cleanroom comes from GregTech 5, but as is often the case, the GT5 implementation is much more punishing. The recipes would run without regard to cleanliness, but had a % chance of completion equal to the cleanliness of the Cleanroom. This caused a lot of accidental voiding.

Furthermore, if the Cleanroom had a maintenance issue, the cleanliness would cap out at 90%, which means 10% of items would be voided.

The GTCEu Cleanroom will never void recipe outputs.

quests.title: HV Prospector
quests.subtitle: Fluids just got easier
quests.desc:
This prospector boasts a higher range than its LV counterpart.

Sneak right-clicking with the Prospector in your hand will change its mode to Fluid Prospection. This will instead display the base yield of Fluid veins, along with how full they are.

Reminder: The yield of a Fluid Drilling Rig is directly proportional to the "fullness" of the vein. When low enough, the output will default to a yield that can be defined in the CEu config.

Tip: When the fullness drops below 30% (alright, rough estimate), you should consider moving to another vein for the sake of power-to-yield efficiency. Aiming for the Advanced Fluid Drilling Rig in EV may interest you, as not only does it significantly boost the output, but it also reduces the rate at which it drains the vein!

Caution: Due to a bug, the Prospector display may be offset by one chunk, so avoid placing your Rig in the corners of a fluid vein, or you may end up pumping the wrong fluid!

quests.title: Fluorine
quests.desc:
Fluorine is the rarest gas to obtain from electrolysis in this game.

You can obtain it from Lepidolite, Bastnatite, and Mica, but the best source is from Topaz.

quests.title: Polytetrafluoroethylene
quests.subtitle: A.K.A - Teflon
quests.desc:
Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) is a Carbon-Fluorine polymer. Similarly to PVC, PTFE can be used to increase the yield of Plastic Circuit Boards and other crafts. Presently, it is used to make the walls of Large Chemical Reactors.

Chlorine is required in the process, which is fully recycled if you electrolyze the Hydrochloric Acid waste.

Fluorine will also be used in the Uranium and Naquadah refinement processes.

Tip: From this point onwards, you should be solidifying Polymers as Bar (Ingot) form, as they can easily be shaped to whichever form you will need using the Extruder.

quests.title: Benzene Nitro
quests.subtitle: Click this link for free Nitro
quests.desc:
Making Nitrobenzene will boost the energy efficiency of Benzene roughly fourfold. You love to see it.

The catch here is that it takes Distilled Water, and consumes a bit of Sulfur with every step.

quests.title: Large Chemical Reactor
quests.subtitle: The Magic Box to end all Magic Boxes
quests.desc:
The Large Chemical Reactor (LCR) is a multiblock Chemical Reactor with more input/output slots. Its Teflon casings allow it to process extra recipes.

The LCR can perform some exclusive reactions which allow you to combine multiple normal Chemical Reactor steps into a single step. Examples below:

- Nitrogen Dioxide (for Nitric Acid and Dinitrogen Tetroxide)
- Sulfuric Acid
- Phenol
- Epichlorohydrin (you need it for Epoxy!)
- Epoxy

There are also LCR exclusive recipes. For example, Radon can only be made in the LCR right now.

All Overclocks in the LCR are 100% efficient (known as Perfect overclocks); each overclock will quadruple the speed, as opposed to doubling it.

The LCR has yet another advantage. You can use 2 Energy Hatches to allow it to run recipes of the next tier, similar to the Electric Blast Furnace.

Tip: Given the amount of LCRs you may end up using, why not try utilising wallsharing to its full potential?

You could share one 2A (standard) Energy Hatch between 2 LCRs.

You might want to share Input/Output Hatches, but make sure each LCR is running the correct recipe.

Keep in mind Maintenance hatches cannot wallshare.

quests.title: TNT
quests.subtitle: An explosive discovery
quests.desc:
TNT (Trinitrotoluene, to be pedantic) can't be prepared with the usual "Gunpowder and Sand" routine anymore. Instead, you'll have to produce Gelled Toluene and react it with Sulfuric Acid to make TNT.

Using the HV Chemical Reactor recipe will double the yield of Gelled Toluene at the cost of requiring additional inputs.

We don't have any easy tips to give you for Toluene. We regret to inform you that making TNT is going to be tricky no matter which route you take.

That being said, don't miss out on the direct Heavy Fuel to Toluene recipe in the single block Distillery.

Toluene can also optionally be used in EV to make Gasoline.

TNT is used in the Implosion Compressor to make Gems. It also blows stuff (and your friends) up. 

ITNT can also be used in the Implosion Compressor at a better rate but require Nitration Mixture to make. It also ignites when punched so be careful when it is placed.

Get either TNT or ITNT to complete this quest.

Technically, Dynamite or Powerbarrels could also be used... but Dynamite is a pain to obtain, whereas Powerbarrels are weaker.

quests.title: Blaze Powder
quests.subtitle: Took a while to get some of this, right?
quests.desc:
If you've killed a Blaze before, you may have noticed it dropped Sulfur Dust instead of a Blaze Rod.

In order to get Blaze Powder, you must instead combine Carbon Dust and Sulfur Dust in a HV Chemical Reactor.

quests.title: Ender Pearls
quests.subtitle: Darn "artificial gating"
quests.desc:
If you've killed an Enderman before, you may have noticed it dropped Enderpearl Dust instead of an Ender Pearl. In order to get Ender Pearls, you must make them from Enderpearl Dust in the Implosion Compressor.

Pro tip for non-Peaceful players: Trapping Endermen in Boats makes them much easier to kill! They also have a higher spawn rate in Warped Forests in the Nether compared to other locations before The End.

In the long term, Enderpearl Dust should be made in an HV Mixer.

quests.title: Phosphorus Boules
quests.subtitle: That's dope, bro!
quests.desc:
Doping is the process of intentionally introducing impurities into semiconductors to alter their properties.

Silicon can be doped with small amounts of Phosphorus to make a better Silicon Boule.

Phosphorus-doped Silicon Boules typically yield 8 times more wafers than their non-doped counterparts. However, they are not strictly needed until EV, where they'll be used in more complex wafers.

Note: All processing of Phosphorus Boules and Wafers will require a Cleanroom.

quests.title: Implosion Compressor
quests.subtitle: Controlled GregTech Explosion
quests.desc:
The Implosion Compressor turns the Dust form of Gems into their Gem form.

It's required to get Ender Pearls.

In descending order of efficiency, ITNT, TNT, Dynamite, or Powerbarrels can be used for the Implosion.

quests.title: Nitric Acid
quests.subtitle: This won't eat your skin
quests.desc:
Nitric Acid's fumes are extremely toxic! It's also a very potent oxidizer.

Nitric Acid is used in:

- Cetane-Boosted Diesel, a better Diesel fuel that you'll get to soon.
- Nitrobenzene, a better Gas fuel that we're not far off making.
- The optional gem slurry chains.
- The Platinum Group Sludge chain in IV.
- Polybenzimidazole (try saying that ten times fast!) in IV.

There are two main ways to make Nitric Acid - the good way and the bad ways!

Therefore, we'll point you towards the best method:

Get a Large Chemical Reactor and use it to make Nitrogen Dioxide. From there, react it in a regular Chemical Reactor with more Oxygen and Water and you got yourself some Nitric Acid!

This requires a lot of Oxygen. Refer to its own Quest in LV for the most efficient ways to obtain in large amount. Notice that this is Hydrogen free!

quests.title: Cetane-boosted Diesel
quests.subtitle: This is to go further beyond
quests.desc:
Cetane-Boosted Diesel is 50% more energy dense than regular Diesel, and only requires 1 part Tetranitromethane (TNM) for every 50 parts Diesel. What a steal, right?

The catch comes in the complexity of making TNM. In order to concoct it, you must react Nitric Acid with either Ethenone or Methyl Acetate, both of which have quite an involved process themselves!

quests.title: SMD Components
quests.subtitle: Alternate components!
quests.desc:
The SMD (surface-mount device) Components are CEu-exclusive components that are required for the next tier of circuits (Nano). They can also be used in place of their non-SMD counterparts in most Circuit recipes.

Of course they're more complicated to make, this is GregTech! What did you expect?

Invest in automation for these - you won't stop needing them.

quests.title: More SMD Components
quests.desc:
Platinum (needed for SMD Diodes) has its own dedicated Quest in the EV tab.

Lore: The SMD Inductors make an appearance in GTCEu for the first time. It's more consistent and convenient to have all Circuit components receive an SMD counterpart.

quests.title: Universal Macerator
quests.subtitle: Shredding your Ores with byproducts
quests.desc:
The HV Macerator finally unlocks more of the slots shown in emi. With this, the first three slots are now available. This allows you to receive the unique ore Byproduct from macerating Thermal Centrifuged Ore.

Macerating Ores costs 32 EU/t with two overclocks, which makes this machine able to fit in LV lines - very, very convenient.

Despite the signicant improvements this machine provides, it still isn't completely worth it to set up advanced processing for every single ore. Energy and time are still things to consider, so only process the ores that produce Byproducts you care for.

Lore: In GregTech 5u, the HV Macerator only gave two slots. Urgh... we weren't planning on using that Stone Dust, anyway...

quests.title: Ammonia
quests.subtitle: The Nectar of the Gods
quests.desc:
Ammonia is optional in HV.

You may use it in the production of:

- Nitric Acid, although we don't recommend this.

- Rocket Fuel, required for Nether Stars in IV.

- Polybenzimidazole (now THAT'S a tongue twister!) in IV.

The "least painful" recipe should be the one in the HV Chemical Reactor. Make sure your Hydrogen production (mostly from Water electrolysis) is enough to support the demand.

You could also obtain Ammonia as a waste product from Platinum Group Sludge processing.

quests.title: Nanomuscles, son!
quests.subtitle: GregTech and armour - a match made in heaven!
quests.desc:
If you heeded our suggestion for Peaceful mode, you shouldn't need to defend yourself from pesky mobs - but this armor provides some nice QoL that you may enjoy!

Helmet: Provides Night vision (the default keybind is M, but check for conflicts)

Chestplate: Provides nothing interesting right now, but can be further upgraded into the Advanced version at the very end of HV.

Leggings: Provides nothing special... perhaps we should have given it a small speed boost?

Boots: Provides Step Assist. Yes, you read that right!

Lore: This armor was inspired by IndustrialCraft, but its first implementation within the "GTCE ecosystem" came with Nuke Powered Utils, an addon mod to GTCE.

quests.title: Large Steam Turbine
quests.subtitle: If you're still running off Steam... boy, do we have a solution for you!
quests.desc:
It's time to be blunt - Steam as a power option isn't gonna cut the mustard anymore. If you're going to stick with Steam until the bitter end, we wish you luck... so read on.

The Large Steam Turbine acts as a multiblock Steam Turbine for HV power. It's quite an expensive multiblock to build just for a measly 1024 EU/t, but it also boasts the powerful ability to scale to higher power production and higher efficiency.

(We're trying our best to sell it here, okay?!)

The Turbine outputs Distilled Water if given an Output Hatch, which you could loop back to your Large Boilers. We don't entirely endorse this, as your Boilers could end up exploding if this system fails.

Each Large Turbine will require a Turbine Rotor and a Rotor Holder. You can find out more about the Turbine mechanics in the next Quest.

Note: This Quest only requires the Controller, but you'll still need all the multiblock parts to construct the Turbine.

Turbines void energy if they're unable to output. If you plan on using this with HV power, use a Rotor close to a 100% Power stat.

quests.title: Large Turbine Mechanics
quests.subtitle: My mechanics are... complex
quests.desc:
The Large Turbines are the most complex Multiblock Generators that you'll find in GTCEu in the present day.

Let's delve into Turbines!

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on Turbine Mechanics in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Upgrading your EBF III
quests.subtitle: Perfectly regular progression
quests.desc:
Starting from here, you can now craft EV Energy and Dynamo Hatches! In EV, Energy Hatches now come with 4A, 16A, 32A or even 64A variants!

This is extremely useful for Multiblock generators, or boosting your GCYM machinery.

quests.title: Large Gas Turbine
quests.subtitle: Gassed Up
quests.desc:
The Large Gas Turbine may not impress you in EV, but it can scale to better production than the base 4096 EU/t, and boasts higher efficiency than those pesky singleblock generators.

Each Large Turbine will require a Turbine Rotor and a Rotor Holder. More on the Turbine mechanics in the next Quest.

Note: This Quest only requires the Controller, but obviously you still need all the multiblock parts to construct the Turbine.

Turbines void energy if they're unable to output. If you plan on using this with HV power, use a Rotor with close to 100% Power stat.

quests.title: Power Integrated Circuit
quests.subtitle: No one machine should have all this power!
quests.desc:
So, what's on the agenda today?

New Cutting Machine? ✓

New Lens? ✓

New Power Circuit? ✓

New EBF Metal required? ✓

New Coil for Energy Hatch? ✓

Note: If you haven't found a Jungle yet, you can centrifuge Stone Dust for Metal Mixture Dust. This doubles as a Brown Dye.

quests.title: Large Combustion
quests.desc:
The Large Combustion Engine is a multiblock that can burn Combustion Fuels for even... more... power generation!

By default, this puppy outputs 2048 EU/t, which is equivalent to 4 Turbo Combustion Generators.

This might not convince you, but unlike the singleblock generators, you can actually boost this by providing Oxygen. This will double the fuel consumption, but triple the power generation. This'll output 6144 EU/t with 150% Fuel efficiency.

You'll also need to supply a small amount of Lubricant to the LCE during operation - nothing too crazy.

The LCE is also void-free, unlike Large Turbines. Any energy that cannot be output will momentarily pause the multiblock.

To harness the cataclysmic power of the 6144 EU/t output, you'll need a 4A EV Dynamo Hatch or an IV Dynamo Hatch.

quests.title: Extreme Fluid Drilling
quests.subtitle: "Extreme" is an apt descriptor
quests.desc:
At the cost of 4x the drilling Power, the Advanced Fluid Drilling Rig gains 16x the output in Fluid.

The depletion of veins is also halved. This means you will get a total of 32x as much Oil (or whatever fluid) from a bedrock fluid vein until depletion!

Even when depleted, you can still use this and be firmly power positive.

One single Advanced Fluid Drilling Rig should be enough to supply Power for a base up to LuV... and possibly beyond!

As a reminder, since this last appeared in MV:

- Light Oil can be used for LPG and Methane to run in Large Gas Turbines.
- Oil can be used for Diesel and Cetane-Boosted Diesel to run in Large Combustion Engines.
- Heavy Oil can be used for Nitrobenzene to run in Large Gas Turbines.

quests.title: Platinum
quests.subtitle: Diamond and Pearl
quests.desc:
How do you get Platinum?

- As a byproduct of Nickel Ore processing, either with a Chemical Bath, or a HV Macerator
- From Sheldonite veins in the End
- From Platinum Group Sludge, a complicated chain granting all Platinum Group metals. Highly unrecommended until IV, but eventually required.

Platinum is needed in small amounts for SMD Diodes, and for a plethora of recipes starting in EV.

Lore: Mekanism's primary material was planned to be Platinum. However, GregoriusT didn't agree - the fact that such a rare material could be found all over the world wasn't quite to his tastes. The Mekanism devs thus opted to make the primary material Osmium.

quests.title: Multiblock Miner
quests.desc:
Yeah, we admit it... this Multiblock is entirely optional. Many playtesters kept using their HV singleblock miners and didn't run into any issues with regards to throughput.

However, the Large Miner will further boost your returns when collecting ores from veins. Ores are mined as if they were macerated, but they yield three times as many Crushed ores.

You'll need to automate Drilling Fluid before you run this. You might also want to use a Quantum Tunnel from AE2 and GTEU P2P Tunnels to transfer items and provide power.

Lore: There were some interesting side effects when we ported this over from Gregicality, as the multiblock actually originated from GregTech 5.

Its current implementation isn't perfect, as you don't really get much of a benefit by upgrading this miner to the advanced versions. We were considering a rebalance of energy consumption and yield per tier.

quests.title: EV Assembler
quests.subtitle: Pushing into EV
quests.desc: You know the drill by now. It's an Assembler - what else do you need to know?

quests.title: Extreme Batteries
quests.desc:
The best battery-like item for EV!

Making a Lapotron Crystal will require quite a bit of nested crafting, so craft these in large batches - or, of course, automate everything with AE2 patterns.

quests.title: Tungsten Chain
quests.subtitle: All in one!
quests.desc:
This quest will cover the Tungsten Chain. Tungsten is obtained from either Tungstate or Scheelite, which are part of the same vein in the End.

Just like the Rutile chain, you will only need to input one item type, and everything else will be looped perfectly!

You will need:

- One Chemical Bath, to make Tungstic Acid.
- One Electrolyzer, to electrolyze both Bath outputs.
- One Chemical Reactor, to loop Hydrogen and Chlorine back into Hydrochloric Acid.

You will be granted either Calcium or Lithium as a byproduct - it's on the house!

Lore: In GTCE, Tungstate or Scheelite took 40sec and 4,000mB of Hydrogen @ 1920 EU/t to electrolyze into 1 Tungsten Dust. Even if it was simpler, it was much more expensive!

quests.title: Extreme Mixing
quests.subtitle: Beginning to notice a pattern?
quests.desc:
The EV Mixer is required for more alloys.

This is important if you want to get your hands on some Tungstensteel.

quests.title: Tungstensteel
quests.subtitle: The main IV Material!
quests.desc:
50% of Tungstensteel's composition is Steel... suffice to say, you'll need large quantities of both Iron and Tungsten!

Right now, Tungsten is a crucial ingredient for the next tier of coils. You're one step closer to moving away from those pesky single block Generators!

Boosting the smelting recipe in the Electric Blast Furnace will require Helium. You can get an initial supply from extracting Monazite or centrifuging Endstone.

In the long term, you will want to get Helium via Liquid Air Distillation.

quests.title: Tungsten
quests.subtitle: Wolfram Alpha
quests.desc:
Tungsten in Swedish translates directly as "Heavy Stone", which is pretty funny. You may know of this element as Wolfram too, hence its formula being W.

Tungsten will be the main IV Cable material, but will also show its face elsewhere!

quests.title: P2P Tunnels
quests.subtitle: Fear not, channels are gone
quests.desc:
Even without channels, P2P Tunnels are still extremely important automation tools!
