Note: There are many fantastic resources online to explain how P2P Tunnels work. The short description is that they allow transfer of things from 1 input to endless output. You can link them using a Memory Card.

This Quest acts as a tutorial on how to use the P2P Tunnels to your own benefit.

quests.title: Gregicality Multiblocks
quests.subtitle: We made it so far...
quests.desc:
If you want a big boost to your processing abilities, look no further than these powerful Multiblocks!

Formerly known as Gregicality Multiblocks, the add-on has been fully integrated into GregTech CEu Modern!

You'll need to make use of many new alloys to build the new Multiblocks. Yay, more content!

This quest unlocks a new tutorial on the GCYM mechanics in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: RTM Alloy Coils
quests.subtitle: What happened to my Tungstensteel!?
quests.desc:
RTM Alloy Coils raise the Heat Capacity to a scorching 4,500K.

These will be required in IV, but as always, you should get them earlier for the Coil bonuses.

Lore: These coils used to be Tungstensteel Coils. This however made most of the EV tier very easy to skip over and postpone to IV, so they were changed to the new RTM Alloy. GTNH was the first to make this change, and used TPV Alloy instead, made from Titanium, Platinum, and Vanadium.

Which coil do you prefer?

quests.title: Diode
quests.desc:
The Diode (the block, not the component) accepts energy inputs from 5 sides, but only outputs through a single side.

Its main purpose is to limit Amperage. It'll help you regulate any centralised power generation, so you can distribute your power everywhere without having to math out what cable size is safe depending on consumers...

They can also be used as an electric equivalent to Repeaters when the voltage loss from Cables (or GTEU P2P tunnels - more on that later!) gets too high.

You can use a Soft Hammer to change the Amperage limit of the output.

Any of the listed Diodes will complete this Quest.

quests.title: Worse Combustion
quests.subtitle: We're gonna need more charcoal
quests.desc:
The Large Titanium Boiler is yet another steam boiler. It's even more efficient than the previous ones, and produces more steam too. However, Steam barely scales at this voltage.

This thing absolutely isn't worth it, and there are so many better options for power by this point, so you should avoid making this. The better options require EV energy hatches, which are later in the chapter.

Note: The LTB is so awful that we'll let you skip this quest (for completion progress) by clicking the Checkmark. You don't actually have to craft one.

Lore: In GT5u, players can construct another multiblock - the Large Heat Exchanger. It's also constructed with titanium, but produced Superheated Steam from hot IC2 coolant, or lava. This was a much better than regular steam, and was actually viable! This might make its way to CEu in the future.

quests.title: The "Fun" will never stop
quests.subtitle: Wait, no, don't leave!
quests.desc:
A lot of Kanthal will be required for the EV Motors. This may feel painful when you're on the edge of EV, but it won't be a problem later - trust us.

Neodymium is the metal used for Magnetic Rods.

Aluminium Cables require PVC to be insulated.

Lastly, you will need Radon for the Quantum Eyes in the emitter recipe.

quests.title: ME Hatches
quests.subtitle: No more interfaces on buses
quests.desc:
ME Hatches directly connect to your AE System, allowing you to skip placing interfaces directly on buses and hatches.

Lore: This feature originally comes from GTNH!

quests.title: EV Circuit Assembling
quests.subtitle: Consider yourself firmly in EV!
quests.desc:
Quite a journey to get here.

The EV Circuit Assembling "unlocks" the next tier of Circuits, Nano.

quests.title: Extreme Chemical Reactions
quests.subtitle: Chemistry gets even harder
quests.desc:
Note that in theory, the EV Chemical Reactor is skippable by using the Large Chemical Reactor instead, but we're evil enough to force you to make this.

It's still useful due to its compact size!

Honestly, if making one of these is a huge problem material-wise, take a look at your infrastructure.

quests.title: Nano CPUs
quests.desc:
Nano CPU Wafers are made by infusing regular CPU Wafers with Glowstone and Raw Carbon Fibers.

They will be needed in large quantities when making the Nano line of Circuits.

Higher-tier polymers will increase your yield of Raw Carbon Fibers. One Epoxy is enough for a full stack.

Tip: Don't like mining Glowstone? Check out Redstone ore processing.

quests.title: Best HV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: The Pinnacle... the Zenith
quests.desc:
Making the best HV Circuits will allow for easier HV Machines for your on-demand automation. Convenient!

Starting from here, all circuit lines will be consistent with each other until the end of the game.

Each tier until ZPM will show its own challenges when acquiring new and better Circuits.

quests.title: Advanced Circuit Boards
quests.subtitle: Quite the gold cost...
quests.desc:
Now that you have Epoxy, you can make Advanced Circuit Boards! The next Circuit tier is oh so close, so keep pushing.

Tips:

- Consider using Electrotine decomposition and Ore Processing for Electrum.
- Consider using the Chemical Bath with Mercury for Gold, from a wide variety of ores.

quests.title: System on Chip
quests.subtitle: Where's the Chip on System?
quests.desc:
The System-on-Chip (or SoC) is a specialty component that miniaturizes all the typical circuit components (like SMDs) onto a single chip.

These are very useful as they unlock the most efficient recipes for creating Refined, Micro, and Nano Circuits.

Each SoC-based circuit recipe requires a different minimum tier of Assembling Machine, with the Nano Circuits requiring you to hit LuV. System on Chip unlocks the best recipe for LV Circuits.

quests.title: More EV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger
quests.desc:
Nothing to say here.

Nice work, we suppose.

quests.title: More IV Circuits
quests.subtitle: Set sail, IV is on the horizon
quests.desc: Entering IV with these Circuits shouldn't be too painful.

quests.title: NOR Gates
quests.desc:
A NOR gate takes two input signals, and outputs a high signal only if both input signals are low.

This doesn't matter for now though, and it's simply a more complex wafer.
It is used for the Data Orb (a key component for when you get into Research), and in Nano Circuit production.

quests.title: First LuV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: I LuV GregTech
quests.desc: Save two of these for the IV Circuit Assembler.

quests.title: EV Superconductors
quests.subtitle: Power Creep
quests.desc: Amperage goes up - we're now at a whopping 6A per Superconductor.

quests.title: Ender Fluid Linking
quests.subtitle: Ender? I ha- Ok, this joke is old
quests.desc:
Ender Fluid Link Covers are GregTech's Ender Tanks. Each Cover sends fluids through a frequency defined by an 8-digit hexadecimal number (0-9, then A-F for each digit). That gives you 4.29 billion channels to work with! Ender fluid Link Covers work as long as their chunk is loaded - regardless of distance or dimension.

Setting the mode to Import will pull fluids from the world location it is attached to, while Export will push fluids to the world. Each channel has a buffer of 64 buckets.

You can also make similar Ender Item Link Covers to transport items, these have the same mechanics as the fluid ones. Craft any of them to complete this quest

Lastly, clicking on the Lock in the ui lock the cover to your own private channel, which is helpful if you're playing on a server.

You can assign a description to each channel in the cover, and list all of them. I recommend you set up a system to organise all those channels. I would also advise against using the default channel (FFFFFFFF) in all cases.

Lore: Before these were added to GTm, we had Ender Storage in the pack. If you are still using them we highly recommend changing them over to the GregTech covers, which can be done in a crafting table.

quests.title: Advanced Energy Detector
quests.subtitle: Save on Redstone contraptions
quests.desc:
The Advanced Energy Detector is an RS-latch for Energy Control.

Slap it on a Battery Buffer, set a Minimum EU near 0, and a Maximum EU near the max capacity.

The Cover will output a Redstone signal until the high threshold is reached, and then it will wait until the low threshold is reached to output redstone again.

This is a must to automate inertia-based power generators such as Boilers, or more importantly Large Turbines.

quests.title: Quantum Tunnel
quests.subtitle: [-h^2/2m∇^2 + V(x,t)]ψ(x,t) = iht'ψ(x,t)
quests.desc:
You can now remotely expand your AE2 network if you're looking to move bases.

A full set of two Quantum Tunnels is no slouch to craft, so you'll need to break a sweat if you want them.

quests.title: Uranium Refinement
quests.subtitle: Let's hope this quest doesn't put you on a list
quests.desc:
Uranium has very few uses in GTCEu right now, as we're yet to implement our Nuclear Reactor (it's planned!).

Make Uranium Hexafluoride from Uraninite. This will allow you to seperate the isotopes between U-238 and U-235.

Fluorine is fully recycled in the reaction, but you will need around maybe 40 buckets to bootstrap.

Hydrogen is consumed, so you will need quite a lot of it. Either electrolyze a lot of water using several LV Electrolyzers for the best energy efficiency, or recycle any unused hydrocarbons such as Methane.

Tip: You can make this setup compact by having one single Centrifuge and using a Fluid Regulator set to Supply Exact 1,000.

quests.title: Radon
quests.subtitle: 200 seconds?! WHAT?!
quests.desc:
Don't worry, this is nothing compared to running an EBF.

Hopefully for you, the LCR recipe in TMI for Radon should be self explanatory on how to set it up.

Input some Uranium, loop back the Plutonium using a Electric Furnace, and let the magic happen.

Lore: This cursed yet charming "chain" is inherited from GregTech 5. Plutonium 239 existing as an ore is a sort of a placeholder to avoid having to obtain it through complicated means.

quests.title: Epoxy
quests.subtitle: Prime circuit board material
quests.desc:
Epoxy is the foundation for all future Circuit Boards in the game. Get ready to make a lot of this stuff.

Note: This questline is quite complicated, so tackle everything else before trying to make Epoxy. You'll need all the help you can get!

There are several ways to obtain the ingredients for Epoxy.

We'll ignore recipes related to Glycerol, as they're never worth it unless modpack devs tweak things around. Hint hint, if you're making your own pack!

Try and use Large Chemical Reactors to shortcut recipes.

Chlorine is only partially recycled, so you'll need a constant supply. Feeding it manually should be sufficient at first.

Recipes involving Sodium Hydroxide could be looped by electrolyzing the Salt Water co-product.

Doing this will probably require you to make some Quadruple Pipes, Pump Covers, Fluid Filters... or route everything using AE2.

In general, closed loop setups are nice to prevent overproduction or overconsumption, but go for whatever's more fun for you.

quests.title: Epichlorohydrin
quests.subtitle: Putting the "Epic" in "Epichlorohydrin"
quests.desc:
You're going to want to use the LCR recipe for your first Epichlorohydrin.

This recipe requires Propene, which means that Oil Processing and Fuel Cracking are now necessary ventures. We're truly sorry if you've been dragging your heels!

It's quite important that you make your first Distillation Tower before progressing to the right.

Last of all, if you haven't noticed, you can loop the Salt Water byproduct to bootstrap the Sodium Hydroxide.

quests.title: Phenol
quests.subtitle: But that's just how I Phenol
quests.desc:
Obtain Phenol from:

1 - Wood Tar distillation.

2 - Reacting Benzene with Chlorine and Sodium Hydroxide This should be performed in an LCR, and the Chlorine is perfectly looped.

3 - Shortcutting the above recipe by ignoring the Sodium Hydroxide. This is net negative on Chlorine.

4 - Reacting Benzene, Propene and Oxygen using a small amount of Phosphoric Acid - normally a catalyst, but consumed here in small quantities. This is called the Cumene process.

If you're having trouble deciding between the options presented, here's the appeal of each route:

1 - Great if you're willing to set up a Pyrolyse Oven + Distillation Tower combo.

2 - This one's our preference! You can use the Salt to make more Salt Water, then electrolyze to create a loop.

3 - Very appealing if you have an infinite supply of Chlorine. You could achieve this by setting up a Fluid Drilling Rig on a Salt Water vein.

4 - ...Phosphoric Acid is a pain to produce. You'd be mad to consider this.

Lore: The Benzene + Oxygen recipe was added in GTCE with the intent of fixing missing chemical recipes, as the Large Chemical Reactor didn't exist. Now that CEu provides more realistic options, we may remove the recipe in future.

quests.title: Acetone
quests.desc:
While distilling Wood Vinegar gives Acetone, the yield is low and might not meet your needs.

You might want to use the direct LCR recipe from Acetic Acid to obtain Acetone.

If you want to be fancy, you can also use the good old Acetic Acid -> Dissolved Calcium Acetate -> Acetone loop, which does not require a LCR, but still recycles the Calcium compound perfectly.

We've been talking about Acetic Acid, but we're yet to tell you how to obtain it. Wouldn't that be great?

Acetic Acid can be synthesized in the Chemical Reactor. Our favorite recipe is from Ethylene.

quests.title: Silicone Rubber
quests.subtitle: An alternate rubber
quests.desc:
Silicone Rubber has its uses in Cable insulation, Conveyor recipes, and more.

You may want to shelve this quest for now, as tackling Silicone Rubber can be a pretty severe Chlorine sink.

Note: Rubber will eventually be usurped by either Silicone Rubber or Styrene-Butadienne Rubber for IV Cables.

Both these rubbers will be required once you make it to LuV.

quests.title: Styrene-Butadiene Rubber
quests.subtitle: Steel Ball Run
quests.desc:
Styrene-Butadiene Rubber (SBR) is an enhanced type of Rubber.

It can be used in place of Rubber in most recipes, and is four times more efficient at insulating Cables. It is also strictly required for certain higher-tier recipes.

This isn't exactly worth making until you get the Distillation Tower, but it eventually becomes easier with Fuel Cracking.

Note: Rubber will eventually be usurped by either Silicone Rubber or Styrene-Butadienne Rubber for IV Cables.

Both these rubbers will be required once you make it to LuV.

quests.title: Gasoline
quests.desc:
Gasoline is an extremely powerful Combustion Fuel. It's over 3 times as energy dense as Diesel!

Note: This Combustion Fuel exists solely as a challenge for you to overcome if you desire to take on it.

It's completely optional, so we'll let you figure out how to obtain it, without any further tips.

quests.title: High Octane Gasoline
quests.subtitle: Fetch me my Draconium!
quests.desc:
Multiply the energy density by 2 and you have High Octane Gasoline.

Note: Automating this requires a lot of Distillation Towers! A huge portion of your base would be dedicated to producing this constantly without anything bottlenecking.

If you have a base successfully running on HOG, feel free to brag and share some screenshots on our Discord.

quests.title: Distillation Tower
quests.subtitle: Time for a real oil refinery!
quests.desc:
The Distillation Tower is a multiblock that will help you extract the most out of fluids. In Distilleries, you had to pick only one product to output. DTs output all of it at once!

Note the special placement rules: Energy, Items, and Fluid Inputs can only be placed on the bottom layer. Maintenance can be placed anywhere. Fluid Outputs can only be placed starting on the second vertical layer, and only allow one per layer as well.

The DT can form at any height from 3 to 13 blocks, allowing 2 to 12 Fluid Outputs. Fluid will always output on the layer corresponding to the recipe in emi.

The DT has one more special rule. You can deliberately build it too short, and it will void whichever Fluid should have been output at the top layers that were removed. This can be useful in some very niche situations.

It is a good idea to use Super Tanks as the output for every layer.

Note: Using the Distillation Tower for Oil -> Fuel -> Diesel is not worth it! You will spend more energy overclocking the Multiblock than what you would get back from the products. It would be better to wait until you can make the Large Distillery (IV) for large scale Diesel production. 

However, the DT is very valuable when extracting Cracked Fuels and Pyrolyse products.

quests.title: True Distillation: Air
quests.desc:
Liquid Air is a great way to get large amounts of Nitrogen and Oxygen at the cost of using more energy than LV Centrifuges.

However, this will also give you both Helium and Argon - which are great to boost smelting in the Electric Blast Furnace.

Note: You will need at least HV Input and Output Hatches to be able to run this recipe.

quests.title: This isn't Cryotheum!
quests.subtitle: You won't find it here
quests.desc:
Some specific fluids are Cryogenic, which are too cold for ordinary pipes. 

Thus... you need to pick the right pipe for the job.

Not many pipes support cold fluids, so you'll want to give Stainless Steel or Iridium a try. Later on you can use Naquadah or most of the fusion materials when you get there.

WARNING: Cryogenics in the wrong pipes will cause voiding, pipe freezing, and high rates of death! Be careful.

Lore: This is the first time GregTech has cryogenic mechanics, a GTCEu-original feature!

quests.title: True Distillation: Fuel
quests.desc:
Here are the four most relevant Hydrocarbons you will be obtaining from distilling Steam-Cracked Fuels.

You will want to set this up before moving to IV.

Steam-Cracked Light Fuel as input is probably the most favorable, but you are free to experiment.

We know what you're thinking - something along the lines of "That Carbon output is just SO infuriating! It's ruining my automation!".

Enable Item Voiding (in the controller GUI) if you don't want to build an Output Bus.

quests.title: True Distillation: Organic
quests.desc:
This Quest requires the Pyrolyse Oven and is optional, yet convenient.

The first four fluids are the important products of Wood Tar Distillation:
- Phenol: useful for Epoxy and PBI
- Benzene: an important hydrocarbon
- Toluene: useful for TNT
- Dimethylbenzene: useful for PBI

Unlike Oil, putting Wood Tar in the Distillation Tower for the byproducts, and using Nitrobenzene for power is arguably worth it.

The bottom three are the important products from Wood Vinegar Distillation:
- Acetic Acid: useful to make more Acetone
- Methanol: a moderately important hydrocarbon
- Acetone: useful for Epoxy and Gasoline

It's also possible to use a "full combo" of 4 DTs -  input Charcoal Byproducts into a first tower, splitting into Wood Tar, Wood Gas and Wood Vinegar.

This isn't the most efficient cost-to-production ratio, but the option is there if you get bored.

quests.title: Multiblock Cracking
quests.subtitle: You Crack Me Up
quests.desc:
The Cracker is a multiblock built with the sole purpose of cracking fluids. It comes with all the recipes of Chemical Reactor Cracking, but it is lossless.

This is a great way to save a lot of Fuel in the long term!

Higher Coil tiers provide a small energy discount - up to 70% with the best Coils available.

quests.title: So Many Alloys!
quests.subtitle: Visions of GT++
quests.desc:
The Alloy Blast Smelter is the only main GCYM machine available in EV. You can use it to produce any alloy that you'd normally use the Mixer for. 

Instead of producing Hot Ingots, the ABS produces Molten Fluids if the recipe is "hot" enough. These fluids require cooling with a Vacuum Freezer. Otherwise, it produces regular liquids.

Don't let the long smelting times scare you off. This machine incorporates the Mixer step directly into the smelting recipe, and actually saves you quite a bit of time over smelting the dust in an EBF!

Nearly every GCYM material is produced in this machine, so if you have trouble finding a Mixer recipe, look for a Molten Fluid!

Lore: This multiblock was inspired by GT++. It didn't use any heating coils, had no temperature requirements, and only produced regular liquids for all materials. It was pretty OP back in the day, but it eventually got restricted to only a single Energy Hatch to balance it out. For some reason, it also allowed you to melt any dust... into liquid...?

quests.title: Getting Rid of Those Old Machines
quests.subtitle: I never liked them anyway...
quests.desc:
You probably have some old machines lying around, right?

You most likely know by now that Arc Furnaces can recycle machines, but by EV, they upgrade to 9 slots!

Note: This quest accepts either an EV or IV Arc Furnace. You don't have to make both.

quests.title: Multiple Fluid Hatches
quests.subtitle: One-To-Many, Many-To-One
quests.desc:
Need to handle multiple fluid inputs and outputs? Look no further - these Fluid Hatches can handle more than one fluid at once!

These cannot be used in a Distillation Tower, but may be particularly useful in Large Chemical Reactors.

quests.title: Platinum Group Processing
quests.subtitle: Platline in 50 hours
quests.desc:
The Platinum Group Metals are six rare metals with useful catalytic properties. In no particular order, they are Ruthenium, Rhodium, Palladium, Osmium, Iridium, and Platinum.

You will need to automate their processing lines, as every single one of these metals will be used extensively in the late game.

To begin, treat various PGM-bearing Purified Crushed Ores with Nitric Acid to produce Platinum Group Sludge.

Then, centrifuge it with Aqua Regia to produce the various PGM salts.

Chlorine can be perfectly recycled from the Raw Platinum Powder.

Nitric Acid is sort of recycled from Raw Palladium Powder through Ammonia, though we'd recommend keeping the excess Ammonia for other uses (Rocket Fuel and PBI).

The other PGM salts have their own special mini-chain associated to them - more on this in the next quests.

Note: Platinum and Palladium can also be obtained from The End or through normal Ore Processing.

Lore: Heard of the infamous "Platline" from either Gregicality or GregTech: New Horizons? The extremely daunting chain was added in GT:NH through Bartworks. The concept is amazing, but we had to tune it down significantly to make entering late game in GTCEu much smoother.

quests.title: Ruthenium and Rhodium
quests.subtitle: Ru-Rho, Rhaggy
quests.desc:
This part of the chain starts with Inert Metal Mixture.

The Sulfuric Acid will be perfectly recycled. You will also produce excess Oxygen and Hydrogen that can be stored.

Ruthenium and Rhodium will gate your progression into IV and LuV respectively. If you automate them now, you won't need to do it later!

quests.title: Iridium, Osmium, and EV
quests.subtitle: Hold your fire just for now
quests.desc:
Yo, a quick note on Iridium and Osmium. 

We know, it's technically possible to obtain these materials now, but you should wait until you reach IV. Producing these materials will suck up a lot of your precious power... and you won't be able to smelt them into ingots until IV anyway!

quests.title: Better Flight!
quests.subtitle: Quite the microcrafting project... but worth it
quests.desc:
The Advanced Nano Chestplate combines protection, a personal battery and an enhanced Jetpack, all in one.

To enable charging, sneak right-click with it in your hand, then equip it (There may be an occasional bug where it refuses to charge, but you'll forgive us, right?).

Note that this stores a lot of energy, and uses it fairly fast. You may want to invest in a HV Turbo Charger.

quests.title: Reservoir Hatch
quests.subtitle: Water, water, everywhere!
quests.desc: This hatch acts like an infinite water source for multiblocks! It can make some setups much more convenient, and also has effectively infinite throughput, so it doesn't have the limitations of the Infinite Water Source Cover not being fast enough.

quests.title: IV Superconductors
quests.subtitle: So you're telling me an Ivy conducted this Super?
quests.desc: This Superconductor will be required for progression during LuV... when you delve deeper into Research.

quests.title: Rare Earth
quests.subtitle: It's got "rare" in the name... oh no.
quests.desc:
If you already made and centrifuged Rare Earth, that's great! If you haven't, you can obtain it as a Byproduct from various ores, or from centrifuging Monazite.

The elements you can obtain are:

- Lanthanum is used in Fusion - you will eventually want a lot of it!
- Yttrium is used in Yttrium-Barium-Cuprate-Oxide (YBCO), a wire material for UV.
- Cerium is not particularly useful.
- Samarium is used for IV Superconductors and Magnetic Rods for LuV+ Motors.
- Neodymium normally makes the strongest magnets, but gameplay balance... y'know.
- Cadmium is not particularly useful.

Lore: Rare Earth Elements (REE) contain a lot more metals than these, but we're struggling as is to give them use.

quests.title: HSS-G Coils
quests.subtitle: Approaching solar temperatures
quests.desc:
HSS-G Coils bring up the Heat Capacity to 5,400K.

The ever-tempting Coil bonuses should be high incentive to make those early, as they become mandatory only during LuV.

At this point, you may find yourself using only one Electric Blast Furnace for your entire base, powered with the highest available Voltage you can supply.

Hot Metals will start taking longer to cool down, as unlike your EBFs, the Vacuum Freezer doesn't get perfect overclocks. For that, you probably want a dual (or quad) Vacuum Freezer.

quests.title: HSS-G
quests.subtitle: This time, we're going at HIGH speed!
quests.desc:
High-Speed Steels (HSS) are resistant Steel alloys typically used in rails.

If you previously made Ultimet for whatever reason, you've already acquired Molybdenum. Otherwise, it can be found in a Nether vein.

We hope you have a lot of Tungstensteel, because you're gonna need more! It's used for the creation of HSS alloys - in this case, HSS-G.

quests.title: HSS-E
quests.subtitle: Turbine Upgrade, perhaps?
quests.desc:
Technically, real-life HSS-E doesn't contain Manganese, but some other HSS alloys do have Manganese, so... close enough?

HSS-E has some niche uses in progression.

This alloy has great durability and damage property for tools. It has the best Power multiplier (until Neutronium!) as a Rotor for Large Turbines, though it's slightly less efficient than HSS-S.

quests.title: Extreme Combustion Engine
quests.subtitle: The ultimate Combustion generator!
quests.desc:
When running the Extreme Combustion Engine, you're gonna want to boost it! Why bother otherwise?

Get yourself a 4A IV Dynamo Hatch as the output, and set up a Vacuum Freezer to provide Liquid Oxygen.

You should now be wielding the equivalent of 1 LuV Amp, all whilst burning fuel at 200% efficiency!

Don't forget to keep it supplied with a small amount of Lubricant.

The ECE is also void-free, unlike Large Turbines. Any energy that cannot be output will momentarily pause the multiblock.

Lore: The ECE comes from GT:NH. However... its implementation wasn't so rosy at first. The stats on the tooltip looked similar to this one - so far, so good.

The bad part came when crunching the numbers. Players ended up realizing they needed to run around 16 whole HV Vacuum Freezers to keep up with the Liquid Oxygen demand...

The one and only fuel it would accept is High Octane Gasoline, which was stated to be burned at 300% efficiency when boosted, but the UI was lying. The efficiency remained at 100%.

The GT:NH team eventually decided to rework the ECE among other power options... phew!

quests.title: Polybenzimidazole
quests.subtitle: GTCEu's toughest polymer!
quests.desc:
You should have all the tools available to produce Polybenzimidazole on your own! Good luck - it's a 14-step process, and it's no slouch at that.

PBI only becomes mandatory to progress from LuV to ZPM, but tackling it earlier will unlock the Advanced SMDs.

quests.title: Advanced SMDs
quests.desc:
Better SMDs? SMDs that'll unlock far cheaper - and faster - Circuit recipes? We're sold!

These'll require Alloys that you haven't made yet, most of which will be commonly used in LuV and onwards. Get on it!

Whilst you don't need these right now, Advanced SMDs will become a hard requirement for the Crystal Circuit line. Note that using these in your current circuits will halve the recipe duration.

quests.title: IV Assembler
quests.desc:
Tungstensteel under your belt? Nanoprocessor Mainframes on demand? Let's make some machinery.

The IV Assembler is required to unlock IV Energy and Dynamo Hatches, as well as HSS-G Coils.

Upgrading this to its GCYM variant isn't a bad idea.

Lore: Did you know that GTCE's content stopped at EV, and all content up to UV was provided by Shadows of Greg, a third party mod?

quests.title: Worst Combustion
quests.subtitle: Good luck fueling this, lmao
quests.desc:
The Large Tungstensteel Boiler is the final Steam Boiler. It's even more efficient than the previous ones, and produces more steam.

This thing is a colossal noob trap, and you should ABSOLUTELY use something else. Don't make this, it's not good, not even as a meme! Steam doesn't scale beyond this point, and this boiler will likely be removed in the future.

Note: This quest can be skipped (for completion progress) by clicking the Checkmark. You do not have to actually craft one.

Lore: No one makes these in GT5u either... y'know, almost makes you feel sorry for the poor boiler.

quests.title: Laminated Glass
quests.subtitle: Another Polymer Rises From The Darkness
quests.desc:
Laminated Glass is the shiny new tier of Glass for IV and above. It's made in the Forming Press using a polymer you won't be familiar with: Polyvinyl Butyral.

None of the steps should be particularly difficult if you already have the Distillation Tower for Cracked Fuels, and you have also automated Epoxy.

If you don't already have both set up, you know what to do!

Note: Polyvinyl Acetate (required for the Glass) can also be used for Glue!

quests.title: HSS-S
quests.subtitle: We made this one up
quests.desc:
HSS-S is another completely fictional High Speed Steel, alloyed with Iridium and Osmium.

HSS-S will be necessary to craft LuV Motors.

This alloy has a great mining speed, which translates into extremely good efficiency when used as a Rotor in Large Turbines.

quests.title: Indium
quests.subtitle: Superconductor Supreme
quests.desc:
Getting Indium requires a lot of Sulfuric Acid, and two different ores: Galena and Sphalerite.

Sulfuric Acid can be produced in large scale in the Large Chemical Reactor, or obtained as a waste from the Platinum Group Sludge process.

In LuV, the mandatory Naquadah chain will grant Indium too, which means this setup will have a limited life.

Lore: This is another small chain inherited from GregTech 5. However, this used to yield one tiny Dust instead, which was... interesting.

quests.title: High Power Integrated Circuit
quests.desc:
The HPIC unlocks IV Energy Hatches and Dynamos... You know the drill!

Making an IV Chemical Reactor is optional. You can craft this with a Large Chemical Reactor if you wish.

If you wish to automate your LCR with patterns, circle back to the P2P Tunnel Quest for more information.

quests.title: There is no limit to "Fun"
quests.subtitle: ...and if you disagree, you'll see the light soon enough
quests.desc:
IV Components - fortunately for you - aren't a massive step up in terms of complexity.

However, the amount of Tungsten required in crafting these is huge.

emitters and Sensors are in their own league, as they require Nether Stars and Iridium, both of which have their own dedicated Quests.

quests.title: Shredding your Ores with greater speed
quests.subtitle: No more being Ore-poor!
quests.desc:
Faster Ore Processing only at the cost of this Macerator! A must have.

Also used to craft the Large Maceration Tower.

And now, it's finally time for mass ore processing automation.

The next Quest will kick things off!

quests.title: Setting up Ore Processing
quests.subtitle: I've been waiting for this!
quests.desc:
Welcome to the age of automation!

Ore Processing is now at your fingertips.

This quest unlocks a definitive tutorial on Ore Processing in the Tips and Tricks tab - check it out to learn more!

quests.title: Assembly Line
quests.subtitle: AKA the "Assline"
quests.desc:
Making LuV Components will require more work. You'll need to make an Assembly Line in order to progress.

This Multiblock has a variable size. Each ULV Input Bus along the bottom can only hold a single stack of items, so you'll need different lengths for different recipes.

You'll want to build this with a size of 10 to get started, or of a size of 14 to accept all possible recipes in base GTCEu. Don't worry if you overbuild - it can run any recipe if it's too long, but you may find the casings quite expensive right now. Choices, choices!

Recipes here will require Fluids, so it's a good idea to keep the Input Hatches permanently filled with Soldering Alloy, Lubricant and Styrene-Butadienne Rubber.

This quest unlocks two new tutorials on the Assembly Line and Research in the Tips and Tricks tab - check them out to learn more!

quests.title: Insane Batteries
quests.subtitle: It's amazing!
quests.desc:
"250,000,000 EU in a Battery? That's 25 minutes of Power when drained constantly."

The Lapotronic Energy Orb should be the beginning of the end of your energy storage struggles.

This form of Energy Storage is perfect if your Power Generation is inconsistent, or if you need to buffer large amounts due to the nature of Large Turbines.

Such a boost in power does not come cheap. Each Lapotronic Energy Orb is worth 8 Lapotron Crystals.

quests.title: First ZPM Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Zero Point Module is (almost) mine!
quests.desc: The ZPM Mainframe is used to make the LuV Circuit Assembler (of course), but also allows you to step into Fusion.

quests.title: Nether Stars
quests.subtitle: Jump Up, Superstar
quests.desc:
Nether Stars are used for high tier Sensors, emitters and Field Generators.

You can also use them for Beacons if you desire the permanent potion effects. Finally, a vanilla multiblock!

We've added a recipe for Nether Star Dust in this pack. (Yes - without any tweaks, GTCEu expects you to farm Withers ad nauseam.)

On the topic of the Nether Air which is required in the recipe: take a look at Liquid Nether Air distillation at your own leisure. It's quite rewarding.

Did you know the vanilla Nether Star texture isn't centred? :)

quests.title: Iridium and Osmium
quests.desc:
This part of the chain starts by obtaining some Rarest Metal Mixture.

You'll need at least an IV Large Chemical Reactor, but the average power draw isn't too bad.

Chlorine is fully looped, and Hydrogen is slightly negative (unless you put Acidic Osmium Solution in the Distillation Tower and electrolyze the water back... on second thoughts, don't do that).

Iridium is required to progress through IV, so get your PGS line automated ASAP!

quests.title: Rhodium-Plated Palladium
quests.subtitle: The main LuV material
quests.desc:
Congratulations on making it this far!

Progressing into LuV will require more than Rhodium Plated Palladium and LuV Circuits alone. You'll also need the Assembly Line.

The resource costs for components will also start to increase significantly as you work towards Fusion.

If you haven't got automation for nearly everything, now (in IV) is a good time to do it. Take advantage of the power granted by Gregicality Multiblocks!

If your power generation relies on Large Turbines, you can pick up the LuV Rotor Holder.

quests.title: Multiblock Miner II
quests.desc:
This Large Miner is nearly identical to the regular one, except it has a wider range and uses more drilling fluid.

It's an optional upgrade - one you might not even need!

quests.title: Power Substation
quests.subtitle: Centralised power, at last
quests.desc:
The Power Substation is a multiblock power storage system. Fill it with 2-18 layers of assorted Capacitor Blocks to increase its storage. Each layer must be full of capacitors, and you can use Empty Capacitors to fill unused spaces.

Capacitors also hold significantly more energy than the batteries used to make them. In the Power Substation, Capacitors can be mixed, so they do not all have to be the same tier.

At IV, you can make EV and/or IV Capacitors.

Energy can be inputted with regular Energy Hatches, and output with regular Dynamo Hatches.

You can also use Substation Hatches to transmit many more amps. These hatches only work in Substations, and do not work in regular multiblocks.

quests.title: Qubit CPUs
quests.subtitle: This actually has no relation to Quantum
quests.desc:
We know... this isn't actually gated behind Indium. However, you gotta trust us - this is the better recipe, so we don't want you wasting your time.

Qubit CPUs are used in the production of Quantum Processors.

quests.title: IV Circuit Assembling
quests.desc:
Woah! It's been tough to get here. You'll appreciate not having to make a new polymer for Circuits Boards.

The IV Circuit Assembler unlocks the Quantum Circuit line.

This also unlocks the best recipe for MV Circuits.

quests.title: Extreme Circuit Boards
quests.desc:
The next Board for the Quantum Circuit line.

Tips: For efficient and fast board production, you should use two Large Chemical Reactors, both supplied with HV power or higher (make them both share the same Energy Hatch):

- One LCR should be dedicated to Iron III Chloride. The Hydrogen is loopable, so reproduce Hydrochloric Acid locally - that way, you'd only need to passively input Iron Dusts and Chlorine.

- One LCR should be making both the coated Boards and the empty Circuit Boards to make future crafting requests with AE2 extremely easy. Keeping an Input Hatch full of Sulfuric Acid shouldn't be a problem, either.

quests.title: Best EV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Quantum Processing
quests.desc:
The first Circuit of the Quantum line. This is the last "easy" circuit line - things are about to get hectic.

If you wish, you could go even further and aim for Advanced SMDs to make these Circuits cheaper. 

Doing so will require PBI... and Alloys that require HSS-G Coils in your EBF.

quests.title: Rocket Fuel
quests.subtitle: Honey, where are my Microverse Projectors?
quests.desc:
Rocket Fuel mostly exists for compatibility with other mods. You'll love it if you're making your own pack.

As an actual fuel for GTCEu generators? Terrible!

In this modpack, Rocket Fuel is required to obtain Nether Stars.

Automating it may be a big hit to your resources, but you can safely batch craft using an overclocked Large Chemical Reactor.

quests.title: Fiber-Reinforced Epoxy
quests.subtitle: The biggest upgrade you've ever seen
quests.desc:
The next board material.

It's as easy as using some Epoxy in the Chemical Bath.

Anyone in favor of this getting reworked?

quests.title: More LuV Circuits
quests.subtitle: Spread the LuV
quests.desc: Good news for any mainframe haters out there - LuV Circuits that aren't mainframes!

quests.title: Even more IV Circuits
quests.subtitle: IV, but it's green now
quests.desc:
Better IV Circuits. 

Your march through IV continues. Stay strong, soldier.

quests.title: Upgrading your EBF for the millionth time
quests.subtitle: This is second nature at this point
quests.desc:
This Quest will be completed with either an IV Energy Hatch... or an IV Dynamo Hatch for your Multiblock Generators.

Don't forget that 4 Amp variants exist! However they require Niobium-Titanium Cables, which need Polyphenylene Sulfide.

Look down in the LuV Chapter for that (don't worry... it's easy to obtain).

quests.title: IV Cutting Machine
quests.desc:
The IV Cutting Machine is required to unlock IV Energy and Dynamo Hatches.

Upgrading this to its GCYM variant isn't a bad idea.

quests.title: QuarkTech
quests.subtitle: No, we didn't trademark this one
quests.desc:
This armor provides more QoL compared to the Nano suit.

Helmet: Provides Night vision (default keybind M but check for conflicts), prevents harmful potion effects, and automatically eats for you!

Chestplate: Nullifies burning, but can be further upgraded into the Advanced version at the very end of LuV. Note that this does NOT include a jetpack without the advanced upgrade.

Leggings: Provides the speed boost which the Nano leggings did not.

Boots: Provides Step Assist, nullifies fall damage, and increases jump height!

quests.title: The Mallet to End All Other Mallets
quests.subtitle: Life would be boring without silly quests...
quests.desc: Polybenzimidazole is the best material for Soft Mallets in vanilla GTCEu. Show us that you're on top of your PBI supply by spending some of it on a superfluous tool upgrade.

quests.title: Ultra High Power Integrated Circuit
quests.desc:
The UHPIC unlocks ZPM Energy Hatches and Dynamos, as well as the Active Transformer. 

You may know the drill, but do you know the screwdriver?

quests.title: Dielectric PCB Coolant
quests.subtitle: Absolutely beneficial for the environment
quests.desc:
Dielectric PCB Coolant is used to maintain some highly advanced late and end-game machinery.

You only need a little bit in LuV, but you'll want to automate it when you get to ZPM.

quests.title: Active Transformer
quests.subtitle: More than a large transformer
quests.desc:
The Active Transformer is a multiblock used to transform energy on a much larger scale. For example, you can convert HV to LV power directly by using the apropriate energy hatches.

It also has a second function, detailed in the above quest.

Lore: This is yet another feature from TecTech, which also required these cables to be painted. They do look fantastic if you paint them, but it's optional in CEu.

quests.title: Mass Data Storage
quests.subtitle: Time for a server room
quests.desc:
The Data Bank is a multiblock capable of storing huge amounts of data. You can fill it with Data Access Hatches, and fill those with Data Storage Items.

After that, you'll need power, and then a way to connect it to your Assembly Line! This is done through Optical Fiber Cables, which can be used to link Optical Data Hatches together.

An Optical Transmission Hatch is used to send data (like an output), and an Optical Reception Hatch to recieve the data (like an input). 

Optical cables have some restrictions however. They can only connect one input to one output. The Data Bank is your answer to this, since you can place many hatches on them to route your data anywhere.

You can use this behavior to chain Data Banks as well. Connecting one Data Bank to another, and then one of them to an Assembly Line will provide the data of both banks!

Note: This multiblock will be required later on in progression, but is very useful to set up early.

Lore: This multiblock also originates from TecTech, but part of its mechanics required optical cables to be painted as well. You don't have to do that here, but it looks really nice if you do!

quests.title: Laser Power Transmission
quests.subtitle: Time for some lasers!
quests.desc:
The Active Transformer has a second use - Laser Power. Lasers are like superconductors, but behave slightly differently. They share the energy input across all connected active transformers, allowing widespread power distribution. You can use Active Transformers to convert regular EU to and from Lasers.

Like optical cables, they can only be connected to one input and output. But, these ones also only can move in straight lines. To turn them, an Active Transformer with a Laser Source Hatch (output) and Target Hatch (input) is needed.

Lore: This is yet again another TecTech feature, but there's a bit more to it this time. TecTech's lasers were tiered by voltage and carried thousands of amps instead. CEu doesn't faff around with any of that tiering. You only need one type of laser here!

quests.title: Naquadah-doped Silicon Boule
quests.subtitle: Doper than ever before
quests.desc:
Doping Silicon with Naquadah will give a better Silicon Boule.

Use it for higher yield on Wafers.

This will also unlock the best recipe for HV Circuits in the LuV Circuit Assembler. You're probably going to want to mass produce these for the many Lapotronic Energy Orbs that'll be required moving forwards.

quests.title: Naquadah
quests.subtitle: Don't you mean Stargatium?
quests.desc:
Naquadah is a late-game material with a variety of uses. It is required when building a Fusion Reactor and progressing to ZPM, to name a few.

For some reason, its fluid is a brilliant green - don't ask us why.

Lore: Naquadah is a material originally from Stargate.

quests.title: Naquadah Coils
quests.subtitle: Hotter than the surface temperature of the Sun!
quests.desc:
Naquadah Coils crank up the Heat Capacity to 7,200K.

These coils are required for progressing to the second half of LuV and beyond.

Lore: Well... not some "juicy GregTech lore", but a cool space fact, owing to the fact you're beating the Sun's temperature with your EBF. The surface temperature of the Sun is 5,800K. We're taking this as an effective temperature - i.e. the radiative temperature of the Sun as if it were a perfectly "black body". Check out the Stefan-Boltzmann law if you're interested in some crude astrophysics.

Oh, and the "photosphere" (the part of the sun we're talking about when we mention its surface)? It's 400 kilometres thick. That's 250 miles. Damn!

quests.title: LuV Superconductors
quests.subtitle: Be glad these don't use pumps
quests.desc:
LuV Superconductors are necessary if you're eyeing a Fusion Reactor. 

Don't ignore our warning here - you're gonna need a lot of them. Luckily, any Indium shortages will be solved by your Naquadah line.

quests.title: Fluid of the Stars
quests.subtitle: The stars are calling...
quests.desc:
Warning: Plasmas are extremely hot, with temperatures exceeding 30k Kelvin! 

However, this doesn't mean that a special type of pipe is required to transport plasma. Plasmas are treated slightly differently, and bend the rules a bit.

Fluid pipes that can handle plasmas, such as Tungsten, Naquadah, or Duranium can hold any plasma regardless of temperature. This means they won't melt under the heat of the plasma!

WARNING: Plasmas in incompatible pipes will cause voiding, pipe burning, and disproportionately high rates of death. Be careful!

Lore: This mechanic originates from GT6, just like the acids!

quests.title: Large Plasma Turbine
quests.subtitle: Plasma? Turbine?! How?
quests.desc:
Use this turbine to extract very large amounts of power from plasma!

Helium Plasma is the best fuel for its cost.

If you're the type to question how realistic Plasma Turbines are... look up Magnetohydrodynamic Generators if you want something that bends fact into fiction.

quests.title: Naquadah Alloy
quests.subtitle: Stargate's Strongest
quests.desc:
This is the main material for ZPM. Having this in stock will be a test of your Platinum processing, so this is a pre-emptive warning to stay on top of mining PGS-bearing ore.

Prepare to make lots... after you make Fusion and Crystal Circuits. Tackle those before you try making this.

quests.title: Naquadah Chain
quests.subtitle: Do not touch the acid
quests.desc:
Refining Naquadah into further products will be your next automation challenge. The primary outputs of this line are Enriched Naquadah, Naquadria, and Trinium. 

This processing line - affectionately known as the "Naqline" - also produces many useful byproducts, such as Titanium and Indium.

Enriched Naquadah currently isn't used for much, but will be integral to power generation in the future.

Naquadria is a lategame material that you'll want to stockpile for your UV components.

Trinium is the material you'll be wanting most right now. You'll use a lot of it to build your first Fusion Reactor, is used for the next set of Coils, and sees heavy usage in most ZPM recipes.

Note: This processing chain presents more of a challenge than any chemistry you've dealt with so far.

As usual, outputs in later steps can be sent back into previous processes... with the caveat that they lose a small amount of fluid with each loop.

This can be challenging to automate. Good luck!

Lore: Many GregTech iterations have Naquadah processing. 

The first was likely Impact, with its own original Naquadah fuel process. 

Gregicality Legacy was hot on its heels with processes for making the Naquadah material (and variants) as well as Fuel. 

After that, GTNH added their own Naquadah processes for the material and fuel. 

Lastly, GTCEu added the process you're about to set up!

quests.title: Trinium
quests.subtitle: Another Stargate material!
quests.desc:
Trinium is a very important material for progression. You'll need it to make a Fusion Reactor, but it stays relevant all throughout ZPM. We (well, this particular questbook writer!) can never have enough of this stuff.

It also requires an LuV EBF to smelt! Perhaps dedicate a setup solely for cooking this, unless you're confident that your infrastructure can deal with it.

Lore: In Stargate, Trinium was strong enough to block a point-blank C4 explosion. It's considered to be a hundred times stronger than steel, at only one-hundreth the weight.

quests.title: Plutonium-241
quests.subtitle: Spicy Plutonium Isotope
quests.desc:
Plutonium-241 is yet another requirement for the Fusion Reactor. It's very easy to make: just centrifuge some Pu-239 to slowly separate some out.

In the future, this material will require a Nuclear Reactor to produce, but that's not in GTCEu yet.

Lore: In GT Impact, making Pu-241 was a challenge. Your first Pu-241 had to be produced as a byproduct of its very expensive Naquadah fuel process, and it could only be made independently with something post-fusion!

quests.title: Fusion Reactor I
quests.subtitle: Stellar power
quests.desc:
First things first - build the version of the structure that uses Fusion Glass in emi. It's far cheaper right now.

To save on Fusion Casings, fit Input/Output Hatches wherever you can (as displayed in the structure).

The Fusion Reactor will pick a free Output Hatch from the many that are available. In order to "force" the Reactor to use a particular Output Hatch, you can always lock all of the hatches to an irrelevant fluid (we usually use Water).

This time, we'll try something new: we're gonna explain the Lore at the same time as the mechanics.

Enjoy the ride!

Lore: GregTech 5's Fusion Reactor stores EU you feed it in its internal buffer. The maximum capacity is determined by the amount of Energy Hatches in the structure.

Recipes can only start if the "EU to start" is stored internally, which will be consumed. With this stored energy, the reactor can keep running recipes until it goes idle. At this point it will need to be topped up on EU to start again.

Shadow of Greg's Fusion Reactor (which is now GTCEu's Fusion Reactor) comes with an added mechanic called Heat.

When the reactor is running, the EU to start is converted to Heat. That Heat is preserved until the Reactor goes idle, at which point it will slowly go down.

Next time a recipe is found, it will use all possible Heat and deduct it from the EU to start, allowing you to save Energy for short pauses.

The Fusion Reactor I can only run recipes up to a required EU of 160 MEU to start. It will also overclock up to LuV at best, even if you upgrade to ZPM Hatches, so don't stress over attempting this.

quests.title: Europium
quests.subtitle: Fusing Europium... whaaat?!
quests.desc:
Europium is the first major Fusion Material. You'll be using this quite a lot going forwards.

Note: The temperature of liquid Europium is very hot, but the fluid isn't a plasma. Plan your piping accordingly!

Lore: In Gregicality Legacy, Europium was replaced with Rutherfordium as the first fusion material, because Europium is actually found in Rare Earth and isn't fused in real life.

quests.title: Crystal CPU
quests.subtitle: These don't even make any sense!
quests.desc:
Crystal CPUs are used to produce Crystal Circuits - who would have thought?

Producing these is quite a bit different from the previous circuit components. Instead of standard silicon wafers, these rely on Emerald or Olivine.

Getting your first Crystal Chips will be difficult, so you may wish to invest in a high-tier Autoclave. If you use a HV Autoclave... well, we didn't take you for the gambling sort.

After you get your first Chip, you can duplicate them with Europium. Don't worry about those other recipes yet, they're for later on in ZPM.

Note: An Advanced Item Detector cover will be useful for automating these. They work just like the Advanced Energy Detector.

Lore: In GT5u modpacks, making your first Crystal Chips required the autoclave to be in Low Gravity, which meant doing this process on a Galacticraft Space Station, or on the moon!

quests.title: Upgrading your Assembly Line
quests.desc:
This Quest will be completed with either an LuV Energy Hatch... or an LuV Dynamo Hatch for your Multiblock Generators.

Starting at LuV, you'll start upgrading more multiblocks, which means more energy hatches! Both the EBF and the Assembly Line will need power upgrades.

Don't forget that 4 Amp variants exist!

quests.title: Ludicrous Fluid Drilling
quests.subtitle: This thing is completely ludicrous!
quests.desc:
At the cost of 16x the drilling Power, the Advanced Fluid Drilling Rig II will be gaining 64x the output in Fluid.
The depletion of veins is also slashed to an eighth of what it was before. This means you will get a total of 128x as much Oil (or whatever fluid) as the regular rig from a bedrock fluid vein until depletion!

Even when depleted, you can safely use this and be very much power positive.

One single Advanced Fluid Drilling Rig II should be enough to last you until the end of the game, for whatever fluid you drill.

quests.title: Ludicrous Ore Drilling
quests.desc:
This Large Miner is nearly identical to the regular one, except it has a wider range and uses more drilling fluid.

It's an optional sidegrade - one you might not even want.

Lore: There may be a rebalance for these in the future. See the Multiblock Miner quest for details.

quests.title: Ludicrous Batteries
quests.subtitle: Even more power!
quests.desc:
The LuV Lapotronic Orb is even better than the IV one! Of course, such power comes at a steep cost...

Lore: In older versions of GT, such as GTNH in the past or in GCYL, these orbs were ludicrously expensive! They required 8 orbs of the previous tier! Thankfully, those days are long past.

quests.title: Even more LuV Circuits
quests.subtitle: Yup... another circuit upgrade
quests.desc: Cheaper LuV circuits! These'll take some of the edge out of those brutal Robot Arm recipes.

quests.title: More ZPM Circuits
quests.subtitle: ...wait, why'd things end here?
quests.desc:
ZPM Circuits that aren't mainframes!

Don't lie - Crystal Supercomputers sound really cool, right?

quests.title: LuV Prospector
quests.subtitle: If you're yet to strike gold, you will now
quests.desc: The LuV Electric Prospector is a major upgrade from the HV one. With its huge range, it'll make finding Fluid and Ore veins very easy.

quests.title: Best IV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: I know where this is going!
quests.desc:
The first circuit of the Crystal line.

Lore: The concept of a Crystal Circuit has existed since the very early versions of GregTech. We're still not entirely sure if they exist in real life.

quests.title: Multi-Layer Fiber-Reinforced Printed Circuit Boards
quests.subtitle: M-L F-R PCB
quests.desc:
If you say the name 3 times in front of a mirror, you summon a CEu dev.

Multi-Layer Fiber-Reinforced Printed Circuit Boards are the next step in your circuit journey. They're not particularly interesting compared to the previous boards: just two of the previous stuck together.

Lore: In Gregicality Legacy, these circuit boards were much more complicated. They required Kapton, a dual polymer material. It was a challenge to say the least. In the future, the GTCEu team might make these boards somewhat more involved to produce.

quests.title: Pure Joy
quests.subtitle: Fun levels - critical
quests.desc:
LuV Components are significantly more difficult to produce than their predecessors. They require an Assembly Line, many different forms of materials, and lots of fluids too!

You will notice that Samarium is required for LuV+ Motors. Samarium by itself isn't very difficult to obtain, but smelting it is slightly more involved. It requires Liquid Helium in the Freezer. If you made IV Superconductors, you've likely seen this already.

Lore: In the original GregTech 5, these components were unobtainable! You'd be free from GT if you were at this point! How times change.

quests.title: Polyphenylene Sulfide
quests.subtitle: Cables need better coating now
quests.desc: A requirement for LuV+ cables.

quests.title: Combatting Lag
quests.subtitle: They call me 007 - 0 Naquadah, 0 EU/t, 7 TPS
quests.desc:
The chances you are having TPS issues playing this modpack are slim - this Quest is more suited for late game bases with GregTech and AE2.

If you run the command /forge tps, you will be given the current TPS and the tick time in milliseconds.

If the tick time exceeds 50ms, the server is under too much load, and won't be able to keep up with a steady 20 TPS.

Any machine will add up to the tick time, but don't be too scared of adding machines. Most of the lag usually comes from things that are unoptimized.

Things to avoid in general:

- Entities are pretty bad. Ground items or mob entities should be kept as low as possible at any given time. Thankfully, this shouldn't be an issue in this modpack.

- Building everything packed together in a small space. Note that this does not affect TPS, but you will suffer FPS drops instead.

- Huge walls of drawers, as iterating through every single one of them for insertion and extraction can be pretty taxing.

Things to avoid with AE2:

- ME Import and Export Buses on large networks. Use ME Interfaces instead.

- Crafting Cards to passively do large requests.

- Recursive insertion and/or extraction through sub-networks. If you use sub-networks, make them do very specific things rather than interacting with another AE2 network.

Things to avoid with GregTech (GTCEu has received a lot of improvements over GTCE, but it is possible to destroy your TPS, as with any mod):

- Many EU Producers connected to many EU Consumers in the same line. Instead, use a Diode or Battery Buffer to separate your line of generators to your line of machines.

- Large amounts of Fluid Pipes. They are not laggy individually, but they can add up if you have thousands of them.

- Big Item Pipe networks. Small networks are better, to reduce the number of iterations over inventories.

quests.title: IV Magnetism
quests.subtitle: Feeling attracted yet?
quests.desc:
Magnetic Samarium (which you'll need for your LuV components) requires an IV Polarizer.

Even so, we hope you weren't running all the polarization off a single HV Polarizer. Were you?

quests.title: Best Flight!
quests.subtitle: I'll be seeing you in the skies!
quests.desc:
The Advanced Quantum Chestplate combines protection, a personal battery and an ultimate Jetpack into a single powerful piece of gear.

Note that this stores a lot of energy. You may want to invest in an LuV Turbo Charger.

Lore: This armor piece is inspired by the IndustrialCraft  Addon Gravisuite, which added the Gravitation Suit, analogous to this chestplate.

quests.title: Two Hatches for the Price of One
quests.subtitle: Compact and Useful
quests.desc:
New in the 1.4 update, Dual Hatches will greatly help with automation with Multiblocks.

They can contain both Fluids and Items simultaneously, meaning you can simply connect a Pattern Provider and be off to the races. No more faffing around with P2P or Buffers!

quests.title: Zero Point Module
quests.subtitle: Unlimited powah!
quests.desc:
The Zero Point Module (item) is a non-rechargable battery with TONS of energy stored. You may have found one of these before now, but you haven't been able to discharge it until reaching ZPM.

These can only be found as loot from Jungle Temples. They're exceptionally rare - don't worry about finding one. 

This quest is optional and is just for fun. Feel free to hit the checkbox if you just want the completion points!

Lore: Zero Point Module, the full name of the ZPM tier, is also from Stargate. Its namesake is a device which generated crazy amounts of energy.

quests.title: ZPM Superconductors
quests.subtitle: Getting tired of all these pitch-black ingots yet?
quests.desc:
ZPM Superconductors are a major gate between you and a Fusion Reactor Mk2, which uses copious amounts for its structure blocks.

You can also use them to make Superconducting Coils for cheaper.

quests.title: Absolute Elation
quests.subtitle: Prepare your platline!
quests.desc:
ZPM Components thankfully aren't too much harder than LuV. 

They use lots of Osmiridium, so make sure your platinum refining process is up to the task.

quests.title: Enriched Naquadah
quests.subtitle: Naquadah, but radioactive!
quests.desc:
Enriched Naquadah is an isotope of Naquadah, Nq-528. It is radioactive and will be used in (as of yet) unimplemented nuclear reactors.

Currently, you just need it to make Trinium Coils.

quests.title: Trinium Coils
quests.subtitle: IT'S OVER 9000!!!
quests.desc:
Trinium Coils bring up the Heat Capacity to 9,001K.

These coils are required for progressing to the second half of ZPM, and beyond.

quests.title: Research Station
quests.subtitle: Your research adventures continue!
quests.desc:
The Research Station is the upgrade to the Scanner. It's used for more advanced forms of research, and will finally allow you to perform research which requires Data Orbs and Modules.

When running the Research Station, energy and Computation is needed. The latter is provided through an HPCA, which is explained in the next quest. It is recieved with a Computation Data Reception Hatch. Note that only one Reception Hatch is allowed in a Research Station.

This multiblock needs computation sporadically, which means the recipe will not reverse progress if you don't supply enough - it will simply halt in-place. However, the machine will still draw energy constantly, even when recieving no computation.

To scan items, the process is relatively similar. In the Object Holder, place the target item in the center slot, and the data storage item in the other slot. Then provide computation, and the data storage item will be updated upon completion. When running, the target item will be Locked and cannot be removed.

Note: To form this multiblock, the Object Holder must face the controller.

quests.title: First UV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: ...ah, here we go!
quests.desc:
The UV Mainframe is required to move into Fusion Mk2.

It's also made in the Assembly Line.

Passing over the ingredients we've already seen before, watch out for the HSS-E Frames.

quests.title: Crystal SoC
quests.subtitle: When engraving it once isn't enough
quests.desc:
The Crystal SoC is used to make cheaper Crystal Circuits. Enjoy the discount!

Crystal SoCs require ZPM emitters, which means you need a Data Orb for their research.

In the future, these may become more difficult to make.

Lore: In GT5u, the texture for this item was red, and did not have the special design on it. There was a "raw" version with the design, but it was used for other unrelated things. Who knows why?

quests.title: Advanced Data Access Hatch
quests.subtitle: Increased storage density!
quests.desc:
This hatch allows you to store more Data Storage Items than the basic one.

Don't forget that this hatch requires a Data Orb for research.

quests.title: Naquadria
quests.subtitle: Naquadah, but radioactive-er!
quests.desc: Naquadria is an isotope of Naquadah, Nq-522. It's highly unstable, but it'll still be used for UV components, Fusion Mk2, and when fusing Neutronium.

quests.title: Sterilized Growth Medium
quests.subtitle: The final processing line, at last!
quests.desc:
Production of Sterilized Growth Medium is the final processing line in GTCEu, for now.

You will need this for your first Wetware Circuits.

Producing it won't be as much of a hassle as PBI, but nearly every step requires a Sterilized Cleanroom. This means you'll have to get creative with how you use your space.

Make sure to scrutinise every step of the chain. Some recipes are much more efficient than others!

Pro Tip: You can wall-share cleanrooms with doors in-between too if you want more space.

Lore: GTCEu's Sterilized Growth Medium chain is loosely based off of Bartworks' from GTNH. The main difference is that GTNH required a Bacterial Vat multiblock, which was more complex than the ordered Assembly Line. GTCEu is considering adding a multiblock like it but with their own spin in the future.

More Lore: In Gregicality Legacy, the Sterilized Growth Medium production chain was very different. It was one of the most difficult processes in the mod, with over 30 complicated steps! It was based off of producing different real-life vitamins and nutrients.

Even More Lore: In Shadows of Greg, this was made with just Distilled Water and Mince Meat. How far we've come...

quests.title: Mega Batteries
quests.subtitle: A power singularity!
quests.desc:
The ZPM Lapotronic Orb is even better than the LuV one! It of course comes at a price...

Lore: Imagine making 64 IV or 8 LuV Orbs for just one of these! See the Lore for the LuV orb for details.

quests.title: Upgrading your Assembly Line II
quests.desc:
This Quest will be completed with either a ZPM Energy Hatch... or a ZPM Dynamo Hatch for your Multiblock Generators.

Don't forget that 16 Amp variants exist!

Don't forget that these hatches require a Data Orb for research.

quests.title: Sterilized Cleanroom
quests.subtitle: Comes with disinfectant!
quests.desc:
The Sterilized Cleanroom is required to produce the next tier of circuits. Nearly every step must be performed inside of one. Good luck fitting everything into such a tight space!

If you only have one Cleanroom, you'll need another for Sterilized recipes.

Note: Normal Cleanroom recipes cannot run in the Sterilized Cleanroom, so you can't just "upgrade" your old Cleanrooms.

quests.title: HPCA
quests.subtitle: That's no Quantum Computer...
quests.desc:
The High Performance Computation Array, also known as the HPCA, is the multiblock used to create Computation, measured in CWU/t (Compute-Work Units per tick).

The HPCA is a modular multiblock with many unique components you've likely not seen before. There are 9 slots available for different components, and there are some example layouts in the TMI previews, so reviewing these may help if you feel confused.

There are two main types of components:

- Computation Components: These generate CWU/t and require EU/t and cooling.
- Cooling Components: These provide a certain amount of cooling, and require EU/t.

Currently Available Components:

- Empty: Fills a slot but otherwise does nothing.
- Heat Sink: Requires no power but provides 1 cooling.
- Active Cooler: Requires power, PCB Coolant, but provides 2 cooling.
- Computation: Creates 4 CWU/t, but requires a lot of power and cooling.

The HPCA draws some power to idle. Its power and cooling requirements will sit between the idle upkeep power and a maximum value depending on how much computation is being used.

If the HPCA is not given enough cooling, Computation components can be damaged if it is allowed to run above 100C (the idle temperature is 20C). Only computation components can be damaged - all other blocks will be unharmed!

The HPCA can only have 1 Computation Data Transmission Hatch. You can connect it to the Reception Hatch of the Research Station with Optical Fiber Cable to transmit the computation.

Basic CWU/t Requirements for ZPM Tier:

- 4 CWU/t for ZPM Sensors, emitters, and Field Generators
- 8 CWU/t for ZPM Enegy Hatches
- 16 CWU/t for UV Circuits and beyond.

Don't feel forced to go for 16 CWU/t immediately. There's lots to do in between these milestones.

Lore: This mechanic is loosely inspired by TecTech's Quantum Computer, which is similar but has some much more annoying mechanics like the Uncertainty Resolver.

quests.title: Stem Cells
quests.subtitle: This texture used to be really ugly...
quests.desc: Stem Cells are a major component of Wetware Circuits. Just don't question why these use Osmiridium.

quests.title: Wetware Printed Circuit Board
quests.subtitle: The board of life itself
quests.desc:
The final circuit board - it's been a long time coming! As you can see, this board is much more expensive than the other ones, but you should be alright. You're a pro now!

Lore: In GTNH, these circuit boards require LuV Sensors, instead of IV. This means that Wetware LuV circuits, needed other (crystal) LuV circuits!

Additionally, because Wetware was so expensive, using Crystal was preferred unless UHV wetware circuits were needed. Thankfully, CEu does not have this problem, and LuV Wetware Circuits are worth making.

quests.title: It's Alive!
quests.subtitle: Neurons for computation
quests.desc: The Neuro Processing Unit serves as the CPU for Wetware. It also takes a circuit board in its recipe, but don't let that scare you.

quests.title: Best ZPM Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Did that circuit just BLINK at me?
quests.desc:
The second circuit of the wetware line.

This is also the best ZPM circuit!

quests.title: Best LuV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Eek, it's alive!
quests.desc:
The first Circuit of the Wetware line.

Congrats!

quests.title: Darmstadtium
quests.subtitle: I want my particle accelerator back...
quests.desc:
Remember Darmstadtium tools from GTCE? Well then... guess who's back?

Darmstadtium is the main material for UV hulls. It is yet again made with Fusion.

Fun Fact: Arsenic does not exist as a liquid at standard conditions. It sublimates directly into a gas! "Realism", my ass.

quests.title: Duranium
quests.subtitle: Who needs Stargate, anyway?
quests.desc:
Duranium is a material from Star Trek, and is used for a few things in GT. It makes some pretty awesome pipes, but otherwise you won't need much of this quite yet.

Lore: In some versions of GT5u, this material was entirely white. In fact, most fusion materials and later were like this. Boring!

quests.title: Americium
quests.subtitle: It'd be nice if we could make this in a nuclear reactor...
quests.desc:
Americium is a Fusion Mk2 material, used in large quantities in CEu progression!

It requires 2 Fusion steps to obtain - Lanthanum and Silicon will make Lutetium, and combining this Lutetium with Chrome will give you Americium.

Lore: In Gregicality Legacy, Americium was replaced with Dubnium. Americium is a material only obtained from nuclear reactors, so GCYL changed it to the synthetic element Dubnium instead.

quests.title: Best UV Circuits!
quests.subtitle: Sentience in circuit form
quests.desc:
The third circuit of the wetware line. Get some Europium to make these.

This is also the best UV circuit!

quests.title: Advanced HPCA Components
quests.subtitle: Time for some computational advancement!
quests.desc:
Advanced HPCA Components are improved versions of their regular counterparts, and allow for increasing the computation you can produce from an HPCA.

The HPCA Advanced Computation Component produces 4x the computation for only 2x the Cooling!

quests.title: Fusion Reactor II
quests.subtitle: Fusion just got more expensive...
quests.desc:
Build the structure displayed with Fusion Glass in emi.

To save on Fusion Casings, fit Input + Output Hatches wherever you can (as displayed in the structure).

The Fusion Reactor Mk2 is even more expensive than the Mk1! The key difference is that it has a higher Energy To Start than the Mk1, allowing you to run new recipes.

It can also overclock lower voltage recipes, such as those in the Mk1 reactor. See the Mk1's quest for how these mechanics work.

quests.title: Tritanium
quests.subtitle: Star Trek strikes again!
quests.desc:
Tritanium is a material from Star Trek, and is used for a few other things in GT. It, like Americium, requires two fusion steps!

You'll need this to get the next tier of circuits, and for UV components.

Lore: In some versions of GT5u, only Tritanium fluid was red. The material was otherwise white. In GTCEu, we decided to make the whole thing red instead. Much better looking!

quests.title: The Finale
quests.subtitle: Is this... the end?
quests.desc:
Crafting the Ultimate Battery marks the end of GTCEu and the GregTech Community Modpack. Congratulations!

Now that you've finished the mod, there's a few things you should do:

- Join the GTCEu Discord and tell us about how your playthrough went! Feel free to show off your battery :) 

- Go outside (and start playing GregTech in real life, right?). Integrate back into regular society.

- Blow up your world with TNT and GT Dynamite. Please, please, PLEASE make a backup first!!!

Thank you for playing the pack! :D
- The GregTech Community Pack Dev Team

quests.title: Cleaning Maintenance Hatch
quests.desc:
This Maintenance Hatch is an upgrade to the Automatic Maintenance Hatch.

It allows your Multiblock to run regular Cleanroom recipes when not contained inside one - but not Sterilized recipes.

quests.title: Ultimate Battery?
quests.subtitle: It holds way too much power!
quests.desc:
The UV Lapotronic Orb is even better than the ZPM one! It of course comes at a price...

Lore: Imagine making 512 IV Orbs, or 64 LuV Orbs... or 8 ZPM Orbs.... for just one of these! See the Lore for the LuV orb for details.

quests.title: Ultimate Battery!
quests.subtitle: Consider touching some grass
quests.desc:
The amount of EU this battery can store is the max value of a Long Integer in Java.

Don't try to win Minecraft - you can't.

Lore: You're probably tired of this by now, but things are different with the battery lore this time. We promise.

GTCEu's ultimate battery uses 16 UV orbs. GTNH's only uses 8. However, GTNH also has a Really Ultimate Battery made of 8 Ultimate Batteries! It's not useful at all, but still exists...

They also added an Extremely Ultimate Battery made of 8 Really Ultimate Batteries, which is ludicrously expensive.

quests.title: Network Switch
quests.subtitle: With our powers combined...
quests.desc:
The Network Switch allows multiple HPCAs to be used in conjunction with each other! This allows you to provide far more computation to your Research Station.

HPCAs can be connected to Network Switches with transmission and reception hatches, and then the Network Switch can output to a Research Station with another transmission hatch.

HPCAs cannot connect to Network Switches by default, and each connected HPCA requires an HPCA Bridge Component to enable this functionality.

quests.title: Wetware Mainframe - The UHV Circuit
quests.subtitle: The final circuit
quests.desc:
This is a huge accomplishment, nice job!

The only ingredient that should be new to you are the Tritanium Frames.

quests.title: Data Module
quests.subtitle: Data storage sure is expensive...
quests.desc: The Data Module is required for Endgame Research. Assembly Lines cannot read from it using Data Access Hatches directly, and instead require the Data Bank.

quests.title: Neutronium
quests.subtitle: Pure Neutrons
quests.desc:
Neutronium is a hyper-dense material normally found in the hearts of neutron stars: the collapsed cores of stars between ten and thirty times the size of the sun. It is the final primary material in GTCEu.

Lore: In GCYL, Neutronium is unlocked at UMV-tier (called UXV in CEu). There is also Cosmic Neutronium, which is equivalent to Avaritia's Neutronium in GT5u, which was unlocked even later in GCYL!

More Lore: Fun Fact: a single Neutronium block is approximately 2884 million metric tons and somewhere around 600000K, yet you're somehow capable of holding one with your bare hands. Don't think about it too much.

quests.title: Tritanium Coils
quests.subtitle: Why would you even need something this hot?!
quests.desc:
Tritanium Coils bring the Heat Capacity to 10,800K.

These coils aren't needed for much, but they'll provide another perfect overclock for most recipes!

quests.title: Certificate of not being a Noob anymore
quests.subtitle: Obtain for G.O.A.T Status
quests.desc:
A goal if you really want to make a point.

If you make this in a UV machine, it'll take you 10 days.

If you overclock a Gregicality Multiblock to UEV (by using a 4A UHV hatch), you'll still be waiting for 2.5 days!

Lore: The NAN Certificate comes from Crops++, a GT5u addon that adds a wide variety of IC2 crops.

quests.title: Total Euphoria
quests.subtitle: Prepare your Fusion Reactors!
quests.desc:
UV Components are a tad harder than ZPM. 

They use lots of Tritanium and Naquadria, so make sure your have enough Fusion Reactors, and a fast enough Naquadah Plant.

They also need a lot more Computation, which means Advanced HPCA Components are now required!

Computation Needs for early UV:

- 32 CWU/t: Basic UV Components and the Network Switch
- 48 CWU/t: UV Sensor, emitter, and Field Generator

quests.title: Upgrading your Assembly Line III
quests.desc:
This Quest will be completed with either a UV Energy Hatch... or a UV Dynamo Hatch for your Multiblock Generators.

Don't forget that 16 Amp variants exist!

Additionally, remember that these hatches require a Data Module for research.

quests.title: UV Superconductors
quests.desc:
UV Superconductors show up a lot in recipes when making a Fusion Reactor Mk3. Better get ready to make a boatload...

You can also use them to cheapen Superconducting Coils even further.

quests.title: Upgrading your Assembly Line for the Last Time
quests.desc:
This Quest will be completed with either a UHV Energy Hatch... or a UHV Dynamo Hatch for your Multiblock Generators.

These are the final energy hatches in GTCEu. It's been a pleasure upgrading our multiblocks together o7

Don't forget about the 16 amp versions!

quests.title: Neutronium-doped Silicon Boule
quests.subtitle: Neutron -> Electron Holes
quests.desc:
This is the final silicon boule, which will allow you to produce a ton of wafers.

It also unlocks the recipe for the Highly Advanced SoC, which is used to make cheaper wetware circuits.

quests.title: Creative ME Energy
quests.subtitle: Ok, maybe there are Creative items.
quests.desc: The Creative Energy Cell is one of the few craftable creative items. It provides infinite energy to your AE system, so you don't have to worry about blackouts or power anymore.

quests.title: Gravi Star
quests.subtitle: Unfortunately, doesn't make you float
quests.desc: The Gravi Star is the final upgraded Nether Star in GTCEu. It's needed for UV Sensors, emitters, and Field Generators.

quests.title: Fusion Reactor III
quests.subtitle: The most expensive form of Fusion
quests.desc:
Build the structure displayed with Fusion Glass in emi.

To save on Fusion Casings, fit Input + Output Hatches wherever you can (as displayed in the structure).

The Fusion Reactor Mk3 is even more expensive than the Mk2! The key difference is that it has a higher Energy To Start than the Mk2, allowing you to run new recipes.
